# AetherLink Progress And Forward Plan

Last updated: 2026-07-21 KST.

This document records what has been implemented so far and what should happen next. It is intentionally broader than the original v0.1 MVP because recent work has moved the prototype toward a more complete product shape.

## 2026-07-21 V1 G0 Publication Follow-Up And Authority Baseline

- Goal and result: execution of the canonical V1 roadmap has started with G0.
  `docs/v1/g0/decision-v1.{md,json}` now records one versioned product, support,
  distribution, security, privacy, quality, evidence, and authority baseline
  against published implementation revision `d32c1846`.
  `docs/v1/g0/assurance-v1.{md,json}` adds the hash-pinned protocol/data-flow
  inventory, threat/risk refresh, closed observability schema, release
  checklist, and incident/rollback runbooks. This is static G0 assurance work,
  not transport implementation.
- Product decision: V1 is Android API 26 through target/compile 36 on arm64
  phones plus macOS 14 or newer on Apple Silicon, with the existing English,
  Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French locales and runtime-mediated
  Ollama/LM Studio. P2P remains a GA gate; Intel macOS and the previously named
  platform/backend/tool expansions remain Post-V1.
- Release decision: Android uses Google Play closed testing followed by staged
  production and Play App Signing. macOS uses Developer ID direct distribution,
  hardened runtime, notarization, and a stapled signed DMG, with manual signed V1
  updates. Android debug `0.1.0` is non-migratable and requires clean install plus
  fresh pairing. Final production application IDs remain unresolved rather than
  being invented from current development identifiers.
- Security decision: the existing TURN plus sealed-emergency-relay profile is
  retained without supersession. G0 selects TLS 1.3 plus signed lease
  capabilities and monotonic pair-epoch recovery with one-sided deny-only
  revocation, fresh-QR replacement, and signed status reconciliation. The
  existing platform-native P-256/HKDF-SHA-256/AES-256-GCM result remains only a
  G1 ratification input. Service-mediated P2P candidate publication and fetch
  require exact signed capabilities; revoked retained state has an absolute
  30-second closure maximum; rollback must succeed 100%; and thirteen
  security-hard-stop classes each allow zero failures.
- Measurement decision: the record fixes per-cell sample/success/confidence,
  traversal/cold-setup/reconnect/handoff/revoke latency, memory, battery,
  crash/ANR, soak, abuse, security-zero-tolerance, and capacity formulas. They
  are release targets and not current product claims. Twelve non-omittable
  network cells now name local, IPv6, home-NAT, CGNAT, NAT64, UDP-blocked,
  forced-TURN, forced-sealed-relay, handoff, VPN, and suspend/resume coverage.
  Six required variants add symmetric NAT, consent loss, deliberate P2P
  failure, TURN/sealed-relay outage, and regional outage. Native-IPv6 and home-
  NAT cells have a separate release-blocking direct-P2P threshold. Four
  measurement contracts bind
  every target to an owner role, source, sample/window rule, and failure action;
  actual named owners remain blockers.
- Assurance result: the versioned machine record pins 29 source files and
  closes nine protocol units, sixteen data flows, 35 guarded protocol
  namespaces, inherited T001-T016 plus T017-T026, ten release-blocking risks,
  nine event classes, five decision-bound release-record classes, seven incident
  classes, rollback
  invariants, and fourteen explicit unassigned approval roles.
- G0 closure derivation: a closed machine contract maps all ten blockers to all
  nine G0 checks, all fourteen accountable roles, and exact evidence kinds.
  Risk evidence is tagged by its required gate so later G2/G4/G5/G6 obligations
  cannot be confused with G0 prerequisites. Acceptance must bind the published
  checkpoint digest, containing commit, scoped blocker IDs, and catalog evidence;
  accepted receipts require non-empty verified references. The current checker
  accepts no receipt. Its activation policy requires a successor to build an
  opaque, independently anchored validation context covering actual commit-tree,
  remote, evidence-artifact and sanitized-log bytes, authenticated owner and
  runner attestations, authority/command bindings, and complete non-ambiguous
  closure coverage before any receipt can participate. A non-authorizing
  candidate context can compare supplied commit and remote bytes. A separate
  factory-only seven-input candidate-context core now also cross-binds supplied
  owner, authority, runner, artifact/log/manifest, and trusted-time
  observations, but fabricates no adapter result and always remains dormant. The local
  sidecar encodes repository/commit/checkpoint/hash/time values drawn from the
  actual session observation but does not persist its acquisition provenance or
  independently reproduce it. The canonical checker still accepts no receipt,
  and no independently anchored activation-capable context exists.
- V2 closure amendment: the committed V1 assurance and checkpoint bytes remain
  unchanged. `assurance-closure-amendment-v2.json` binds their raw/canonical
  hashes, defines eleven exact ordered JSON Pointer operations, advances the
  effective and nested closure schema identities, and records the independently
  reconstructed effective V2 assurance digest. Its separate V2 checkpoint and
  composite publication receipt bind parent, amendment, effective, commit, and
  remote-readback identities. Unknown/reordered paths, array operations, missing
  replace targets, occupied add targets, hash drift, symlinks, validation-time
  replacement, authority promotion, and parent mutation fail closed.
- Executable gate contract candidate: only `full_no_device_aggregate` and
  `android_and_macos_release_compilation` are command gates. Their two
  canonical profiles pin ordered argv, repository-root execution, clean
  environment and full environment digest requirements, toolchain attestation,
  exact allowed/forbidden side effects, offline Gradle plus OS-enforced egress
  denial, sanitized logs, and required evidence kinds. The remaining seven G0
  checks close from publication/catalog/owner evidence and cannot be forced
  through a command receipt. Both profiles remain `not_authorized`; catalog
  presence grants no build, IPC-socket, loopback-socket, or execution authority.
- Dormant publication-receipt boundary: a factory-only immutable candidate
  context snapshots the four exact commit blobs, separately reviewed target,
  and distinct-provenance remote checkpoint bytes. Strict receipt validation
  performs no receipt-directed I/O and returns failures only. Eight mutation
  tests cover bundle reconstruction, mutable-input isolation, provenance,
  fields, formats, targets, and malformed/oversized inputs. The canonical G0
  checker rejects any explicitly supplied bundle, including a well-shaped one;
  the private exact matcher also returns `dormant_non_authorizing`, and
  acceptance, authority, and G0 state remain unchanged.
- V3 complete-bundle contract: a new versioned successor preserves all V1/V2
  bytes, reconstructs effective V2, then applies thirteen exact operations to
  pin bundle, owner, evidence, authority, runner, gate, approval, and six-blob
  publication profiles. The private pure compiler derives 14 roles, 15
  non-derived evidence kinds, 15 role/blocker pairs, and two executable checks
  from effective V3. It rejects self-asserted outcomes, partial or ambiguous
  coverage, cross-binding and time drift, and unbounded input. Its exact
  synthetic complete fixture still returns `dormant_non_authorizing`. The local
  exact 13-field publication-receipt sidecar binds published commit `12c38154`,
  the six unchanged lineage records, the remotely read V3 checkpoint bytes, and
  the observed UTC time. Its raw bytes and final file identity are pinned, but
  fresh-clone/no-alternates and 18-file comparison provenance are not persisted;
  no independent trust adapter, activation path, or G1a authority was added.
- Sparse owner/catalog intake: the separate content-addressed
  `owner-catalog-input-candidate-v1.json` binds the published target and effective
  V3 digests. Its published `70350f5e` form starts with `responses: []` and all
  state flags false. After explicit user input on 2026-07-21, the public dormant
  compiler produced the current exact 1,452-byte candidate at raw
  SHA-256
  `0221d2d49e4bcccfd34fb6905102117fbf5632e27d3d2f2e23d53e29f47752bc`.
  It contains only the `roadmap_and_g0_checkpoint_publication`
  `proposed_as_written` response, `owner-candidate:repository-owner:v1`, and
  `user-input:session-20260721:item-2`; the ordered `reviewed_commit_scope:v1`
  and `published_checkpoint:v1` evidence references are present, both supporting
  artifact paths are null because their selectors are false, the change request
  is null, and every state remains false. Its checker derives the
  ten-blocker role/evidence graph from effective V3, admits only canonically
  ordered sparse proposals whose references are mechanically bound to exact
  role/evidence-kind/blocker slugs and versions, rejects free-form catalog
  values, mismatched or unsafe paths, contradictory dispositions, and
  non-derived-evidence violations, and always returns
  `draft_unverified_non_authorizing`. The packet contains no owner identity,
  catalog value, accepted decision, approval, credential, verified evidence, or
  blocker-closing input; actual candidate artifact bytes require separate typed
  review.
- Non-authorizing evidence payload profile:
  `evidence-supporting-artifact-candidate-profile-v1.json` is one versioned
  custom profile, not an artifact instance. Its exact 17,353 bytes are pinned at
  SHA-256
  `f8ad6742fcb569f408b5f4087b20f11f32cb497a8f9eec2fc3f255d8b22c226f`.
  It defines the closed common envelope, subject binding, resource bounds,
  sensitive-data exclusions, missing independent inputs, and immutable false
  authority state for both future artifacts. The reviewed-scope branch binds
  the exact `12c38154` parent/tree and all 18 ordered path/mode/blob/byte/hash
  entries; the checkpoint branch binds the exact 4,692-byte V3 checkpoint and
  bounded remote-readback observation while keeping the standalone acquisition
  transcript null. It also hash-binds the exact item-2 owner/catalog selector
  bytes and projects the blocker, source, owner/evidence reference, candidate
  version, selector index, reserved path, and false/null artifact state into
  each envelope. Any selector change requires a new profile, and repository
  artifact instances remain forbidden while the snapshot references are null.
  The pure supplied-bytes validator performs no artifact-directed filesystem,
  Git, network, process, or clock access, rejects selector drift, and always
  returns the non-authorizing sentinel. Both reserved `docs/evidence` instances
  remain absent while the selectors are false.
- Baseline-gate evidence readiness: the separate exact 19,697-byte
  `baseline-gate-evidence-readiness-profile-v1.json` is pinned at SHA-256
  `a0c8f45167e9a8f3a4fccbba65afbb928b29b88df2ea2090cc96043ba960af17`.
  It derives the five non-derived evidence kinds for
  `g0_assurance_artifacts_and_baseline_gate` from effective V3, cross-binds the
  three execution-result shapes to the exact two command-profile and three step
  digests, and defines bounded common envelope, ordered manifest, result,
  sensitive-data, and supersession contracts. The pure compiler emits the exact
  3,640-byte dormant plan at SHA-256
  `ce679bbb4ebf01e4f838726d4c8f224e48cdd8170b3b205e89a4a54ce2d32227`
  only in memory. Both commands stay `not_authorized`; authority and runner
  candidate refs in the plan are null, execution/acquisition and every state
  transition are false, and all five reserved evidence artifacts remain absent.
  The supplied-bytes static compiler deterministically returns the 5,763-byte
  canonical-assurance candidate at SHA-256
  `2d193cb2f3bddf4d202129b4a746a3bd3cbba05f1a879e748f8001eb5c138db4`
  and the 10,771-byte source-readback candidate at SHA-256
  `5df6ba51f3177424407078424fcff90dc2faa8d1c1d4e80e79e96486c3a54fc6`.
  The exact pair remains dormant and creates no artifact file. Twenty-two
  mutation tests cover all five shapes, rehash all 29 actual supplied source
  blobs and every ordered synthetic execution-manifest blob, bind the
  single canonical egress/process observation manifest digest, cross-bind
  command/step/session/time/log/output digests, require the full-gate success
  marker exactly once, reject duplicate/noncompact/oversized JSON and mutable
  pair re-snapshot, and preserve a no-file/process/socket/clock-I/O pure
  boundary. Even an exact synthetic candidate returns only the final dormant
  sentinel and cannot create a catalog record or receipt.
- Independent-validation candidate context: the new private module derives the
  exact seven trust-input kinds from effective V3 and accepts only factory-owned,
  opaque, deep-immutable result snapshots with one exact target binding. It
  cross-checks all six lineage bytes, the independent V3 checkpoint and
  readback time, owner/approval, authority, runner/gate, fifteen artifact bytes,
  two runners' manifest/log bytes, and the trusted validation-time ceiling.
  Nine mutation tests reject missing/reordered/duplicate/ambiguous inputs,
  target or remote drift, mutable/released/oversized buffers, material
  missing/extra/hash/length drift, coherent self-asserted candidate changes,
  late events, and I/O or clock use. Exact synthetic input returns only the
  distinct dormant sentinel. The module exports no acceptance or activation
  API and implements no real registry, revocation, owner, authority, runner, or
  artifact verifier; no consumed-bundle ledger, G0 exit, or G1a authority was
  added.
- Release-evidence boundary: a content hash alone cannot satisfy a release
  metric. The exact evidence envelope must carry the complete raw sample array,
  bind campaign/build/metric/threshold/platform/context, name an approved
  release-evidence signer, and pass injected Ed25519 verification over its
  canonical payload. Rates, Wilson bounds, counts, nearest-rank percentiles,
  maxima, capacity invariants, and gate outcomes are derived from those signed
  samples. The signer registry remains unavailable until the quality owners are
  named, so real campaigns currently fail closed.
- Evidence resource and interoperability boundary: the canonical signature
  profile pins UTF-8 ordering, escaping, literals, non-exponent decimal grammar,
  and one exact byte/SHA-256 vector. Envelopes stop at 4 MiB and 100,000 samples
  before signature verification or sorting. Decimal bytes are assembled exactly
  without ambient precision/trap dependence. Signer IDs use one pinned
  case-sensitive registry-key pattern, and 64-byte unpadded base64url signatures
  must round-trip to identical text before verifier dispatch. Baseline network
  cells require the `none` variant, while each required variant signs one exact
  raw observation per attempt. One-based attempt order, a 120,000ms timing
  ceiling, homogeneous per-record direct/fallback/rule-selected routes, affected
  plane/region, outage result, ordered restore and authenticated recovery, plus
  zero downgrade/post-consent counters drive the aggregate outcome; attempt
  count or a result string alone cannot represent successful fail-closed
  recovery.
- Assurance readback candidate: `docs/v1/g0/assurance-checkpoint-readback-v1.json`
  now records the exact assurance raw-byte and canonical SHA-256 values and an
  ordered readback of all 29 declared source records. Its standalone validator
  rehashes every exact repository-relative regular non-symlink file rather than
  trusting recorded `match` strings; eleven mutation tests reject checkpoint-
  byte, assurance-byte/canonical, exponent-overflow, over-128-digit integers,
  recursive type-confusion, source count/order/path/role/hash, the 4 MiB source
  ceiling, symlink, same-descriptor and final-path identity, owner, publication,
  blocker, status, and authority drift. The candidate explicitly
  remains `candidate_observed_not_immutable`: this embedded frozen state is not
  changed by the later publication observation and is not owner acceptance,
  receipt activation, or G0 exit evidence.
- Guard evidence: `script/check_v1_g0_decision.py` passes after verifying exact
  canonical decision/assurance content, nine decision source hashes, 29
  assurance source hashes, current Android/macOS configuration and locales,
  active protocol message/error parity, all ten blockers, and every false
  authority. Its 63 mutation tests reject nested unknown fields, security
  downgrades, missing hard stops, protocol/threat/user-loop removal, forbidden
  observability fields, invented approvals, arbitrary or unsigned evidence,
  forged percentile scalars, oversized or noncanonical evidence, ambient-
  Decimal precision collisions, malformed signer IDs, noncanonical base64url
  terminal bits, zero-based/late/mixed network-variant observations, unbound
  network-variant outcomes, and evidence-free checklist passes. The G0 checker
  now also requires the candidate readback validator. Its separate eleven-test
  mutation suite, the dormant publication candidate's eight-test suite, and
  the V3 lineage/bundle, sparse-intake, and evidence-profile candidate's
  seventeen-test suite form the earlier 99-test subtotal. The baseline-evidence
  readiness suite contributes 22 more tests, forming the previous 121-test
  subtotal. The independent-context suite contributes 9 more to form 130, the
  repository/remote-source suite contributes 8 to form 138, the owner-trust-
  bootstrap suite contributes 11 to form 149, and the external-evidence
  readiness suite contributes 11, so the nine focused G0 suites now contain
  160 tests in total and are included in the default no-device gate.
- Repository/remote source boundary: the new default checker reconstructs the
  exact 18-entry `929fda5f -> 12c38154` scope directly from Git objects and
  matches its pinned digest, exact parent/tree, and all six lineage blobs while
  ignoring HEAD, index, and worktree document bytes. It rejects replace,
  alternate, graft, shallow, and promisor/lazy-fetch state both before and
  after bounded streaming object reads. Evidence collection therefore requires
  one normal checkout with a complete local object store; shallow clones and
  linked worktrees are intentionally unsupported and fail closed. The remote half is deliberately only a
  pure matcher for supplied unauthenticated bytes and contains no socket client;
  it records remote-ref reachability, TLS peer authentication, and collector
  authentication as false. Eight tests pass, including worktree/network I/O
  absence and rejection of the two mechanical identities as a 2/7 context.
  This reduces adapter implementation work but closes none of the ten G0
  blockers and creates no trusted adapter, evidence, receipt, or authority.
- Consumed-bundle ledger stop boundary: a caller-selected host-local directory
  with same-UID marker files is not the external irreversible ledger required by
  V3. Different directories, directory replacement, snapshot rollback, or an
  unauthenticated early claim can defeat global one-shot semantics or cause
  permanent denial of service. A future activation design must instead pin one
  versioned namespace under an authenticated sole writer/coordinator, serialize
  every host, resist or reconcile storage rollback and backup restore, bind the
  canonical validated target/bundle from the 7/7 context, and durably verify the
  parent entry before success. No local ledger substitute was retained and no
  activation state was added.
- Offline gate hardening: all five Gradle invocations inside
  `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now include `--offline`. Future authorized
  execution must additionally use a runner-level egress deny and preseeded,
  hash-attested wrapper/plugins/dependencies because the wrapper may otherwise
  attempt bootstrap before Gradle applies project flags. No aggregate or release
  compilation was run by this correction. Copy hygiene now recognizes only the
  offline Gradle prefix for its active-command inventory and authoritative
  three-class coverage guard, so later removal of `--offline` fails statically.
- Current non-socket static verification also passes copy hygiene across 92
  user-facing files, docs hygiene across 12 current documents, Android/macOS
  five-locale parity, both protocol schemas, closed P2P/NAT and production-relay
  design validators, 21 documentation/launcher/Phase-A tests, shell syntax, and
  diff hygiene. This is not the full no-device aggregate.
- G0 status: `blocked_before_g1a`. The bounded V2/V3 packet is intentionally
  published at `12c38154`, and a fresh no-alternates repository directly matched
  all 18 approved remote file bytes. The V3 checkpoint readback ran from
  `2026-07-20T12:05:21Z` through `12:05:44Z`, yielding 4,692 bytes at SHA-256
  `37462cd8303ce61742bc480d0f7d37e0ccb380ec12375cc8c8d10169aebf4dc5`.
  V1/V2/V3 lineage bytes remain unchanged.
- Receipt/intake successor publication: the exact nine-file successor is
  published at `70350f5e9e5e39d1b793862c1e58d09edf637405`. A distinct fresh
  no-alternates HTTPS fetch matched all nine remote bytes from
  `2026-07-20T13:54:08Z` through `13:54:12Z`; the ordered remote file-manifest
  SHA-256 is `feffe729aba826c4692fb408f9e4b4f42f7f4823f92dc6325587c0aac7a8dd46`.
  The generic immutable commit subject does not define scope; the canonical
  handoff records the path-order serialization and nine individual blob hashes.
  That manifest is reproducible from the commit, but no standalone HTTPS
  acquisition transcript persists the separate no-alternates/readback session.
- Truth-sync/compiler successor publication: the exact seven-file successor is
  published at `025a4ef5e6c3e52c46a6b79ee3a06a6eb47de4e0`, with parent
  `70350f5e` and tree `e1272bdf`. A fresh no-alternates HTTPS `blob:none`
  partial clone matched remote `main`, the ordered changed-path set, modes, blob
  IDs, byte counts, and seven raw blob hashes from `2026-07-21T01:15:22Z`
  through `01:15:28Z`. The canonical seven-line manifest SHA-256 is
  `d534e068f412bed2ea4926f5eb206b6a4343fa4ed8d04f87c11193bc4a5cdb25`.
  This session observation is not a trusted receipt and grants no G0 authority.
- Evidence-readiness/source successor publication: the exact sixteen-file
  successor is published at `b24c5ecb77067539c185d88f0c2fbbc2cb119c15`,
  with parent `025a4ef5` and tree `c8aa9e69`. A strict fresh HTTPS `blob:none`
  partial clone with isolated Git configuration and no alternates, grafts,
  replacement refs, linked worktree, or shallow state observed remote `main`
  from `2026-07-21T07:55:12Z` through `07:55:22Z`. All sixteen target blobs
  were initially absent, then matched the exact commit-object bytes, lengths,
  raw SHA-256 values, and recomputed Git object IDs; commit, parent, and root
  tree bytes also matched. The canonical 1,706-byte manifest SHA-256 is
  `1b91a321de9a39faf9fb519b47ffa6e82ce85dd48595f092a63581875c9d4a37`.
  The ephemeral 9,265-byte command transcript SHA-256 was
  `98d08c6bd76289c4d89218d689d50e788cc8b4167b559cb618ddd7c9ae886690`,
  but its bytes were not persisted or signed. This is a bounded session
  observation independent only of the existing worktree/local object database,
  not an authenticated collector receipt or G0 authority.
- Publication-receipt status: the tracked exact 13-field sidecar encodes the
  observed target/checkpoint/hash/time only as a dormant candidate. It does not
  persist or independently reproduce the parent acquisition provenance and is
  not consumed by an independently anchored activation-capable trust adapter.
  The new candidate context core does not change that fact and establishes no
  owner, authority, runner, gate, approval,
  G0-exit, or G1a state.
- Owner/catalog-input status: the packet published at `b24c5ecb` is content-addressed
  and contains exactly one explicit publication proposal with the repository
  owner candidate plus two evidence candidates whose artifact references are
  null.
  Structural validation proves only that this proposal is bound to the canonical
  target and graph; it does not authenticate the proposed owner, verify an
  artifact, accept the disposition, activate a receipt, or close any of the ten
  blockers. The empty packet remains the historical published `70350f5e` blob.
- Owner trust-adapter readiness audit: effective V3 already machine-pins the
  exact ten-field owner binding and eight-field approval receipt, including
  role, validity, evidence, and opaque revocation/provenance references; the
  separate publication receipt supplies bundle-level repository/commit/checkpoint
  binding. Neither the current checker nor the generic independent-context
  factory authenticates an external registry snapshot, caller-supplied source,
  or provenance. The remaining gap is not another generic owner schema: no
  external owner identity registry/trust anchor, credential and proof-of-control
  mechanism, registry/revocation acquisition and rollback policy, exact adapter
  output/context contract, or trusted-time/skew policy has been selected. The new
  provider-neutral owner-trust-bootstrap profile records that gap without filling
  it. The user has now declared one sole human project owner. V3 does not require
  fourteen different people: it requires fourteen unique opaque role-scoped
  `ownerIdentityRef` values. The profile therefore binds the exact canonical
  fourteen-role digest to one human principal while preserving the V3 requirement
  for one future binding and one separately authenticated receipt per role. This
  interpretation requires no V4
  closure amendment. The external registry must later prove that all fourteen
  role aliases resolve to the same authenticated principal. All ten trust
  selection references remain null, all eleven state flags remain false, and the
  adapter projection remains `not_implemented`. Its supplied-byte-only checker
  reuses the existing V3 owner-binding/approval-receipt definitions and never
  constructs an adapter result. A signature profile and any detached replay
  challenge remain conditional choices, not implicit V3 receipt fields. No
  adapter, selector, evidence, receipt, blocker, G0-exit, or G1a state was created.
- Owner trust-bootstrap static verification: the profile raw SHA-256 is
  `229120fcaf7a03b0920b67466ef281a6e739146da72ffab34c10a1f6ed49542b`.
  Eleven focused mutation tests pass. Its earlier complete default no-device
  aggregate exited zero, but that run predates the external-evidence addendum and
  is not used as evidence for the expanded gate. These are no-device
  static/build/smoke results, not external owner or trust proof.
- External-evidence typed readiness: the 25,082-byte
  `docs/v1/g0/external-evidence-candidate-profile-v1.json` is pinned at raw
  SHA-256 `e5233e7b52369299aa50f07adb726ec57c0ea5e4ffe138e0b2725b8d5f87b371`.
  It revalidates and content-addresses the existing five-kind baseline and
  two-kind supporting profiles, subtracts those exact seven kinds from the
  effective V3 set of fifteen, and derives the remaining eight kinds plus their
  seven blockers, five non-executable checks, and seven owner roles. Typed
  readiness is therefore 15/15, while eight candidate artifacts remain absent.
  All candidate selectors remain null/false, all trust and authority states
  remain false, and candidate references carry only allowlisted class, SHA-256,
  and version data. Eleven focused mutation tests and the combined nine-suite
  160-test G0 run pass; all nine G0 checkers also pass. This creates no public
  value, account, owner authentication, evidence verification, approval,
  receipt, blocker closure, G0 exit, or G1a authority.
- Reference-only proposal preparation: the receipt-bundle module now exports
  one dormant preview compiler. It snapshots and strictly parses bounded JSON
  selector-request bytes, derives canonical blocker/role/evidence references
  and safe optional artifact paths from the exact V3 graph, normalizes order,
  keeps all state false, and returns deterministic bytes plus SHA-256 only after
  dormant validator round-trip. It performs no file, network, or process I/O,
  authentication, acceptance, or activation. The approved selector for the
  publication blocker now replay-compiles to the tracked bytes and pinned
  SHA-256 exactly.
- Post-publication worktree status: the preview compiler and preceding
  truth-sync are published in `025a4ef5`; the exact sixteen-file selector,
  non-authorizing-readiness, candidate independent-context, and mechanical
  repository/remote-source successor is published and read back at `b24c5ecb`.
  `main` and `origin/main` were clean and aligned before the current twelve-path
  local scope: four canonical truth-sync documents, the owner-trust-bootstrap
  profile/checker/tests, the external-evidence candidate profile/checker/tests,
  and the no-device plus copy-hygiene registrations. None is staged, committed,
  or pushed. The assistant performs only edits and verification; the sole owner
  retains staging, commit, and push control after review.
- Remaining boundary: accountable owner acceptance, catalog artifacts,
  authorized aggregate/release-compile evidence, namespaces/accounts/custody,
  provider/service/privacy/quality/capacity ownership, and all later execution
  authorities remain unresolved or false. G1a, source acquisition, P2P selection
  or compile, sockets, network I/O, production keys, signing, upload, and
  deployment remain closed. The user-directed local development diagnostics
  below do not satisfy or alter those canonical command-authority gates.
- Live Android evidence boundary: an authorized USB `SM-S936N` on Android 16/API
  36 was used. The current debug APK rebuilt successfully and `adb install -r`
  preserved data. Cold launches completed in 632 ms and 612 ms; force-stop
  removed the process, relaunch changed the PID, and the saved trusted-runtime,
  latest-QR-required, and auto-reconnect state remained visible. Three
  ADB-deeplink/local-development-relay smokes passed: pairing plus saved-route
  reconnect; physical UI send/delta/cancel/done plus complete chat/model/drawer/
  settings/launcher PNG/XML capture; and send/three-delta/natural-done plus

## 2026-07-20 Android Runtime Session Summary Linear Merge

- Goal and result: the authoritative Android runtime-history batch merge now builds the deleted-session set, first-wins persisted-session index, local-only collision set, and incoming-summary deduplication set once. It no longer rebuilds suppressions or linearly searches every persisted session for every incoming summary, reducing merge lookup work from `O(summary * (persisted + suppressed))` to `O(summary + persisted + suppressed)` without changing UI, protocol, ordering, or trust behavior.
- Compatibility contract: the first legacy persisted row for a duplicate session id still wins, manual titles, composer drafts, messages, archive state, and runtime search metadata keep their existing merge rules, duplicate incoming summaries remain first-wins, Android-local ID collisions remain local, and deletion tombstones remain fail-closed. The existing final `sanitized()` ordering and active-session cleanup remain unchanged.
- Deterministic regression: `runtimeSessionSummaryBatchMergeReadsPersistedStateLinearlyAndPreservesFirstWinsState` uses 1,003 persisted rows, 1,001 suppressions, and more than 1,000 summaries. A counting list proves the original session list is read at most once plus one tolerance and the suppression list at most twice plus two tolerance, including final sanitization; it also checks first-wins state, local collision, deletion suppression, and search metadata. This is an access-count proof, not a wall-clock benchmark.
- Current Android evidence: the three focused summary regressions pass, all 634 `RuntimeClientViewModelTest` tests pass, and `build/qa/android-session-summary-linear-full-20260720.log` records the complete Android run and `:app:assembleDebug` succeeding in 30 seconds. The refreshed JUnit XML reports contain 1,141 app, 162 protocol, 95 transport, and 130 pairing JVM tests with zero failures, errors, or skips.
- Gate repair: the standalone 11-test documentation-handoff suite exposed three stale Status fixture expectations after the earlier UI checker update. Its fixture now models `performRuntimeOverviewAction`; callback-swap, comment, and string decoys remain rejected, and all 11 tests pass. Copy hygiene pins the one-time indexes, first-wins lookup, deterministic regression, and no-device coverage marker.
- Integrated evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-session-summary-linear-final-20260720.log` exits zero across 8,806 lines in 580.459 seconds. It contains one overall success marker, one session-summary linear-merge marker, one 143-filter Swift subsumption marker, all 1,809 Swift tests with two explicit environment-dependent skips and zero failures, the Android selection including the complete ViewModel class, authenticated direct/relay smokes, and both Swift product builds.
- Agent and authority boundary: GPT-5.6 Sol read-only review selected this lower-risk Android slice and separately identified strict JSON validator allocation reduction as a future candidate. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. No protocol/schema, UI/design, persistence format, trust, permission, provider, network, P2P Phase B, production, or deployment authority changed. The Android phone remains disconnected, so no physical-device, TalkBack, camera, haptic, or process-death evidence is claimed.

## 2026-07-19 macOS Debug Local QR And Android Optical Pairing Recovery

- Goal and scope: restore the complete macOS debug path from route readiness through pairing-session creation, QR payload generation, and visible rendering, then prove the QR with the physical camera on an attached Android device. This is a same-Wi-Fi development recovery only; release remote-route policy, external networking, P2P/NAT Phase B, production permissions, and deployment authority are unchanged.
- Root cause: the macOS product UI exposed only `remoteRequired` pairing even when a clean development machine had no relay bootstrap or lease, so no pairing session reached the renderer. Android's lower parser already understood explicit `local_diagnostic` payloads in debug builds, but the optical/deep-link entry path still forced remote-route validation and rejected a QR that the camera had recognized.
- macOS result: one model-owned UI request now prefers a complete remote route and otherwise permits an explicit local-diagnostic route only under a debug assertion build. It starts the runtime when needed, requires the listener to reach `advertising`, binds the QR to a validated nonloopback host and actual listener port, and leaves release/default remote APIs fail-closed. A caller-supplied constructor override cannot enable the path in a release build. The default host selector rejects virtual interfaces and prioritizes SystemConfiguration's primary IPv4 interface before other physical candidates. Pairing, toolbar, menu-bar, Status quick actions, and startup use the generic decision; Connection Recovery has a separately wired remote-only callback so `Generate Latest QR` cannot silently create a local QR.
- Android result: debug optical/deep-link admission accepts only the already-defined explicit local-diagnostic route scope, while release builds continue to require a canonical remote route. Tests pin both sides of that build policy and prove the same compact local QR is rejected under the release-style requirement.
- Development launch result: the ad-hoc debug launcher supplies an owner-only file-backed runtime identity, avoiding repeated Keychain authorization prompts that could prevent the listener from becoming ready. A reproduced LaunchServices `open --env` startup stall was removed by directly launching the signed bundle executable under `nohup`, retaining the parent for a measured five-second SwiftUI startup window, and checking the exact launch PID before returning. Production runtime identity behavior remains Keychain-first.
- Render and device evidence: the live AetherLink window exposed `pairing-active-qr`; Vision decoded the actual full-screen QR to an `aetherlink` pairing URI with all required fields, `direct_host=192.168.0.113`, `direct_port=43170`, and `route_scope=local_diagnostic`. The protected payload was not persisted in the repository; its SHA-256 was `efc77b1402ed6270b741e5ee69bb30a7527ad563876f58eee31e7587ef9544ef`.
- Physical optical evidence: an attached `SM-S936N` on the same Wi-Fi scanned the displayed QR through the camera, without URI or deep-link injection. Android then connected to `192.168.0.113:43170` with source `PairingQr`, completed `pairing.request`/`pairing.result`, the hello/challenge/response authentication exchange, and `runtime.health`; macOS reported one trusted device. After the core route remediations and a fresh macOS app build, Android force-stop and relaunch rediscovered the host through Bonjour, authenticated with the stored trust relationship, and received `runtime.health` again. The subsequent UI-only remote-callback and local launch-script fixes were Mac-verified after the phone was released: the final app listened on port 43170 and its visible QR decoded to the same primary-interface endpoint and local-diagnostic scope.
- Regression evidence: the complete `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests` selection passed 525/525 before the review remediations. Five focused final tests pass 5/5 for debug generation, failed-listener closure, release override closure, local recovery after explicit remote failure without allocator replay, and primary-interface scoring. Localization passed 137/137. The 15-configuration live-path QR render regression passed again and Vision decoded its English/light bitmap; the separate 15-configuration primary-surface render regression covers Connection Recovery. Android debug pairing/navigation policy tests passed, `:app:assembleRelease` passed including `lintVital`, and the final debug APK installed and launched on the attached device. Copy/localization/script/docs hygiene, shell syntax, release Swift build, and the verified ad-hoc app build also passed. Final GPT-5.6 Sol review reported no remaining P0-P2 finding.
- Proof boundary: this proves current-screen optical QR recognition, pairing, authentication, health exchange, trust persistence, and relaunch reconnection for one Android device on one same-Wi-Fi debug route. It does not prove expiry/retry UX, TalkBack/VoiceOver traversal, different-network routing, relay allocation, external-network reliability, P2P/NAT traversal, Phase B, production performance, deployment, or production readiness.

## 2026-07-19 Android Bounded Volatile Persistence Optimization

- Goal and scope: remove Android `RuntimeClientViewModel` whole-state `SharedPreferences` write amplification from composer typing and active nonterminal `chat.delta` handling without weakening terminal, lifecycle, session, setting, trust, approval, or pairing-secret durability. UI and in-memory state still update immediately. Wire/schema behavior, pairing trust, secret lifetime, networking, P2P Phase B, and production authority are unchanged.
- Coalescing contract: only those two volatile call sites use one fixed first-change window capped at 250 ms. The coordinator retains a dirty bit and generation, not a secret-bearing snapshot, and reads the latest complete sanitized state only when it flushes. One hundred composer updates therefore produce one scheduled save, and one hundred deltas produce one scheduled save. An update at 249 ms does not move the original 250 ms deadline.
- Supersession contract: every immediate write increments the generation, cancels the pending job, and writes its own latest complete snapshot while holding the coordinator lock. A canceled stale job cannot later restore an old session, route, draft, or deleted relay-secret reference.
- Durable barriers: `chat.done`, malformed terminals, active and local errors, rejected sends, cancel dispatch, send/session mutation, settings, trust and approval state, pairing-route creation/replacement/deletion, `onActivityPaused`, `onActivityStopped`, `onActivitySaveInstanceState`, and `onCleared` use confirmed synchronous metadata writes. Pairing relay secrets implement a separate durable-store contract; a durable write saves the new encrypted secret first, commits metadata, and only then removes an obsolete secret, with every synchronous result checked. A secret store without confirmation support fails closed before metadata is committed.
- Partial-failure contract: pending-route metadata commit failure compensates a newly written secret, while secret-bound `pending-relay-v2` handles prevent a failed same-route replacement from overwriting the previous secret. Replacement/deletion records the old handle in the same metadata commit and removes it through a retryable cleanup journal. Trusted-route DataStore updates use a secret-bound `relay-v2` handle, persist the new encrypted secret before metadata, atomically journal the previous owned handle, and retry failed cleanup while excluding the currently referenced handle. Both stores compare the protected value first and skip unchanged secret rewrites.
- Writer ownership: `MainActivity` is `singleTask` with document launching disabled, and its existing `onNewIntent` path receives launcher, deep-link, share, and diagnostic entry intents. This prevents ordinary duplicate Activity/ViewModel full-snapshot writers; externally forced exotic multi-task or multi-process writers still require an application-scoped versioned repository or compare-and-swap design.
- Focused evidence: the 19 deterministic regressions pass 19/19, including the GPT-5.6 Sol remediation for malformed/error boundaries, same-route metadata failure, current-safe cleanup journals, unchanged-secret write suppression, and trusted-secret rotation. The complete Android app suite passes 1,125/1,125, including 633 ViewModel, 15 local-store, and 158 navigation tests; pairing passes 130/130, release Kotlin compilation succeeds, and the synchronized copy gate tracks 749 unique suite-subsumed Android evidence selectors. The final GPT-5.6 Sol closure review reports `No actionable P0-P3 remains`. The synchronized aggregate `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-android-volatile-persistence-final-v2-20260719.log` exits 0 across 8,998 lines in 593 seconds with exactly one overall success marker, one dedicated persistence marker, five successful Gradle invocations, passing copy/docs hygiene, freshness across 56 local relay connections, and 905 encrypted frame bodies.
- Lint boundary: `:app:lintDebug` reaches analysis but remains red on five pre-existing CameraX/Compose opt-in or context-access errors in untouched files. The new synchronous preference commits are narrowly annotated because their purpose is to return a durability result; this is not a production latency benchmark.
- Residual behavior: the scheduled 250 ms path intentionally uses asynchronous `apply()`. Abrupt process death before a lifecycle/terminal barrier, or before that asynchronous write reaches storage, can lose the newest draft or partial delta. A stalled main dispatcher can extend the 250 ms wall-clock delay, and the infrequent durable barriers can synchronously block the main thread. These limits are explicit rather than treated as crash-proof persistence or measured device performance.
- Proof boundary: current evidence is Android JVM, Robolectric, static, and release-compile evidence without an attached device. It does not prove physical-device process killing, camera QR, TalkBack, external networking, peer receipt, production performance, deployment, or production readiness.

## 2026-07-19 Whole-Codebase Optimization And Hardening Pass

- Goal and coverage: preserve the existing QR-recovery work while re-auditing the complete tracked first-party source and core documentation for correctness, concurrency, memory and I/O bounds, security boundaries, UX/accessibility, and build/test cost. Independent GPT-5.6 Sol passes covered the macOS runtime and stores, providers and relay, macOS UI, Android app and core modules, scripts, and evidence contracts. No P0 survived validation; validated P1 findings and low-risk measurable P2 findings were implemented with direct regressions.
- SQLite chat store: ordinary append and batch operations validate each sanitized event and its targeted compaction binding, then add one per-event search projection in the same transaction. They no longer replay or rebuild the complete durable history on every append. A stale mutation revision or projection-version migration still performs one fail-closed full-history repair and validation before marking the projection current. Exact normalized substring candidates currently use `instr` over the owner-scoped event projection; multi-delta streams are conservative candidates, and only candidate sessions are replayed for exact JSONL-equivalent ranking.
- Host approval and model residency: a wrong, repeated, or concurrent reservation receipt fails immediately instead of waiting on a persistence callback that may never return. Ambiguous persistence poisons the coordinator, clears every pending in-memory review and active execution, blocks approve/dismiss, and requires explicit unfinished-work recovery. Cancellation and deadlines return without waiting for cancellation-ignoring persistence. Aggregate chat and embedding streams use bounded buffering; cancellation can leave a shared or self-started provider unload running while the cancelled caller returns, without admitting a replacement model early.
- Provider and file bounds: Ollama and LM Studio nonstreaming bodies are incrementally capped at 32 MiB with a 60-second absolute deadline, status-preserving bounded errors, cancellation-driven URL task teardown, and duplicate-key rejection. Ollama model-detail fanout is capped at four concurrent requests with deterministic catalog order. Document ingestion snapshots an opened, descriptor-validated regular file into an owner-only temporary file before PDFKit, archive tools, or `textutil` read it; the snapshot is no-follow and capped at 32 MiB plus a sentinel. One cumulative cooperative 30-second budget covers snapshot work and interruptible external helpers, but synchronous filesystem calls, PDFKit, and in-process rich-text parsing cannot be forcibly interrupted. Public direct ingestion/chunking also rejects more than 1 MiB UTF-8 or 200,000 characters. Trusted-device persistence is capped at 1 MiB, 256 rows, and bounded identifiers, names, and keys while retaining owner-only permissions and ACL removal.
- Android client: attachment loading streams directly into bounded Base64 output on one I/O lane with cancellation checks, a 384 KiB per-file and per-request decoded ceiling, a 512 KiB final encoded-request ceiling, 255-character display names, 128-character MIME types, and a one-byte oversize sentinel. The final protocol envelope and frame are preflighted before draft or attachment state is cleared, and one cooperative 15-second timeout bounds interruptible attachment I/O. Composer/session mutation is locked while ingestion is active. Trusted-source approval and dismissal confirmations bind the exact review, channel, authentication generation, session, and message state and are consumed once; stale or replayed confirmations cannot mutate trust.
- Relay and QR recovery: every smoke-owned ephemeral loopback relay is launched through a durable supervisor that records exact process identity, resets child termination signals before `exec`, bounds TERM/KILL cleanup, and can recover its bound child after abrupt supervisor loss. The relay also receives the outer gate parent pid and exits if that owner dies; bootstrap smoke additionally requires an allocation before accepting a peer. This changes test-process ownership only and adds no production relay authority. Connection Recovery stores one atomic model-owned active/completed request state, clears obsolete completed state on every route invalidation, preserves active state across view recreation, consumes only the matching terminal request id, and reports refresh success only when route preparation produces a different pairing-session identity.
- Current focused evidence: Android Studio JBR passes all 1,110 app JVM tests, including 625 ViewModel, 280 Compose, and 9 local-store tests, plus `:app:compileReleaseKotlin`. Swift passes approval TSan 29/29, combined approval plus SQLite chat-event 116/116, provider/store/approval selections totaling 303 tests with two intentionally disabled localhost live tests, document/trusted-device 68/68, localization plus aggregate residency 180/180, full `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests` 523/523, and paired-route refresh 10/10. The monotonic approval-boundary regression passes 50/50 repeated runs after its deadline watcher is deterministically armed. The durable no-ADB lifecycle module passes 12/12, and the synchronized five-module Python selection passes 31/31.
- Final integrated evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-whole-codebase-pass1-20260719.log` exits 0 across 9,005 lines with one overall success marker and five successful Gradle invocations. It includes 136/136 macOS localization, 19/19 rendered UI, the previously failing broad 272-test approval selection, local emit-only QR generation and decode, and authenticated loopback relay coverage. After documentation synchronization, `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-whole-codebase-final-20260719.log` is the final-source aggregate. The final GPT-5.6 Sol delta review reports no P0/P1/P2 finding; the model-pull deadline wait remains internal test-only API.
- Deferred with evidence: owner-scoped `instr` candidate search and the multi-delta boundary query remain linear scans, and the first v2 projection repair intentionally reads the complete event log. Replacing them safely requires a versioned trigram/cross-event projection migration and differential search evidence, so it is not folded into this low-risk pass. At this checkpoint Android draft/stream coalescing was deferred; the bounded implementation and its explicit process-death limits are now recorded in the section above. Residual P3 test/process risks are a one-second pair-refresh completion poll on extremely slow CI, second-resolution `lstart` plus command identity under an extreme PID-reuse collision, and a kernel-uninterruptible child that can outlive bounded TERM/KILL cleanup; no portable stronger process birth identifier or user-space D-state termination was available without weakening secret handling or bounded teardown.
- Authority and proof boundary: no wire/schema shape, pairing trust, secret lifetime, approval policy, source acquisition, P2P candidate, socket destination, runtime-network permission, Phase B, production network, or deployment authority was expanded. Current evidence is local Swift/JVM, static, temporary-file, and loopback evidence; it is not physical Android camera/TalkBack, real VoiceOver traversal, live provider, external-network, peer-receipt, P2P/NAT, production-capacity, or deployment proof.

## 2026-07-18 macOS Pairing QR Recovery and Rendered Payload No-Device Gate

- Scope: repair the macOS QR generation path while the Android phone is disconnected. No physical Android camera scan, optical reliability, external relay, production P2P/NAT, or real different-network claim is made.
- Root cause: default pairing requires complete canonical remote route material, but clean first-run Pairing hid the only Connection Recovery entry point and disabled QR generation. The stale bundled app also predated the current source. The first asynchronous repair still had four fail-open edges: a timeout could leave its socket worker alive, malformed nonempty bootstrap lists could be accepted, a fresh lease could look QR-ready without its Keychain secret, and destructive actions from different windows could race one shared allocation.
- Result: clean first-run Pairing exposes Connection Recovery and expands its setup when neither saved nor automatic route preparation exists; Status keeps clean-first-run diagnostics hidden. Pairing, toolbar, menu-bar, Status, and Connection Recovery actions consume one core-owned `canRequestRemotePairingForUserInterface` decision, so UI availability cannot outrun the canonical core preconditions.
- Result: pairing, manual, bootstrap, startup, and renewal allocation use cancellation-aware requests. The built-in allocator separately proves that one absolute deadline covers DNS, connect, send, and incremental response reads and that cancellation drives `NWConnection.cancel()`. The model separately keeps asynchronous and public synchronous allocation entry points closed until its allocator worker posts completion acknowledgement; every late completion is inert. These tests prove both contracts independently, not one end-to-end timeout-to-socket trace.
- Result: a nonempty bootstrap list is accepted only when every comma-delimited endpoint is canonical and has a valid nonzero port. Missing or failed protected-store secret read-back, a mismatched secret handle, host-only environment override, ineligible relay host, missing lease, stale lease, or noncanonical material keeps QR generation unavailable. Only a secret explicitly supplied by the active relay environment is treated as ephemeral environment authority. Empty bootstrap removal and destructive route actions use guarded core operations, so separate windows cannot clear shared state during allocation.
- Result: failed renewal preserves the last visible QR. QR image rendering is injected and cached by exact active payload; a transient render failure changes the complete active card to localized retry guidance, suppresses claims that depend on a visible QR, and retries without adding a direct/local fallback. Relay client callbacks remain generation-bound.
- Render evidence: a `remoteRequired` pairing QR rendered on screen in light and dark appearances was decoded from the actual bitmap with Vision. The decoded value exactly matched the active compact payload, included complete canonical remote relay fields, and omitted direct host/port fallback fields. The complete failure card renders without a decodable QR across five languages and System/Light/Dark appearances.
- Packaging result: `script/build_and_run.sh` now seals the completed bundle with a local ad-hoc deep signature after resources and `Info.plist` are installed, then requires strict deep signature verification. A fresh `dist/AetherLink.app` was built, verified on disk, launched, and observed running; this is local packaging evidence, not an optical scan result.
- Guardrail: focused Pairing, Transport, CompanionCore, localization/accessibility, and rendered-QR tests cover canonical boundaries, worker cancellation, absolute deadlines, malformed endpoint lists, missing secrets, multi-window destructive races, renewal preservation, clean-first-run recovery, stale callback suppression, and exact bitmap decode.
- Caveat: this is no-device SwiftPM/render/source evidence. A physical Android camera scan, real TalkBack/VoiceOver traversal, live external route allocation, production relay/P2P/NAT behavior, and real different-network pairing remain separate proof boundaries.

Verified in this working pass:

- Nine focused fail-closed regressions pass 9/9: draining-worker retry closure, public synchronous allocation-entry closure, malformed bootstrap rejection, shared-allocation destructive-action rejection, missing-secret rejection, failed protected-store write/read-back rejection, host-only environment handle mismatch rejection, explicit environment-secret control, and built-in allocator slow-trickle absolute deadline.
- The broad CompanionAppModel pairing/relay/route selection passes 54/54; PairingCoordinator, RelayPeerClient, paired allocation, paired route refresh, and TCP allocator selection passes 59/59.
- Full localization passes 128/128. The latest-source 19/19 rendered UI suite includes actual on-screen QR bitmap Vision decode, completes in 267.530 seconds, and writes no credential-bearing QR evidence to disk.
- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, `python3 script/check_macos_localization.py`, P2P and production-relay security design validators, and `git diff --check` pass at the post-remediation checkpoint.
- Final GPT-5.6 Sol delta re-review reports no P0-P3 finding after the restart regression was made causally eligible for saved-route renewal and the aggregate gate guard began parsing one exact nine-selector Swift test command.
- `./script/build_and_run.sh verify` produced, strictly signature-verified, and launched a fresh `dist/AetherLink.app`. The full SDK-path `adb devices -l` reports no attached device. A live window screenshot is not claimed; deterministic SwiftUI bitmap rendering remains the visual evidence.
- Integrity refresh: the 13-file closed P2P/NAT collection is `6e6dfbfc0cdb70370c30f54222584b69042a6e22b6df04c7f3e65043c38522bd` and the 17-file still-unselected production-relay collection is `e188e1b885419e376b9dcea85282b4aafb1d48692b134edc13aff2eedfbf6b66`. Both validators pass while compiler, socket, runtime-network, Phase B, production implementation, and deployment gates remain closed.

It records five successful Gradle invocations, 128/128 macOS localization tests, 19/19 macOS render tests in 260.992 seconds, freshness across 56 local relay connections, and 905 encrypted relay frame bodies.

It directly executes the exact nine QR regressions 9/9 in 9.727 seconds, records five successful Gradle builds, passes 128/128 localization and 19/19 render tests in 259.464 seconds, and verifies freshness across 56 local relay connections plus 905 encrypted frame bodies.

## 2026-07-18 Cross-Platform Readiness UI Pass

- Goal: macOS Runtime and Android client keep their distinct platform roles while each first-run surface presents the current blocking state and one next action. This slice changes presentation and navigation only; existing pairing callbacks, QR payloads, trust decisions, connection policy, permissions, and network behavior remain unchanged.
- macOS: active pairing now uses regular, compact side-by-side, and stacked layouts rather than a material card nested inside `CompanionPanel`. The QR scales from 252 points to 184 or 220 points by the fitting layout, while route status, expiry progress, and `Generate New QR` remain on the same task surface. The screen owns its window background for deterministic light/dark rendering. The successful-payload QR cache retries a transient render failure instead of permanently caching `nil`. A first trusted-device transition from `0` to `1` moves the first-run Pairing screen to Status; pairing an additional device keeps Pairing visible.
- Android: before a Runtime is trusted, the primary Settings section shows only QR pairing, the Runtime privacy boundary, and pending-route status when applicable. After trust, the setup QR surface is hidden and Settings exposes exactly one state-appropriate pairing action: connect a usable saved route or scan the latest QR for recovery. Route status remains informational in Settings, while actionable Status surfaces use an explicit text button instead of making the full status card clickable. QR actions use `QrCodeScanner`, secondary text uses `onSurfaceVariant`, and readiness surfaces use the shared 8 dp radius.
- Focused verification: four macOS navigation/cache tests, two compact pairing render tests, and the full 279-test Android no-device Compose class pass. The Android bounds cover 260 dp width, 1.5 font scale, and English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French. macOS render tests cover five languages, System/Light/Dark, accessibility text, and in-memory QR/renewal frame bounds at 520 by 640 points; one deterministic light-English render is also decoded with Vision and must equal the exact active pairing payload. No pairing payload or QR bitmap is persisted by the render tests.
- Review: final GPT-5.6 Sol platform re-reviews report no remaining P0-P3 finding after the TalkBack static-copy suppression, explicit action/card split, exact icon mapping guard, exact rendered-QR decoding, selected-section integration guard, and render-artifact persistence guard were tightened.
- Final aggregate: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-cross-platform-readiness-ui-final-20260718.log` exits 0 across 12,018 lines in 757.00 seconds with one overall success marker and one readiness UI marker. It records 124/124 macOS localization tests, 16/16 macOS render tests in 248.520 seconds, the Android no-device Compose class in the passing app selector, freshness across 56 local relay connections, and 905 encrypted frame bodies. Post-gate `adb devices -l` lists no attached device.
- Scope: this is local no-device frontend evidence. No Android device or emulator is attached, and no physical camera QR, TalkBack, VoiceOver traversal, live-provider, external-network, Phase B, production, or deployment behavior is claimed.

## Historical 2026-07-18 Cross-Codebase Optimization Pass

- Coverage: five independent GPT-5.6 Sol audits inspected the complete disjoint inventory of 302 tracked first-party code files at baseline at least once: 95 macOS runtime/storage/provider files, 55 relay/protocol/transport files, 15 macOS UI files, 70 Android files, and 67 script/shared/schema/build files. This pass adds one reviewed `RuntimeLocalStoreTest.kt`, so the current inventory is 303 code files. Binary assets, generated output, retained archives, logs, screenshots, and evidence documents were not treated as source code. No P0 finding survived validation.
- Previous slice retained: relay epoch material remains cached per direction and epoch, pairing QR images remain cached by exact payload, Android frame reads still fill one exact destination buffer, and the macOS localization/render suites still execute once. The prior final aggregate remains historical evidence for those changes.
- SQLite chat store at this historical checkpoint: append and batch paths refreshed only affected session-level FTS rows after the then-current validation path. The 2026-07-19 pass supersedes this implementation with a per-event projection and targeted candidate replay, so this entry is retained as historical evidence rather than a description of current code.
- macOS localization and relay: the five supported localized `Bundle` objects are resolved once through a thread-safe immutable cache while current language selection and string lookup remain live per call. Direct relay forwarding now sends the valid prefix of its reusable 64 KiB buffer without constructing `Data(buffer.prefix(...))`; partial send, `EINTR`, broken-pipe, reset, shutdown, syscall count, and wire bytes remain unchanged.
- Android persistence and transport: `RuntimeLocalStore` keeps immediate `SharedPreferences.apply()` timing, re-reads the latest persisted secret reference on every save so interleaved store instances clean up the current handle, and computes one sanitized disk projection per save. Direct transport retains its single complete-frame write and existing open-socket failure policy. Relay transport, which already closes on every write failure, writes the exact four-byte prefix and body separately under its send mutex to remove the body-sized combined allocation without changing wire bytes.
- Gate efficiency: Android no-device selection moved every valid named selector already covered by an active complete class into a non-executing reviewed evidence block. The retained snapshot now has 730 unique selectors; copy hygiene rejects duplicates and selectors that do not name an actual Kotlin `@Test` method. The same non-subsumed selectors remain active while Gradle invocations fall from 11 to 5 and active selectors from 786 to 48. Dynamic JUnit XML checks still run immediately after the complete app classes.
- Focused verification: after final-review remediation of direct partial-frame reuse, stale cross-instance secret refs, and selector source parsing, Android Studio JBR passes `RuntimeLocalStoreTest` 5/5, direct `RuntimeTransportClientTest` 4/4, and relay `RuntimeRelayTcpClientTest` 20/20 with no failure, error, or skip. The integrated source also passes all 76 `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests`, both localization-cache regressions, the relay raw-buffer/socket regressions, copy/docs/localization/production-relay static validation, Python compilation, shell syntax, and `git diff --check`.
- Final aggregate and review: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-cross-codebase-optimization-v2-final-r2-20260718.log` exits 0 across 11,614 lines in 682.05 seconds with one overall success marker and one optimization marker. ## 2026-07-18 v0.5 Model-Pull Audit Recovery Integrity

- Implemented: v2 schema verification now pins the exact event-to-operation `PRAGMA foreign_key_list` definition and `ON DELETE RESTRICT`. `SQLiteRuntimeModelPullApprovalStore.recoverUnfinished(at:)` calls `validateAllRecordHistories` inside its `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction before selecting unfinished approvals. The validator runs both `PRAGMA foreign_key_check` and an independent orphan anti-join, streams one ordered `LEFT JOIN`, and retains only the current operation plus at most three events rather than loading all IDs and issuing N+1 full-history reads.
- Capacity and fail-closed result: the store admits at most 10,000 audit operations. Exact-limit recovery is allowed, while a larger store or additional intake fails with `capacityExceeded`. Corruption aborts recovery before any transition; broker initialization records the failure, and subsequent intake returns `storageUnavailable` with no pending review and zero provider dispatches.
- Regressions: the store tests cover completed timestamp corruption, orphan events, removal of the otherwise exact required FK, 10,000-operation streaming/intake capacity, and `testRecoveryRejectsAuditHistoryAboveMaximumOperationCount` for explicit 10,001-operation recovery rejection. The broker tests cover all three corruption classes, preserve any unexpectedly accepted operation id, attempt approval, and require the dispatcher to remain at zero. Copy hygiene structurally pins the exact single FK tuple, both exact capacity comparisons, and each broker `enqueue` to `approve` to zero-dispatch proof path.
- Current verification: the exact eight-test focused command and all 43 `RuntimeModelPullApproval` tests pass with zero failures; `testRecoveryStreamsMaximumAuditHistoryAndBlocksAdditionalIntake` completes exact-limit recovery in 0.265 seconds on this host. The adjacent permission/coordinator selection passes 29 tests, copy hygiene passes across 91 files, docs hygiene passes across 11 documents, and the 57-file P2P security-design preflight confirms that compiler, socket, runtime-network, Phase B, and production gates remain closed. Final GPT-5.6 Sol re-review reports `no P0-P3 findings`.
- Final aggregate: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-model-pull-audit-recovery-integrity-final-20260718.log` exits 0 across 12,192 lines with exactly one overall success marker, one model-pull audit-recovery addendum, 43/43 model-pull tests, 8/8 exact focused regressions, 88 existing local development-relay matches, and 905 encrypted frame bodies. Post-gate `adb devices -l` lists no attached device.
- Scope: no schema, protocol, capability, permission, UI, provider-routing, source-acquisition, socket, or network authority changed. P2P candidate closure and all Phase B/production restrictions remain in force.
- Evidence boundary: this is no-device macOS Swift persistence and approval-state verification. The timing is a local regression ceiling, not a capacity claim. This is not physical Android, optical QR, live-provider download, external-network, P2P/NAT, Phase B, deployment, or production-readiness proof.

## 2026-07-17 v0.2 Android Chat Sessions Bulk Terminal Authority

- Bound runtime-authoritative archive-all and delete-all success results to the exact pending request id, lifecycle type, receiving channel object, connection generation, and authenticated authority generation before any bulk counter, persisted session state, progress state, or reconciliation request can change.
- Bound malformed and ordinary bulk errors to the same request/channel/connection/authentication authority. A foreign or stale `authentication_required` terminal can no longer revoke the current session, and stale errors cannot consume or fail the current bulk operation.
- Made the asynchronous send-failure branch use the same authority matcher against the captured dispatch channel and generation before publishing failure or starting reconciliation.
- Added the `chat-sessions-bulk-` request namespace so prior-batch and completed-operation errors are discarded before generic error handling instead of publishing a stale UI error.
- Added `chatSessionsBulkLifecycleRequiresExactTerminalAuthority`, `chatSessionsBulkMalformedCurrentErrorConsumesOnlyExactAuthority`, and `chatSessionsBulkSendFailureRequiresExactDispatchAuthority`. They cover foreign-channel, previous-connection, previous-authentication, prior-batch, malformed-current, exact send-failure, and delayed stale send-failure terminals before confirming exact-current completion.
- All three focused regressions pass, all 597 `RuntimeClientViewModelTest` tests pass, and all 1,071 Android app JVM tests pass with zero failures, errors, or skips under Android Studio JBR.
- Added all three tests to the default no-device selector and updated `check_android_chat_sessions_bulk_terminal_authority_junit` to require one executed testcase for every canonical name with no skip, failure, or error. Copy hygiene pins all terminal paths, the request namespace, executable tests and helper, selectors, exact JUnit tuple, marker, protocol text, and roadmap/progress/QA alignment.
- Final aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-chat-sessions-bulk-terminal-authority-final-reviewed-20260717.log` exits 0 across 12,089 lines with one overall success marker, one dedicated bulk terminal-authority marker, one dynamic JUnit proof marker, two zero-delay registry self-tests, 88 local development-relay match lines, freshness across 56 authenticated relay connections, and 905 encrypted frame bodies.
- This changes no wire schema, capability, source acquisition, permission, client execution, socket/network authority, controlled-spike Phase B, production networking, or deployment authority. All four controlled-spike approvals remain bounded Phase A.

## 2026-07-17 v0.2 Android Authenticated Read Rollover And Session-List Receive Authority

- Bound `chat.sessions.list` result handling to the exact request id, receiving channel object, connection generation, and authenticated authority generation. The check now runs before page accounting, metadata validation, accumulation, history publication, search state, bulk-lifecycle state, or any continuation dispatch.
- Wrong-channel, old-connection, prior-authentication, duplicate, and superseded results are inert. A stale page cannot increment `pageCount`, append summaries or session ids, change cursors or snapshot count, trigger malformed-terminal handling, advance pagination, or report a terminal failure against the current request.
- Reused the same receive-authority matcher for malformed and ordinary protocol errors, request send failures, and stale-history-error classification. Exact-current terminals keep their existing single-use behavior, while delayed terminals cannot consume a replacement request or revoke a newer authenticated session.
- Successful same-channel reauthentication now clears pending `memory.list` and `research.notebooks.list` authority. Memory-list request ids use the `memory-list-` namespace, so delayed old results and errors are rejected before generic error handling; this also cancels the old research timeout and permits immediate usable replacements.
- Authentication revocation explicitly clears pending `memory.list` authority and continues to clear `research.notebooks.list` through research-session cleanup. A sibling `authentication_required` error therefore cannot leave future memory refreshes blocked behind an unreachable pending id.
- Added `chatSessionsListRequiresExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumesOnce`, `chatSessionsListWrongSourceCannotAdvancePaginationOrTriggerTerminalFailure`, `sameChannelReauthenticationReplacesPendingMemoryAndResearchListAuthority`, and `siblingAuthenticationErrorClearsConcurrentPendingMemoryListAuthority`. The focused JUnit XML reports four tests with zero skips, failures, or errors.
- All 594 `RuntimeClientViewModelTest` tests and all 1,068 Android app JVM tests pass under Android Studio JBR. The default selector now includes the four tests and a dedicated dynamic JUnit check; copy hygiene pins the implementation, executable tests, selectors, exact JUnit tuple, marker, and documentation.
- The first GPT-5.6 Sol review found one P2 stale `memory.list` error fallthrough and one P3 global-count copy-guard weakness. A targeted re-review found one additional P2 exact malformed-error correlation leak and one P3 ordering-guard gap. Namespaced exact-current handling, clear-before-publication, replacement-and-late-error coverage, and handler-specific structural checks remediate all four findings; the final GPT-5.6 Sol re-review reports no P0-P3 findings.
- The refreshed default no-device aggregate at `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-authenticated-read-rollover-authority-final-reviewed-20260717.log` exits 0 across 12,083 lines with one overall success marker, one dedicated rollover marker, one dynamic JUnit proof marker, two zero-delay registry self-tests, 88 local development-relay match lines, freshness across 56 authenticated relay connections, and 905 encrypted frame bodies.
- This changes no wire schema, capability, provider route, source acquisition, permission, client execution, socket/network authority, controlled-spike Phase B, production networking, or deployment authority. All four controlled-spike approvals remain bounded Phase A.

## 2026-07-17 v0.2 Android Authenticated Transcript And Document Read Authority

- Replaced request-id-only Android correlation for `chat.messages.list`, `index.documents.list`, and `retrieval.query` with pending records bound to the exact request id, channel object, connection generation, and authenticated authority generation.
- Transcript results now pass the full current-authority check before pending correlation is consumed, messages are published, or device history is persisted. A wrong-channel, old-connection, prior-authentication, duplicate, or superseded result is inert, while an exact malformed result closes only its own request.
- Document catalog and search results now use the same authority contract before transient catalog/search state changes. The semantic-search fallback is retained, but only an exact-current `retrieval.query` error can start one lexical retry from the payload captured by that pending request.
- Request-specific errors and send failures use the same single-use authority matcher. A delayed old document failure cannot close its replacement, and stale transcript/document errors cannot publish an error, revoke the current session, trigger fallback, mutate transient state, or persist transcript data.
- Successful same-channel reauthentication tombstones prior transcript authority and clears document authority. Disconnect, authenticated-session revocation, receive-failure closure, and ViewModel clear also remove pending authority and transient document state.
- Ten focused regressions now cover exact-current catalog/search consumption, exact-current catalog error closure, immediate catalog send-failure retry and late-frame isolation, delayed replaced document and transcript send failures, document and transcript disconnect/revocation/ViewModel cleanup, exact-current transcript publication, and same-channel reauthentication. - The default no-device gate selects all ten tests, parses the generated JUnit XML, and requires one executed testcase per exact name with no skip, failure, or error before emitting `Covered v0.2 addendum: Android authenticated transcript and document read current-request authority.` Copy hygiene now requires the implementation, request-bound terminal types, test helpers, ten selectors, dynamic JUnit proof, marker, and aligned roadmap/progress/QA sections.
- The pre-implementation GPT-5.6 Sol audit reports no P0-P3 production finding and confirms the four residual tests can use existing seams. The first evidence review found one P3 raw-name copy-guard false-pass risk, and the first remediation review found one additional P3 because source regex alone could accept ignored or commented tests. The final evidence review found one P3 because copy hygiene did not pin every canonical name into the dynamic JUnit tuple.
