### 2026-07-03 Response-Only Message Direction Rejection No-Device Gate

- Scope: continue runtime protocol hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. `auth.challenge`, `pairing.result`, `models.result`, `chat.delta`, `chat.done`, `chat.title.result`, and `error` are runtime-to-client response frame types, so clients must not be able to send them back into AetherLink Runtime as request frames that look like accepted pairing, auth challenges, model lists, streamed assistant output, title results, or runtime errors.
- Result: Response-Only Message Direction Rejection No-Device Gate: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter` now returns `unexpected_message_direction` for client-sent response-only protocol types instead of letting known reverse-direction frames fall through the generic unknown-message path.
- Result: `testResponseOnlyMessageTypesReturnDirectionProtocolError` proves `auth.challenge`, `pairing.result`, `models.result`, `chat.delta`, `chat.done`, `chat.title.result`, and `error` return non-retryable `unexpected_message_direction` with the original request id.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` now sends `smoke-response-only-auth-challenge`, `smoke-response-only-pairing-result`, `smoke-response-only-models-result`, `smoke-response-only-chat-delta`, `smoke-response-only-chat-done`, `smoke-response-only-chat-title-result`, and `smoke-response-only-error` over an authenticated RuntimeDevServer relay session and requires `unexpected_message_direction`.
- Result: RuntimeDevServer authenticated relay smoke rejects `auth.challenge`, `pairing.result`, `models.result`, `chat.delta`, `chat.done`, `chat.title.result`, and `error` when a client sends response-only protocol frames back to the runtime.
- Result: `packages/protocol-schema/protocol.schema.json`, `script/check_protocol_schema.py`, and `docs/protocol.md` now include `unexpected_message_direction` as a named protocol error code for known message types sent in the wrong direction.
- Boundary: this does not implement client-supplied pairing results, client-supplied auth challenges, client-supplied model result overrides, client-driven assistant stream frames, client-authored title results, or client-authored runtime errors. It keeps those frame families runtime-owned outputs only.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the router direction-error path, focused Swift regression, RuntimeDevServer smoke markers, protocol error code, no-device summary phrase, roadmap, progress, and QA evidence.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Agent state: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. A GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed the response-only protocol types were not previously pinned by a named no-device gate.
- Caveat: this is no-device source/unit/schema/smoke evidence only. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, optical camera scan reliability, live hardware pairing/auth from a phone, production relay deployment, production P2P/rendezvous traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-07-03 Pre-Auth Unknown Metadata Rejection No-Device Gate

- Scope: continue runtime pairing/auth boundary hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. `pairing.request`, `hello`, and `auth.response` are active pre-runtime-command messages, so mobile payloads must not silently carry response-only pairing/auth fields, forged runtime identity/proof fields, backend URLs, backend credentials, provider URLs, route tokens, relay secrets, requested route tokens, workspace IDs, permission grants, source paths, source-control state, or direct-provider metadata before trust, challenge, or authentication mutation.
- Result: Pre-Auth Unknown Metadata Rejection No-Device Gate: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter` now enforces strict request payload key allowlists for `pairing.request`, `hello`, and `auth.response` before trusted-device writes, auth challenge creation, or authenticated-session mutation.
- Result: `testPairingRequestRejectsUnknownPayloadMetadataBeforeTrusting`, `testHelloRejectsUnknownPayloadMetadataBeforeChallengeCreation`, and `testAuthResponseRejectsUnknownPayloadMetadataBeforeAuthentication` prove malformed payloads return `invalid_payload`, leave trusted-device storage empty or the connection unauthenticated, and still allow later valid same-session pairing/auth flows.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` now sends `smoke-pair-unknown-metadata`, `smoke-hello-unknown-metadata`, and `smoke-auth-unknown-metadata` over the RuntimeDevServer relay path, requires `invalid_payload`, and proves follow-up runtime commands still require authentication.
- Result: `script/check_protocol_schema.py` now verifies the closed request schemas for `pairingRequestPayload`, `helloPayload`, and the request branch of `authResponsePayload`.
- Boundary: this does not add client-supplied runtime identity updates, client-chosen auth nonces, client-supplied runtime proofs, backend/provider routing, permission grants, workspace-scoped pairing, or direct provider access. It keeps pairing and auth as runtime-owned trust and challenge-response operations.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the runtime allowlists, focused Swift regressions, RuntimeDevServer smoke markers, closed schema, protocol docs, no-device gate entry, roadmap, progress, and QA evidence.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Agent state: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. A GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed `pairing.request`, `hello`, and `auth.response` as active pre-auth payload-boundary gaps.
- Caveat: this is no-device source/unit/schema/smoke evidence only. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, optical camera scan reliability, live hardware pairing/auth from a phone, production relay deployment, production P2P/rendezvous traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-07-03 Android Route-Token Remote Material Isolation No-Device Gate

- Scope: continue private-overlay route hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. `routeToken` is paired runtime identity/routing metadata; it must not become P2P session/rendezvous material or a raw relay route id at the generic Android core transport seam.
- Result: `RuntimeConnectionManager` now rejects prepared remote routes that reuse the paired runtime `routeToken` as P2P `sessionId`, P2P rendezvous token, relay id, or relay rendezvous token before any P2P or relay connector is called.
- Result: `RuntimePeerToPeerRoutePreparation` rejects a paired `routeToken` as `recordId`, and `RuntimeRelayRoutePreparation` rejects a paired `routeToken` as `relayId`, keeping standard route preparation on explicit route material.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeConnectionManagerTest.remoteRoutePreparerRejectsRoutesThatReusePairingRouteTokenMaterial`, `RuntimePeerToPeerRoutePreparationTest.peerToPeerRoutePreparationRejectsPairedRouteTokenAsRecordId`, and `RuntimeRelayRoutePreparationTest.relayRoutePreparationRejectsPairedRouteTokenAsRelayId` are covered by the no-device gate, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` requires the manager/preparer guards, focused tests, no-device summary, current QA evidence, and roadmap coverage.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Agent state: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. A GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed the active app planner already uses explicit relay/P2P route material and identified the stale core transport test/gate contract.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android core transport/source/script/docs evidence. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, optical camera reliability, live phone pairing, phone relay reachability, live provider-backed chat/cancel, production relay deployment, production P2P traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

- Scope: continue QR-only route-refresh hardening after the Android phone was disconnected. Relay scope belongs to relay route material, but a P2P-only QR path could carry stray `relay_scope=remote` without a complete relay route.

- Result: `trustedRuntimeFromRouteRefreshQr` now fails closed for P2P route-refresh QR payloads that carry `relayScope` without a valid relay route, preventing a hand-built or malformed latest-QR payload from mixing route families before trusted route storage changes.

### 2026-07-03 Relay Allocation Base64 Requested-Secret No-Device Gate

- Scope: close the aggregate no-device QR emit failure found after the relay control-line work. The no-ADB QR smoke timed out because RuntimeDevServer sent a base64 requested relay secret containing `=` padding, and `RelayAllocationRequest.parse` treated any `=` in the secret slot as unknown metadata.
- Result: `RelayAllocationRequest.parse` now rejects only explicit request metadata keys such as `backend_url`, `provider_url`, `requested_route_token`, and `relay_secret_debug` before treating a value as requested relay secret material.
- Result: `RelayAllocationTests/testParsesAllocationRequestWithBase64RequestedRelaySecret` proves allocation requests accept base64-style requested relay secrets with `+`, `/`, and `=` padding while `RelayAllocationTests/testRejectsUnexpectedAllocationRequestMetadata` continues rejecting backend/provider/request metadata fields.
- Result: a no-ADB local relay QR emit smoke passed after the parser fix, generating and round-trip verifying `pairing-uri.txt` and `pairing-qr.png`.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the focused Swift regression and reports `relay allocation base64 requested-secret addendum`.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves SwiftPM/no-device allocation parsing and local QR artifact generation only. It does not prove physical Android pairing, optical QR scanning, production relay deployment, live provider-backed chat/cancel, production P2P traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter 'RelayAllocationTests/testParsesAllocationRequestWithBase64RequestedRelaySecret|RelayAllocationTests/testRejectsUnexpectedAllocationRequestMetadata|RelayAllocationTests/testRejectsBlankAllocationTokenAndRelaySecret|RelayHandshakeTests/testRejectsNonCanonicalRelayID|RelayProbeTests/testRejectsNonCanonicalRelayID'`
- `./script/no_adb_external_relay_pairing_smoke.sh --relay-host 127.0.0.1 --relay-port <free-port> --start-local-relay --emit-only --timeout 30 --work-dir <temp-dir>`

### 2026-07-02 Physical Android Live Ollama Chat Cancel Close

- Coverage label: Physical Android Live Ollama Chat Cancel Smoke.
- Coverage label: macOS chat.cancel immediate `chat.done(cancelled)` close.
- Scope: close the live-provider gap found on the connected `R3CXC0M76VM` / `SM_S936N` phone. The first live run paired successfully and reached `chat.send`, `chat.delta`, and `chat.cancel`, but failed because the runtime did not emit the target `chat.done` quickly after acknowledging cancellation.
- Result: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter` now sends `chat.done` with `finish_reason = "cancelled"` for the original `chat.send` request as soon as `chat.cancel` successfully cancels the backend generation. It also suppresses later provider stream events for requests already marked cancelled, while keeping the stored runtime-owned cancelled event de-duplicated.
- Result: the physical live smoke passed with Ollama available on `127.0.0.1:11434`, selected model `gemma4:e4b-mlx`, and LM Studio unavailable on `127.0.0.1:1234`.
- Result: runtime log evidence shows `chat.send`, streamed `chat.delta`, `chat.cancel`, the cancel acknowledgement, and `chat.done` for the original request id after cancellation.
- Device state: this is not no-device evidence; it used the connected physical Android phone `R3CXC0M76VM` / `SM_S936N`.
- Evidence: passing work directory `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.9DOg4v`; chat/cancel screenshot `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.9DOg4v/aetherlink-chat-cancel-smoke.png`; final screenshot `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.9DOg4v/aetherlink-pairing-smoke.png`.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testChatCancelAcknowledgementPersistsRuntimeOwnedCancelledEvent` now asserts that a successful cancel produces one `chat.done(cancelled)` for the target request and does not duplicate terminal messages or stored cancelled events.
- Caveat: this is physical Android proof through ADB-injected QR deeplink delivery, USB `adb reverse` local relay reachability, and a live Ollama-backed RuntimeDevServer path. It does not prove optical camera QR scanning, external public relay reachability, LM Studio-backed live chat/cancel, production P2P traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter 'LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testChatCancelAcknowledgementPersistsRuntimeOwnedCancelledEvent|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testChatCancelReturnsCancelAcknowledgement|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testChatCancelUnknownGenerationReturnsProtocolError'`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --relay --serial R3CXC0M76VM --live-backend --expect-chat-cancel --chat-delta-timeout 180 --chat-text AetherLink_live_ollama_cancel_fix_smoke`
- `python3 -m json.tool` against `http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags` confirmed Ollama model availability; `curl --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1/models` confirmed LM Studio was unavailable for this pass.
- `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l`

### 2026-07-02 Physical Android Relay Reconnect Race And Locale-Resilient Chat/Cancel Smoke

- Coverage label: Physical Android Relay Reconnect Race Gate.
- Coverage label: Physical Android Locale-Resilient Chat Cancel Smoke.
- Scope: fix the latest physical `--expect-chat-cancel --expect-reconnect` smoke on the connected `R3CXC0M76VM` / `SM_S936N` Korean-locale phone after QR deeplink pairing could reach chat but the smoke and saved-route relaunch path exposed two hardware-only gaps.
- Result: `script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh` now resolves localized Android `strings.xml` content descriptions before tapping chat controls, so the physical smoke can tap Korean `메시지`, `메시지 전송`, and `생성 취소` instead of hard-coding English `Message`, `Send message`, and `Cancel generation`. The chat input helper also falls back to the enabled `android.widget.EditText` when content descriptions are merged differently by the UI tree.
- Result: Android relay preflight now separates route-id reachability from runtime-waiting readiness. `isRelayProbeKnown()` lets saved trusted relay reconnect continue when the relay reports `known=true runtime_waiting=false` during the relaunch race, then the real relay client waits until the runtime host reconnects and the relay bridges the pair.
- Result: the physical smoke passed end to end after the fix: QR deeplink injection, accepted pairing, initial `runtime.health`, `models.list`, Korean-locale chat send/cancel (`chat.send`, `chat.delta`, `chat.cancel`, `chat.done`), force-stop/relaunch, second `runtime.health`, and second `models.list`.
- Device state: this is not no-device evidence; it used the connected physical Android phone `R3CXC0M76VM` / `SM_S936N`.
- Evidence: passing work directory `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.gLGY6T`; chat/cancel screenshot `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.gLGY6T/aetherlink-chat-cancel-smoke.png`; final reconnect screenshot `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.gLGY6T/aetherlink-pairing-smoke.png`.
- Evidence: the passing relay log includes the relaunch race shape: `probed ... known=true runtime_waiting=false`, then `accepted role=client`, `waiting ... role=client`, `accepted role=runtime`, and `matched ... runtime<->client`.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeKnownParserAllowsRuntimeReconnectRace` pins the `known=true runtime_waiting=false` reconnect-preflight path while keeping `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeResponseParserRequiresKnownRouteAndWaitingRuntime` for route-level readiness probes. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` require both regressions plus the locale-resilient physical smoke helpers.
- Caveat: this is physical Android proof through ADB-injected QR deeplink delivery, USB `adb reverse` local relay reachability, and the RuntimeDevServer mock backend. It does not prove optical camera QR scanning, production external relay reachability, live Ollama/LM Studio model quality, physical file-picker behavior, production P2P traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeResponseParserRequiresKnownRouteAndWaitingRuntime --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeKnownParserAllowsRuntimeReconnectRace --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayQrPairingFailsBeforeConnectWhenDeviceCannotReachRelayRoute -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --relay --serial R3CXC0M76VM --expect-reconnect --expect-chat-cancel --chat-text AetherLink_physical_smoke`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py script/check_protocol_schema.py`
- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`
- `bash -n script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh script/check_no_device_quality.sh`
- `git diff --check -- apps/android/app/src/main/java/com/localagentbridge/android/runtime/RuntimeClientViewModel.kt apps/android/app/src/test/java/com/localagentbridge/android/runtime/RuntimeClientViewModelTest.kt script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_no_device_quality.sh docs/progress.md docs/qa-evidence.md docs/roadmap.md`
- `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l`

### 2026-07-02 RuntimeDevServer Future Python Namespace Rejection No-Device Gate

- Coverage label: Protocol Reserved Python Namespace No-Device Gate.
- Coverage label: RuntimeDevServer Future Python Namespace Rejection No-Device Gate.
- Scope: continue Queue 6 future-runtime-boundary coverage by reserving Python execution messages before any runtime Python sandbox, permission prompt, resource-limit, or audit-log design exists.
- Result: `script/check_protocol_schema.py` now treats the `python.` prefix as a reserved future namespace alongside skills, MCP, web search, projects, and automation. Its self-check rejects synthetic `python.run` and `python.exec` names through the same helper used for active message enum hygiene.
- Result: RuntimeDevServer authenticated relay smoke rejects `python.run` and `python.exec` with `unknown_message_type` through `smoke-future-python-run` and `smoke-future-python-exec` after pairing/auth.
- Result: the relay ciphertext boundary marker list now includes `python.run`, `python.exec`, both Python request ids, and representative future Python payload text so unsupported Python request details must stay inside encrypted relay frame bodies.
- Result: `docs/protocol.md` now names `python.run` and `python.exec` only as candidate future messages and keeps `python.*` reserved until runtime-owned sandboxing, permissions, resource limits, and audit semantics are designed.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the protocol reserved prefix, RuntimeDevServer Python namespace rejection smoke, no-device summary, protocol docs, roadmap inventory, and current evidence. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports the protocol reserved tools/search/Python namespace guard and RuntimeDevServer future Python namespace rejection addendum.
- Device state: ADB currently recognizes `R3CXC0M76VM` / `SM_S936N`, but this slice is still no-device protocol/runtime smoke evidence and does not exercise the physical phone.
- Caveat: this proves no-device protocol schema and RuntimeDevServer authenticated relay fail-closed behavior for future Python message names only. It does not implement Python execution, sandboxing, approval UI, audit logs, physical Android QR scanning, live provider-backed chat/cancel, production relay deployment, production P2P traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-07-02 Protocol Reserved Skills/MCP/Web Search Namespace No-Device Gate

- Scope: continue roadmap v0.5-v0.7 boundary work while no Android phone is attached. Skills, MCP, and web search remain future runtime-host capabilities, not active client/runtime protocol commands.
- Result: `script/check_protocol_schema.py` now treats `skills.`, `mcp.`, `web_search.`, `projects.`, and `automation.` as required reserved future prefixes and includes a named self-check that synthetic active message names under all five prefixes are rejected by the same helper used for the active enum.
- Result: RuntimeDevServer authenticated relay smoke rejects `skills.run`, `mcp.tool.call`, and `web_search.query` with `unknown_message_type`, proving those future namespaces fail closed over an authenticated relay session instead of silently executing or reaching a backend/tool path.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now pins the schema self-check, the authenticated RuntimeDevServer future-namespace smoke, protocol docs, roadmap coverage, current QA evidence, and no-device summary phrase. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports `protocol reserved tools/search namespace guard addendum`.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves static protocol reservation and no-device RuntimeDevServer fail-closed behavior only. It does not implement skills, MCP, web search, runtime permission broker UI, production tool audit logs, physical Android QR scanning, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

- Caveat: this proves no-device macOS runtime boundary behavior only. It does not implement live provider-backed semantic ranking, production embedding indexing, physical Android QR scanning, live chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

- Scope: continue QR-only trust-boundary smoke hardening while no Android phone is attached. Android already has accepted-pairing runtime identity mismatch rejection coverage; this pass adds the positive RuntimeDevServer relay smoke check that accepted pairing.result confirms the QR-pinned runtime identity.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift` now requires accepted relay `pairing.result` to echo the QR-pinned `runtime_device_id`, `runtime_public_key`, and `runtime_key_fingerprint` before the smoke treats the device as trusted.
- Result: the accepted pairing verifier also recomputes the fingerprint of `runtime_public_key`, so a pairing result cannot satisfy the smoke with a mismatched public key/fingerprint pair.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports `RuntimeDevServer accepted pairing runtime identity confirmation addendum`; `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` requires the accepted-pairing verifier helper, runtime identity field checks, current docs evidence, and the default no-device summary phrase.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves no-device RuntimeDevServer development-relay accepted-pairing identity confirmation only. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, client-side hardware persistence, live phone auth, production relay/P2P traversal, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py`
- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

### 2026-07-02 RuntimeDevServer Relay Trusted-Hello Runtime Proof Smoke

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 6 trust-boundary smoke hardening while no Android phone is attached. The focused router regression already proved trusted `hello` can include a runtime proof when a signer is available; this pass promotes that contract into the full RuntimeDevServer relay smoke.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift` now starts RuntimeDevServer with a per-run `AETHERLINK_DEV_RUNTIME_IDENTITY_FILE` instead of fake `AETHERLINK_DEV_RUNTIME_PUBLIC_KEY` / `AETHERLINK_DEV_RUNTIME_KEY_FINGERPRINT` values, so the emitted QR `runtime_public_key` and `runtime_key_fingerprint` come from the same file-backed private key that signs `auth.challenge`.
- Result: the smoke now requires the development pairing URI and `AETHERLINK_DEV_PAIRING_INFO` to agree on `runtime_public_key` and `runtime_key_fingerprint`, verifies the fingerprint of the QR-pinned runtime_public_key, and verifies every trusted relay `auth.challenge` runtime_signature against the nonce-bound message `AetherLink runtime auth challenge v1`.
- Result: the same verifier rejects replay of the captured `runtime_signature` against a different nonce before the client sends `auth.response`.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports `RuntimeDevServer relay trusted hello runtime proof addendum`; `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires the runtime identity file env, runtime proof verifier helpers, runtime_signature/runtime_key_fingerprint checks, current progress/QA evidence, and the default no-device summary phrase.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves no-device RuntimeDevServer development-relay trusted-hello proof emission and smoke-side verification only. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, client-side runtime proof verification on hardware, live phone auth, production relay/P2P traversal, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh`

- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`

### 2026-07-02 macOS RelayPeerClient Registration Ready Timeout No-Device Gate

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 5/6 relay/private-overlay hardening while no Android phone is attached. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that Android relay TCP ready-timeout coverage existed, but the macOS runtime-host relay client could still wait indefinitely after TCP connect if the relay never sent `AETHERLINK_RELAY registered` or `AETHERLINK_RELAY ready`.
- Result: `RelayPeerConfiguration` now carries a bounded `controlLineTimeout` while preserving the previous public initializer shape for existing call sites; the default is 45 seconds so physical QR cold starts have enough time to reach relay ready.
- Result: `RelayPeerClient` fails with explicit `.failed(...)` statuses if relay registration does not arrive before the control-line timeout or if the relay registers the runtime but never sends the ready line.
- Result: `RelayPeerClientTests.testRelayPeerConfigurationDefaultControlLineTimeoutAllowsPhysicalQrStartup`, `RelayPeerClientTests.testRelayPeerClientTimesOutWhenRegistrationLineNeverArrives`, and `RelayPeerClientTests.testRelayPeerClientTimesOutWhenReadyLineNeverArrivesAfterRegistration` prove the default is physical-QR tolerant while TCP connect alone is not treated as relay-ready state.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` runs the focused RelayPeerClient timeout regressions and reports the physical-QR-tolerant 45s control-line timeout addendum; `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` requires the implementation, focused tests, no-device summary, current QA/progress evidence, and roadmap coverage.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves macOS SwiftPM/no-device relay control-line timeout behavior only. It does not prove physical Android pairing, optical QR scanning, production relay/P2P traversal, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter 'RelayPeerClientTests/testRelayPeerConfigurationDefaultControlLineTimeoutAllowsPhysicalQrStartup|RelayPeerClientTests/testRelayPeerClientTimesOutWhenRegistrationLineNeverArrives|RelayPeerClientTests/testRelayPeerClientTimesOutWhenReadyLineNeverArrivesAfterRegistration'`
- `swift test --filter RelayPeerClientTests`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 4/5/6 while no Android phone is attached. - Result: `RuntimePairingPayloadParserTest.allowsPrivateOverlayRelayHostsOnlyWithExplicitScope` proves the same relay host family remains accepted when `relay_scope=private_overlay` is present.
- Result: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.privateOverlayRelayQrParseFailureReportsScopeRequired` maps that parser failure to `pairing_relay_route_rejected` and `route_diagnostic_private_overlay_scope_required` so Settings/Chat route recovery asks for a fresh reachable QR.
- Result: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` continues to run the focused ViewModel mapper regression and now reports `Android private-overlay QR missing-scope diagnostic addendum`.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires the parser focused regressions, exact parser error, diagnostic-code mapping, localized UI diagnostic resources, default no-device gate entry, exact summary phrase, current progress/QA evidence, and roadmap coverage.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves no-device parser/ViewModel/UI diagnostic wiring only. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, production relay/P2P reachability, live provider-backed chat/cancel, phone reachability, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-07-02 macOS QR-Only Clean First-Run Pairing No-Device Gate

> Superseded on 2026-07-18: hiding Connection Recovery here created an unrecoverable first-run state when no automatic route source was configured. The current contract is recorded in `macOS Pairing QR Recovery and Rendered Payload No-Device Gate`.

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 4 while no Android phone is attached. Physical recovery flows still need device review, but the macOS clean first-run path can be revalidated so normal users are not shown Connection Recovery setup before QR pairing or a real route issue exists.
- Result: `shouldShowPairingRouteSetupPanel(model:)` still reuses `shouldShowRouteDiagnosticsPanel(model:)`, so clean first-run Pairing hides Connection Recovery, while saved route state or concrete route-preparation issues still expose the panel.
- Result: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests/testRouteDiagnosticsPanelStaysHiddenOnCleanFirstRunAndPairingHidesSetup` proves clean first-run Status and Pairing both hide the recovery panel, and saved-route / route-issue states still show it.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires the shared visibility helper, rejects the older pairing-only fallback that exposed setup merely because automatic route preparation was unavailable, requires the focused SwiftPM regression, default no-device summary phrase, current progress/QA evidence, and the roadmap QR-only pairing item.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves macOS SwiftPM/source policy only. It does not prove physical Android recovery flows, optical QR scanning, live provider-backed chat/cancel, production relay/P2P traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-07-02 Android Opaque Route Material Size Bound No-Device Gate

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 5/6 QR/private-overlay hardening while no Android phone is attached. A GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that opaque P2P route fields rejected blanks and whitespace but did not have a size ceiling before QR parsing, trusted restore, or P2P route preparation.

- Result: `PairingStore` now applies the larger bounded body ceiling when deciding whether restored trusted P2P rendezvous material is complete, so oversized stored P2P material is removed instead of becoming a reconnect candidate.
- Result: `RuntimePeerToPeerRoutePreparation` now rejects oversized P2P record ids, encrypted candidate bodies, and anti-replay nonces before creating `PreparedRemoteRuntimeRoute.PeerToPeer`.
- Result: the default no-device summary now reports `Android opaque route material size-bound addendum`; the related parser, PairingStore, and P2P route-preparation test classes are already part of the default no-device gate.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires the size-bound constants, parser/store/transport regressions, default no-device summary, and current docs/roadmap coverage.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this proves no-device Android QR/parser/storage/route-preparation boundaries only. It does not prove physical Android QR scan, optical camera reliability, production P2P signaling, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-07-02 RuntimeDevServer Memory-Summary Draft And Route-Refresh Freshness Smoke

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 6, Queue 5 relay freshness hardening, and v0.2 memory polish while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; GPT-5.5 read-only explorer work identified RuntimeDevServer smoke coverage gaps around current memory-summary draft decisions and authenticated `route.refresh` lease freshness.
- Result: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter` now accepts an injected long-inactivity memory-summary policy, and RuntimeDevServer can take dev-only `AETHERLINK_DEV_RUNTIME_MEMORY_JSONL_FILE`, `AETHERLINK_DEV_MEMORY_SUMMARY_MIN_INACTIVE_SECONDS`, and `AETHERLINK_DEV_MEMORY_SUMMARY_MIN_MESSAGES` settings. The smoke uses these to keep runtime memory isolated from the user default store and make a fresh smoke chat eligible for deterministic draft review.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` now validates `memory.summary.drafts.list`, `memory.summary.draft.approve`, `memory.summary.draft.dismiss`, approved memory-summary memory.list visibility, approved-draft hiding, dismissed draft hiding, dismissed draft no memory.list entry, and the existing unavailable-draft errors over RuntimeDevServer.
- Result: the same authenticated relay smoke now compares the initial QR relay lease with the authenticated `route.refresh` response. It fails if `route.refresh` reuses the QR relay nonce or does not advance `relay_expires_at`, while allowing stable relay id/secret reuse.
- Result: RuntimeDevServer now retires the superseded relay client after a successful refreshed allocation so the current relay session can finish, but the old relay nonce cannot reconnect later and race the refreshed route.
- Result: `RelayPeerClientTests.testRelayPeerClientRetireKeepsCurrentConnectionAndSuppressesReconnect` now pins that `retireAfterCurrentConnection()` leaves the active relay session usable for in-flight protocol frames while preventing a second stale-nonce relay handshake after the current socket closes.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the RuntimeDevServer memory store/policy env seam, smoke request ids `smoke-memory-summary-approve`, `smoke-memory-summary-after-approve`, `smoke-memory-summary-memory-list`, `smoke-memory-summary-dismiss`, `smoke-memory-summary-after-dismiss`, and `smoke-memory-summary-dismiss-memory-list`, plus the route.refresh lease freshness assertions and default no-device gate coverage phrases.
- Device state: the Android phone remains disconnected, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this is no-device RuntimeDevServer protocol evidence only. It does not prove physical Android QR scan, camera reliability, hardware accessibility traversal, live provider-generated memory summaries, physical relay reachability, production relay allocation, or real different-network connectivity.

- `swift build --package-path apps/macos --product RuntimeDevServer`
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter RelayPeerClientTests`

### 2026-07-02 macOS Remote QR Lease Monotonicity Guard

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 5 and Queue 6 by hardening the runtime-host side of temporary remote-route bootstrap while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified Companion-side allocator result acceptance as the next no-device-verifiable route hardening gap.
- Result: `CompanionAppModel` now treats same-relay bootstrap lease replacement as monotonic. A saved relay lease for a stable relay id is replaced only when the new lease advances `relay_expires_at` and changes `relay_nonce`.
- Result: fresh saved remote QR leases are not renewed unnecessarily before QR generation, so repeat QR generation can keep using current route material instead of creating false non-advancing lease errors.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionAppModelAcceptsAdvancingSavedBootstrapLeaseForStableRelayID` pins the accepted renewal path, and `testCompanionAppModelRejectsNonAdvancingSavedBootstrapLeaseForStableRelayID` pins rejection of a same-relay lease that reuses the current nonce. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs both tests, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the source/test/script/docs contract.
- Device state: the Android phone remains disconnected, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this hardens runtime-host route material acceptance only. It is not production relay allocation, P2P NAT traversal, production session-key exchange, physical Android QR scan, real phone relay reachability, or live provider-backed chat/cancel proof.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter 'LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelAcceptsAdvancingSavedBootstrapLeaseForStableRelayID|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelRejectsNonAdvancingSavedBootstrapLeaseForStableRelayID'`

- `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh` (syntax only)

### 2026-07-02 AetherLinkRelay Allocation Store Load Resilience Guard

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 5 and Queue 6 by hardening the temporary development relay allocation store load path while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed duplicate persisted `relay_id` tickets could trap relay startup through `Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues:)`.
- Result: persisted allocation store loading now folds tickets safely instead of building a dictionary from potentially duplicated keys. It deduplicates persisted relay tickets and skips malformed ticket entries on load.
- Result: duplicate same-id persisted tickets follow the same advancing-renewal rule as runtime `store(_:)`: only a ticket with a later `relay_expires_at` and fresh `relay_nonce` can replace the current loaded ticket.
- Guardrail: `RelayAllocationTests.testAllocationRegistryLoadsDuplicatePersistedRelayIDsWithAdvancingTicket` pins duplicate load behavior, and `testAllocationRegistrySkipsMalformedPersistedTicketsOnLoad` pins malformed-entry filtering. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs both focused tests, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the source/test/script/docs contract.
- Device state: the Android phone remains disconnected, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this hardens development relay startup resilience only. It is not production relay allocation, account-level abuse control, P2P NAT traversal, production session-key exchange, physical Android QR scan, real phone relay reachability, or live provider-backed chat/cancel proof.

### 2026-07-02 AetherLinkRelay Allocation Renewal Monotonicity Guard

GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified relay allocation renewal monotonicity as the next no-device-verifiable relay hardening gap.

- Result: `AetherLinkRelay` allocations now derive stable opaque `rt1-` relay IDs from route tokens instead of returning the raw route token as the relay room id.
- Result: relay allocation logs now report only allocation state and `relay_id`, not raw route tokens, allocation tokens, or relay frame secrets.
- Guardrail: `RelayAllocationTests.testAllocationDerivesOpaqueStableRelayIDFromRouteTokenAndRequestedSecret` and `testAllocationRegistryPersistsOpaqueRelayIDWithoutRawRouteToken` pin the derivation and persistence boundary. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now verifies persisted allocations keep raw route tokens out of allocation stores and relay logs; `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the source/test/script/docs contract.
- Device state: the Android phone remains disconnected, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this hardens the development relay allocation surface. It is still not a production relay allocation service, keyed rendezvous token system, NAT traversal, production session-key exchange, physical Android QR scan, real phone relay reachability, or live provider-backed chat/cancel proof.

- Result: authorized preflight allocation with the correct token still succeeds without persisting a route, while authorized persisted allocation stores only relay id, expiry, and nonce metadata. The guard verifies unauthorized route tokens, preflight route tokens, relay frame secrets, and allocation tokens are absent from the allocation store.

- Caveat: this protects the development relay allocation endpoint and persisted lease boundary. It does not prove physical Android QR scan, real phone relay reachability, pairing on hardware, production relay allocation, production P2P traversal, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

### 2026-07-01 AetherLinkRelay Exposed Bind Allocation Token Guard

- Scope: continue Immediate Queue 5 and Queue 6 by hardening the temporary development relay while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer inspected the no-device relay scripts and docs.
- Result: `AetherLinkRelay` now defaults to `127.0.0.1` and validates its configuration before binding. Tokenless AetherLinkRelay binds are loopback-only (`127.0.0.1`, `::1`, or `localhost`); wildcard, DNS, private, CGNAT, ULA, and public binds require `--allocation-token` or `AETHERLINK_RELAY_ALLOCATION_TOKEN`.
- Result: `script/run_allocation_relay.sh` now defaults to loopback and fails dry-run/startup for non-loopback binds without an allocation token. The no-ADB QR and different-network runtime helpers keep tokenless `--start-local-relay` on loopback for artifact/preflight diagnostics, but require a token when a non-loopback advertised relay host forces a wildcard bind.
- Guardrail: `RelayAllocationTests` covers loopback tokenless allowance, wildcard/non-loopback token requirement, wildcard/non-loopback token success, and invalid allocation-token rejection. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now behavior-tests tokenless wildcard failure plus wildcard-with-token startup and reports `tokenless AetherLinkRelay binds are loopback-only`.
- Device state: the Android phone remains disconnected, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this hardens the temporary development relay allocation surface. It does not complete production private overlay, NAT traversal, production session-key exchange, real different-network phone reachability, camera QR scan, pairing, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or physical Android reconnect proof.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter RelayAllocationTests`
- `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh script/no_adb_external_relay_pairing_smoke.sh script/run_different_network_dev_runtime.sh script/run_allocation_relay.sh` (syntax only)
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py`
- `script/run_allocation_relay.sh --host 0.0.0.0 --port 43171 --dry-run` returned exit 2 with the expected allocation-token requirement.
- `./script/no_adb_external_relay_pairing_smoke.sh --relay-host 127.0.0.1 --relay-port <free-port> --start-local-relay --emit-only --timeout 30 --work-dir <temp-dir>`
- `script/run_different_network_dev_runtime.sh --relay-host 127.0.0.1 --relay-port <free-port> --start-local-relay --preflight-only --summary-json <temp-file>`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/check_no_device_quality.sh`

### 2026-07-01 RuntimeDevServer Model Residency Unload-Failure Health Smoke

- Scope: continue roadmap v0.2 Runtime Resource Policy smoke coverage while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer recommended RuntimeDevServer unload-failure health coverage as the next no-device-verifiable slice.
- Result: `RuntimeDevServer Authenticated Model Residency Smoke` now runs the aggregate mock backend with `AETHERLINK_DEV_MOCK_AGGREGATE_RESIDENCY` plus `AETHERLINK_DEV_MOCK_UNLOAD_FAILURES`, activates a dev-only `dev-mock-unload-failure` model, switches away from it, and verifies `runtime.health.model_residency.last_unload_failure` exposes only provider, model id, and reason.
- Result: the smoke now proves same-model unload suppression, missing-model rejection without unload, idle unload, and unload-failure runtime.health redaction through RuntimeDevServer in one authenticated relay path.
- Result: `AggregatingLlmBackend` now clears model-residency in-flight state before a client observes the terminal `.done` event, avoiding stale foreground-generation health state while still allowing runtime-owned title generation to appear as legitimate in-flight work.
- Guardrail: the mock unload error intentionally includes backend URL and relay-secret-like text, so `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift` fails if raw provider error strings, backend routes, route tokens, or relay secrets leak through health responses. Android still does not send unload commands or call Ollama or LM Studio directly.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now require the RuntimeDevServer model-residency unload-failure health smoke markers, including `AETHERLINK_DEV_MOCK_UNLOAD_FAILURES`, `last_unload_failure`, and the no-device coverage phrase `unload-failure runtime.health redaction`.
- Device state: the Android phone remains disconnected, so this pass is explicitly no-device.
- Caveat: this is no-device Swift/mock relay evidence. It does not prove live Ollama or LM Studio unload behavior, physical Android rendering, hardware TalkBack/VoiceOver traversal, haptics, physical Android QR scan, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

- Scope: continue roadmap v0.2 Runtime Resource Policy polish while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; this slice surfaces the existing `runtime.health` model-residency snapshot without moving residency policy into the Android client.
- Result: Android Connection Status now shows a localized model-residency status line from `RuntimeUiState.modelResidency`, including active provider/model, in-flight generation count, idle-unload delay, unsupported runtimes, and waiting-for-runtime states.
- Result: macOS Status Quick Actions now include a localized `Refresh Model Residency` action wired to `CompanionAppModel.refreshModelResidencyStatus()` with selected-language accessibility value and hint coverage.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.connectionStatusModelResidencyLineLocalizesAndStaysBoundedAcrossSupportedLanguages`, `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testQuickActionAccessibilityUsesSelectedLanguage`, `script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, `script/check_macos_localization.py`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now require the Android Connection Status model-residency status UI and macOS model-residency refresh quick action.
- Gate: the full no-device quality gate passed after this slice and now reports both `Android Connection Status model-residency status UI` and `macOS model-residency refresh quick action` in the default coverage summary.
- Device state: `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` returned no attached devices, so this pass is explicitly no-device.

### 2026-07-01 Runtime Compaction Backend Source Span

- Scope: continue the roadmap context-window compaction work without physical-device access. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed the smallest safe slice is transient backend-only source-span metadata, not schema, storage, Android model, or durable memory-source changes.
- Result: oversized active `chat.send` histories now annotate the backend-only `Runtime conversation summary:` system message with a deterministic `Source span:` line for the compacted client-visible conversation turn range. Runtime capability guard, runtime-owned memory context, stale client-supplied compaction summaries, archived sessions, and deleted sessions remain outside that source-span calculation.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testChatSendCompactionAnnotatesBackendOnlySourceSpanWithoutPersisting` proves the backend request receives `Source span: client-visible conversation turns 1-6 of 18.`, recent turns remain verbatim, stored request events keep all original client-visible messages, and neither `Runtime conversation summary:` nor `Source span:` is persisted to visible chat history. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` require the regression and coverage phrase.
- Caveat: this is no-device Swift/router evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical QR scanning, live provider-backed summarization quality, production P2P traversal, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that the Connection Status connected-state actions had accessibility coverage but no narrow large-font bounds coverage for the action buttons below the status panel.
- Result: `ConnectionStatusActions` now exposes stable tags for the actions container, `Refresh health`, `Disconnect`, and their visible labels. The visible button labels are capped to two lines with ellipsis so supported-language copy cannot expand the full-width controls outside the compact status surface.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.connectionStatusConnectedActionsStayBoundedAtLargeFontAcrossSupportedLanguages` renders a connected trusted-runtime status screen on a 260 dp, 1.5 font-scale surface across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French. It verifies the action container, both buttons, and both visible labels stay inside bounds and the stacked actions do not overlap while preserving localized click labels.
- Caveat: this is no-device Compose/Robolectric evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, hardware TalkBack traversal, real device haptics, optical QR scanning, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.ui.ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.connectionStatusConnectedActionsStayBoundedAtLargeFontAcrossSupportedLanguages -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 6 while the Android phone is disconnected. - Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` now sends unauthenticated relay-routed `memory.summary.drafts.list`, `memory.summary.draft.approve`, and `memory.summary.draft.dismiss` requests and requires `authentication_required` before payload handling.
- Guardrail: the relay ciphertext boundary marker list now includes the three memory-summary draft command names. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the new unauthenticated smoke request ids, command names, and default no-device coverage phrase.
- Caveat: this is no-device relay/runtime auth-gate evidence. It does not prove physical Android install, optical QR scanning, hardware TalkBack traversal, real provider-backed memory-summary behavior, physical relay reachability, or real different-network connectivity.

### 2026-07-01 Authenticated Relay Image Attachment Vision-Gate Smoke

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 6 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that authenticated RuntimeDevServer relay smoke covered document attachment success and non-vision image rejection, but not the positive vision-capable image path.
- Result: RuntimeDevServer aggregate mock mode now exposes `dev-mock-vision` with `chat` plus `vision` capabilities. `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` requires `models.list` to include that vision-capable mock model, then sends `smoke-chat-image-non-vision` through non-vision `dev-mock` and `smoke-chat-image-vision` through `dev-mock-vision`.
- Result: the non-vision path must return `unsupported_attachment` before backend deltas, while the vision-capable path must stream `chat.delta` / `chat.done` and include the deterministic attachment marker from the mock backend.
- Guardrail: the relay ciphertext boundary marker list now includes the image prompt, file name, base64 marker, request ids, error code, vision-gate message, and vision model id so encrypted relay frames cannot expose those plaintext values. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the RuntimeDevServer vision mock model, image rejection smoke, image success smoke, and default no-device coverage phrase.
- Caveat: this is no-device relay/runtime smoke evidence. It does not prove Android picker behavior on hardware, real device content-provider reads, optical QR scanning, physical relay reachability, live provider-backed vision inference, or real different-network connectivity.

### 2026-07-01 App Icon Small-Size Readability Guard

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 3 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that `script/check_app_icons.py` verified the launcher asset chain and dimensions but did not inspect pixels for small-size readability.
- Result: `script/check_app_icons.py` now decodes the source, preview, Android density launcher PNGs, round launcher PNGs, adaptive foreground PNG, and PNG-backed macOS `AppIcon.icns` chunks with a stdlib PNG reader. It rejects blank, mostly transparent, mostly white, low-contrast, off-center, or edge-less icons before a launcher/Dock screenshot pass.
- Guardrail: the app icon check now requires visible pixel coverage, non-white foreground coverage, dark foreground coverage, center-mark coverage, luminance contrast, and a strong-edge ratio for Android launcher assets and macOS Dock icon chunks. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the readability functions and default no-device gate coverage phrase, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports the app-icon readability addendum.
- Caveat: this is source/script/resource-processing evidence. It does not prove Android launcher rendering on a physical device, OEM icon masks, macOS Dock rendering, human small-screen perception, app-store metadata, or screenshot aesthetics.

### 2026-07-01 Android Relay Probe Strict Readiness Contract

- Scope: no-device progress on QR-only route repair and relay-readiness hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that Android still accepted a legacy `AETHERLINK_RELAY probe ready` shortcut even though the Swift relay only documents keyed readiness fields.
- Result: Android relay reachability parsing now treats a relay route as ready only when the non-consuming probe response reports a known or allocated route and `runtime_waiting`; the legacy `probe ready` token no longer bypasses the route-known plus runtime-waiting contract.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeResponseParserRequiresKnownRouteAndWaitingRuntime` now asserts that `AETHERLINK_RELAY probe ready` is rejected, while preserving accepted `known=1 runtime_waiting=1` and `allocated=true runtime_waiting=true` responses.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the negative legacy probe-ready regression alongside the existing relay preflight parser and QR pre-connect failure coverage.
- Caveat: this is no-device JVM/source/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android relay reachability from a phone network, optical QR scanning, live pairing, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real production arbitrary-network connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeResponseParserRequiresKnownRouteAndWaitingRuntime --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayQrPairingFailsBeforeConnectWhenDeviceCannotReachRelayRoute --console=plain`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py script/check_protocol_schema.py`
- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/check_no_device_quality.sh`
- `"$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb" devices -l` (no attached devices)

### 2026-07-01 Pairing QR Schema Whitespace Contract

- Scope: no-device progress on the QR-only remote-route roadmap and trust-material hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that the decoded pairing QR schema still allowed whitespace-mutated trust/bootstrap tokens even though parser/verifier/docs reject them.
- Result: `packages/protocol-schema/pairing-qr.schema.json` now defines `noWhitespaceString` and uses it for QR trust/bootstrap identifiers and aliases including `pairing_nonce`, runtime id aliases, `runtime_public_key` aliases, `runtime_key_fingerprint` aliases, `route_token` aliases, and relay nonce aliases.
- Guardrail: `script/check_protocol_schema.py` now requires the `noWhitespaceString` definition and verifies that QR trust/routing identifiers reject whitespace either through that shared ref or an equivalent `^\S+$` field pattern.
- Guardrail: `script/check_docs_hygiene.py` now pins the product QR bootstrap docs: normal product scans require `runtime_public_key`, `route_token`, remote route material, and the QR verifier command includes `--require-production-bootstrap` plus relay/no-direct checks.
- Caveat: this is no-device schema/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android install, camera/optical QR scan reliability, hardware haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real production arbitrary-network reachability.

Verified after this change:

- `python3 script/check_protocol_schema.py`

### 2026-07-01 Android Product QR Bootstrap Enforcement

- Scope: no-device progress on the QR-only remote-route roadmap while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified that the normal QR scan path still accepted identity-only QR payloads in debug builds.
- Result: Android normal QR scan and pairing deeplink handling now require product bootstrap material regardless of build type. `trustRuntimeFromPairingQr` defaults to requiring a runtime public key, route token, and complete non-expired relay/P2P route material; identity-only QR can still exercise the old USB reverse fallback only when diagnostic/test code explicitly opts out with `requireRemoteRoute = false`.
- Result: strict QR parsing now disables local direct diagnostic endpoints while product route material is required, so fixed host/port QR data remains diagnostic-only instead of becoming the normal scan path.
- Result: `script/verify_pairing_qr.swift` now has `--require-production-bootstrap`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` uses it with `--require-relay-route` to prove generated QR PNGs contain runtime public key, route token, complete relay route material, and no direct host/port fallback.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.productPairingQrParserRequiresRuntimePublicKeyAndRouteTokenWhenRemoteRouteIsRequired`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustRuntimeFromPairingQrRejectsIdentityOnlyQrInNormalScanPath`, and scanner raw-value tests cover the stricter normal path. `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.diagnosticIdentityOnlyPairingQrCanUseUsbReverseFallbackWhenRemoteRouteIsNotRequired` keeps the old debug fallback explicitly diagnostic.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the strict app wiring, ViewModel default, production bootstrap parser guard, QR verifier source, no-device gate entries, and coverage phrases.
- Caveat: this is no-device JVM/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android install, camera/optical QR scanning, hardware haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real production arbitrary-network reachability.

Verified after this change:

- `swiftc -typecheck script/verify_pairing_qr.swift`
- temporary QR PNG render/decode smoke: complete production relay bootstrap QR passed; missing `runtime_public_key` and missing `route_token` QR PNGs failed under `--require-production-bootstrap`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.productPairingQrParserRejectsIdentityOnlyQrWhenRemoteRouteIsRequired --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.productPairingQrParserRequiresRuntimePublicKeyAndRouteTokenWhenRemoteRouteIsRequired --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustRuntimeFromPairingQrRejectsIdentityOnlyQrInNormalScanPath --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.diagnosticIdentityOnlyPairingQrCanUseUsbReverseFallbackWhenRemoteRouteIsNotRequired --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustRuntimeFromPairingQrWithCompactRelayUriConnectsRelayAndSendsPairingRequest --console=plain`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.AppNavigationTest.pairingQrRawValueAcceptsCompactRelayPayloadsFromScanner --tests com.localagentbridge.android.AppNavigationTest.pairingQrCandidateAllowsInvalidAetherLinkPairUrisToReachStructuredErrors --tests com.localagentbridge.android.AppNavigationTest.pairingQrScannerClassifiesRawValuesBeforeConsumingCameraResult --console=plain`
- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh` (syntax only)
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py`
- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

- Result: Android QR scanner chrome now exposes stable scanner, title, close, camera surface, instructions, detail, feedback, cancel, permission-panel, permission-title/detail/action/cancel tags. Compact-height scanner targets use a smaller upper scan frame so invalid/unsupported QR feedback panels do not cover the target area, while normal-height scanner targets keep the larger frame.

It verifies scanner chrome/title/action bounds, title/action separation, scan-target/instruction-panel separation, feedback/detail/cancel separation, and permission-panel title/detail/action/cancel separation.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the stable scanner tags, focused multilingual compact-bounds regression, default no-device gate entry, and coverage phrase `Android QR scanner compact large-font bounds`.
- Caveat: this is no-device Compose/JVM and local gate evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, hardware permission-dialog behavior, real camera preview framing, hardware torch behavior, optical QR scan reliability, TalkBack traversal, launcher icon readability, live provider-backed chat/cancel latency, or real different-network reachability.

### 2026-07-01 Android Authenticated Route Refresh Default-Off

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 4 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; GPT-5.5 subagent audit was used for the route-refresh policy review.
- Result: Android production dependencies now keep authenticated `route.refresh` disabled by default. The normal Android client does not advertise `route.refresh` in `HelloPayload.capabilities`, does not schedule lease refresh, does not retry refresh failures, and does not send `MessageType.RouteRefresh` unless `authenticatedRouteRefreshEnabled` is explicitly enabled for diagnostic/test coverage.
- Result: normal route repair stays QR-first: users recover stale, expired, or incomplete relay/P2P route material by scanning the latest QR. The protocol DTOs, payload validators, and macOS runtime handler remain in place so diagnostic coverage can still validate authenticated route-refresh responses when deliberately opted in.
- Guardrail: default-off Android regressions cover compact relay QR pairing and trusted relay auto-reconnect without sending `route.refresh`; route-refresh-specific ViewModel and real relay TCP integration tests now opt in explicitly. Copy hygiene and the no-device gate pin the coverage phrase `Android authenticated route.refresh default-off addendum: production Android does not advertise or send authenticated route.refresh unless explicitly enabled for diagnostic coverage`.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM and local relay TCP evidence. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, optical camera reliability, hardware TalkBack traversal, production P2P traversal, or real different-network reachability.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.clientCapabilitiesAdvertiseRuntimeOwnedHistoryMemoryAndAttachments --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.compactRelayQrPairingResultPersistsTrustedRelayAndClearsPendingRoute --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.viewModelAutoReconnectsTrustedRelayOnInitAndRefreshesRuntimeState --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedRuntimeRefreshesRelayRouteMaterial --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedRuntimeSchedulesRouteRefreshBeforeLeaseExpiry --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedP2pRuntimeSchedulesRouteRefreshBeforeRecordExpiry --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.disconnectCancelsScheduledRouteRefreshRetry --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.forgetTrustedRuntimeClosesConnectionAndClearsPendingRouteRefresh -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelRelayIntegrationTest -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`

### 2026-07-01 macOS QR-Only Pairing First-Run Recovery Gate

> Superseded on 2026-07-18: the pairing-only recovery entry point is required when no route exists. Status continues to hide diagnostics on a clean first run.

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 4 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only audit found that macOS clean first-run Pairing still exposed `Connection Recovery` setup fields even though normal users should pair or recover routes by scanning QR.
- Result: `shouldShowPairingRouteSetupPanel(model:)` now reuses the narrower route-diagnostics visibility rule. Clean first-run Pairing hides `Connection Recovery`; saved route diagnostics, saved bootstrap route settings, or a concrete route-preparation issue still expose the recovery panel.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testRouteDiagnosticsPanelStaysHiddenOnCleanFirstRunAndPairingHidesSetup` proves clean first-run Status and Pairing both hide the recovery panel, while saved route state and route-preparation issues still show it. Copy hygiene rejects the older pairing-only fallback that exposed setup merely because automatic route preparation was unavailable, and the no-device gate reports the macOS QR-only pairing addendum.
- Caveat: this is no-device macOS source/XCTest evidence. It does not prove physical Android QR scan, optical camera reliability, hardware VoiceOver/TalkBack traversal, production P2P traversal, or real different-network reachability.

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 runtime resource policy while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only audit continued to flag QR-only pairing UX as the next larger route-hardening gap, while this smaller model-residency provider-failure slice was closed first.
- Result: `AggregatingLlmBackendResidencyTests` now covers provider-specific unload failure reporting. When the previous provider model fails to unload during a model switch, the aggregate runtime emits an `.unloadFailed` residency event with provider, model id, reason, and provider message, while the next chat still routes to the newly selected provider model.
- Guardrail: the default no-device quality gate now runs `AggregatingLlmBackendResidencyTests/testUnloadFailureEmitsProviderSpecificFailureEventWithoutBreakingNextChat`, and copy hygiene pins both the focused regression and the no-device coverage phrase.

### 2026-07-01 Long-Inactivity Memory Summary Draft Dismiss

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 longer-inactivity memory summarization while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; the GPT-5.5 read-only explorer recommended durable dismiss before LLM-generated summaries or richer inactivity policy.
- Result: `memory.summary.draft.dismiss` is now an authenticated runtime command. The runtime recomputes the owner-scoped current draft, validates optional stale-guard metadata, persists an owner-scoped dismiss decision by draft id, hides dismissed drafts from later `memory.summary.drafts.list` responses, and does not create or update runtime memory entries.
- Result: Android advertises the dismiss capability, serializes dismiss payloads with `expected_session_id` and `expected_source_message_count`, tracks per-draft pending dismiss state, renders a Dismiss Suggested Memory action in Settings > Memory, removes dismissed drafts from review state on success, and keeps draft previews/source excerpts out of device storage.
- Guardrail: dismiss is idempotent for the same current draft id, owner-scoped, authenticated, and separate from approval. The runtime does not trust Android for session ownership, and dismissed decisions do not call a model, inject chat context, or write memory.
- Caveat: this is no-device Swift/Android JVM/Compose evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, TalkBack traversal on hardware, optical QR scan, live provider-backed summarization quality, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter 'LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummaryDraftDismissRequiresAuthentication|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummaryDraftDismissHidesOwnerScopedDraftWithoutWritingMemory'`

### 2026-07-01 Approved Memory Source Metadata

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 longer-inactivity memory summarization while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer recommended durable source metadata for approved memory entries before adding reject/dismiss persistence.
- Result: approved long-inactivity memory entries now carry optional durable `source` metadata. The runtime stores this metadata in the JSONL memory event log, includes it in `memory.summary.draft.approve` responses and later `memory.list` results, and preserves it when the same memory entry is updated without an explicit source.
- Result: Swift, Android, and JSON schema protocol definitions now share the approved-memory source payload shape: source kind, draft id, deterministic summary method, source session metadata, source range, bounded visible-transcript source pointers, and source message count.
- Result: Android protocol DTOs, runtime cache mapping, and in-memory UI state preserve approved-memory source metadata, while the existing runtime-owned memory redaction path still keeps approved-memory source excerpts out of device storage. The newer approved source review UI is tracked in the section above.
- Guardrail: `source` is optional for backwards compatibility with existing memory logs and clients. The runtime derives source metadata from the recomputed owner-scoped draft instead of trusting client-supplied metadata, and source pointers remain bounded visible transcript excerpts that exclude assistant reasoning, runtime-only system context, archived/deleted sessions, and other owners' transcripts.
- Caveat: this is no-device Swift/Android JVM evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, TalkBack traversal, optical QR scan, live provider-backed summarization quality, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity.

### 2026-07-01 Long-Inactivity Memory Summary Draft Approval

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 longer-inactivity memory summarization while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer reviewed the approval/injection slice and recommended a single authenticated approval command before any reject/dismiss store.
- Result: `memory.summary.draft.approve` is now an authenticated runtime command. The runtime recomputes the owner-scoped draft by `draft_id`, validates optional stale-guard metadata, writes an idempotent runtime memory entry with id `memory-summary:<draft_id>`, and hides approved drafts from later `memory.summary.drafts.list` responses for that owner.
- Result: Android advertises the approval capability, serializes approval payloads with `expected_session_id` and `expected_source_message_count`, tracks per-draft pending state, renders an Add Suggested Memory action in Settings > Memory, updates runtime memory state from the response, and keeps draft previews/source excerpts out of device storage.
- Guardrail: this older approval slice did not add reject/dismiss persistence, source-pointer persistence on approved memory entries, LLM-generated summaries, or editable approval UI. Source-pointer persistence for approved entries is now tracked in the newer approved memory source metadata section above. The runtime does not trust client-provided session ids for ownership; unavailable or wrong-owner drafts fail without cross-owner disclosure, and stale drafts require refresh/review before approval.
- Caveat: this is no-device Swift/Android JVM/Compose evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, TalkBack traversal, optical QR scan, live provider-backed summarization quality, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter 'LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummaryDraftsListRequiresAuthentication|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummaryDraftsListReturnsOwnerScopedActiveVisibleDraftsOnly|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummaryDraftApproveRequiresAuthentication|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummaryDraftApproveWritesIdempotentOwnerScopedMemoryAndHidesApprovedDraft'`

### 2026-07-01 Android Memory Summary Draft Review UI

### 2026-07-01 Long-Inactivity Memory Summary Drafts

### 2026-07-01 Long-Inactivity Memory Summarization Eligibility

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 longer-inactivity memory summarization while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer reviewed the smallest safe first slice.

- Guardrail: this slice intentionally does not call `RuntimeMemoryStore.upsert`; it identifies eligible source sessions only. LLM-generated summaries, deterministic summary drafts, durable source pointers, approval, and runtime-memory injection remain future work.
