### 2026-07-01 Context-Window Compaction Metadata

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 context-window-aware session compaction while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer checked the protocol/Android impact before implementation.
- Result: `models.result` can now carry optional `context_window_tokens`; macOS emits it from `ModelInfo`, Android protocol/state mapping preserves positive values, and the protocol schema/docs define it as runtime-host-derived metadata rather than a client-side provider API contract.
- Result: Ollama `/api/show` and LM Studio model-list parsing now preserve context-window hints when the backend exposes them. Missing hints remain optional and fall back to the existing runtime compaction budget.
- Result: macOS `chat.send` resolves the installed local chat model before compaction and uses the model's context-window hint to choose the runtime-side compaction character budget. The request event still stores only client-visible messages, while backend-only memory/capability/compaction context stays out of client-visible history.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now pins the protocol/schema/Android mapping, backend parsing, model-aware router compaction call, focused regressions, and no-device coverage phrase.
- Caveat: this is no-device macOS unit/backend and Android JVM evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical QR scan, live provider-backed chat quality, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity.

### 2026-07-01 SQLite Deleted-Session Retention Pruning

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 SQLite/FTS rollout hardening and retention policy while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only sidecar recommended this storage-internal slice.
- Result: `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStore` now exposes an explicit `pruneDeletedSessions(ownerDeviceID:deletedBefore:limit:)` primitive for sessions whose latest lifecycle event is `deleted` and whose deletion timestamp is older than the supplied cutoff.
- Result: pruning is scoped to one owner key at a time, deletes only the matching SQLite event rows, rebuilds the FTS index after physical deletion, and leaves active, archived, recent-deleted, and same-session-id/different-owner rows intact.
- Result: pruned owner/session pairs are written to `runtime_chat_retention_tombstones`, so later legacy JSONL freshness imports skip those sessions instead of resurrecting rows that retention already removed. Ordinary appends to a pruned owner/session fail loudly.
- Guardrail: `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests` covers cutoff behavior, owner-scope isolation, same session id across owners, idempotency, post-prune append rejection, active/archived search preservation, and tombstone-backed legacy backfill resurrection prevention. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the SQLite retention method, tombstone schema, transaction, cutoff guard, focused tests, and no-device coverage phrase.
- Caveat: this is a SQLite storage primitive only. It is not yet exposed through router protocol, UI, or user-facing retention policy settings, and it does not prove physical Android rendering, optical QR scan, live provider-backed chat, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py script/check_protocol_schema.py`

- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

### 2026-07-01 SQLite Runtime Chat Default Store Rollout

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 SQLite/FTS rollout after the parity and JSONL backfill slices. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only sidecar reviewed the default-switch risk and guardrail list.
- Result: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter` and `CompanionAppModel` now default runtime chat history to `RuntimeChatEventStoreDefaults.productionStore()`, which constructs `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStore` with `JSONLRuntimeChatEventStore.defaultFileURL()` as the legacy backfill source.
- Result: the production default factory can still be pointed at temp SQLite/JSONL URLs for focused tests, avoiding real Application Support writes while proving the same default-store construction path.
- Result: SQLite backfill metadata now records a legacy JSONL freshness signature with path, size, and modification time, so a later legacy append can be imported idempotently instead of being skipped just because the file path was already seen.
- Result: the SQLite runtime chat database now reuses the runtime event-log file protection policy, creating or correcting the AetherLink support directory to `0700` and the database file to `0600` before serving the production default store.
- Guardrail: `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests` now covers the production default factory, SQLite legacy backfill, marker-refresh import path, and SQLite database owner-only permission creation/correction. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the router/model default-store snippets, factory construction, freshness signature, SQLite protection calls, focused tests, and no-device summary phrase.
- Caveat: this is no-device macOS SwiftPM/source/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical QR scan, live provider-backed chat, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity. Corrupt legacy JSONL remains surfaced as a runtime chat-store error rather than silently falling back.

### 2026-07-01 JSONL-to-SQLite Runtime Chat Event Store Backfill

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 SQLite/FTS migration while preserving the existing JSONL runtime chat event authority. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; GPT-5.5 read-only sidecars reviewed the backfill and guardrail slice.
- Result: added JSONL-to-SQLite backfill to `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStore` without switching production defaults away from JSONL.
- Result: the backfill reuses JSONL decoding/validation, sanitizes imported events before SQLite storage, preserves nil and authenticated `ownerDeviceID` scopes, skips already-imported event ids for idempotent migration, and keeps ordinary live appends failing loudly on duplicate event ids.
- Result: imported rows rebuild the session FTS index once per batch, so backfilled sessions are searchable through the same deterministic `search.rank`, bounded `search.snippet`, and `search.matched_fields` path as newly appended SQLite rows.
- Guardrail: `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests` now covers idempotent import, owner-scope preservation, legacy nil-owner isolation, inline attachment byte redaction before storage/FTS, search metadata after import, and corrupt JSONL rejection without partial silent migration.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` still runs `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests`, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now pins the backfill entry point, JSONL decode reuse, idempotent import path, focused regressions, and no-device coverage summary phrase.
- Caveat: this is no-device macOS SwiftPM/source/script evidence. It does not prove production default switching, physical Android rendering, optical QR scan, live provider-backed chat, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests`
- `swift test --filter 'SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testRuntimeChatStoreSearchesSessionSummariesAndTranscriptWithinOwnerScope|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testChatSessionsListQueryFiltersRuntimeOwnedSummaries'`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py script/check_docs_hygiene.py script/check_protocol_schema.py`

- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

### 2026-06-30 Runtime-Owned Session Search Filter

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap v0.2 session/search work while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; GPT-5.5 explorers Huygens, Jason, and Parfit were used for read-only roadmap/slice review.

- Result: Android Settings > Chat history keeps the existing immediate local `filterChatHistorySessions(...)` behavior, and its explicit refresh button now forwards the current trimmed search text to `refreshRuntimeChatHistory(query:)` for runtime-owned search.
- Result: the protocol schema now matches the runtime history handler contract that `limit: 0` is a valid empty result window for both `chat.sessions.list` and `chat.messages.list`.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now includes `testRuntimeChatStoreSearchesSessionSummariesAndTranscriptWithinOwnerScope`, `testChatSessionsListQueryFiltersRuntimeOwnedSummaries`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.refreshRuntimeChatHistoryCanSendTrimmedQuery`, and `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsChatHistoryRefreshUsesCurrentSearchQuery`; `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the store/router/Android DTO/UI/test/no-device coverage.
- Caveat: this is no-device SwiftPM/protocol/schema/JVM/Compose evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, TalkBack traversal, optical QR scan, live provider-backed chat, production P2P traversal, or real different-network connectivity.

### 2026-06-30 RuntimeDevServer Malformed Pairing Identity Relay Smoke

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap items 4 and 6 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed malformed client identity was still covered only by lower-level router tests, not by the active RuntimeDevServer relay smoke.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` now sends a valid QR nonce/code with a malformed client public key through the RuntimeDevServer relay path and requires `pairing_invalid_device_identity` through `smoke-pair-invalid-identity`.
- Result: the same rejected connection then sends `runtime.health` through `smoke-pair-invalid-identity-health` and requires `authentication_required`, proving the malformed identity request did not authenticate the connection.
- Result: a fresh `hello` for the same canonical device id returns `pairing_required`, while the active QR remains usable for the later valid `smoke-pair` request. This proves malformed pairing identity rejection keeps the device untrusted while preserving the active QR for a later valid pairing.
- Result: relay plaintext-boundary markers now include the malformed identity request prefix, `pairing_invalid_device_identity`, and the invalid identity device name. The focused relay smoke verified 224 encrypted frame bodies after this expansion.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the malformed identity helper, request IDs, `pairing_invalid_device_identity`, same-connection auth gate, valid-later-pairing documentation, no-device summary, and docs caveat. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports `RuntimeDevServer relay malformed pairing identity rejection keeps the device untrusted while preserving the active QR for a later valid pairing`.
- Caveat: this is no-device RuntimeDevServer relay/script evidence over the local development relay. It does not prove physical Android QR scan, optical camera reliability, physical Android rendering, TalkBack traversal, real provider-backed model output, production P2P traversal, or real different-network reachability.

Verified after this change:

- `bash -lc 'swiftc -typecheck script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift && ./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh'`

### 2026-06-30 RuntimeDevServer Multi-Device Owner Isolation Smoke

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 6 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed the safest design is to pair device A through the active development pairing flow and pre-seed device B into the same temporary trusted-device file because a successful pairing consumes the active pairing session.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` now authenticates two trusted devices through sequential fresh RuntimeDevServer relay connections and validates multi-device owner isolation over the active dev-server path.
- Result: the smoke proves device B cannot see device A memory before B writes its own same-id note, device A and B keep separate same-id memory contents, deleting B's memory does not delete A's memory, device B cannot list or read device A chat messages, device B cannot rename/archive/delete device A's session and receives `chat_session_not_found`, and device A cannot see device B's session or messages.
- Result: relay plaintext-boundary markers now include the owner-isolation request prefix and per-device memory/chat text so the encrypted-frame check covers the two-device isolation traffic as well. The focused relay smoke verified 210 encrypted frame bodies after this expansion.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the pre-seeded trusted-device helper, owner-isolation smoke helper, request IDs, `chat_session_not_found` assertions, no-device summary, and docs caveat. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports `authenticated relay smoke validates memory, chat session, message, and session mutation owner-device boundaries across two trusted devices`.
- Caveat: this is no-device RuntimeDevServer relay/script evidence over the local development relay. It does not prove physical Android QR scan, optical camera reliability, physical Android rendering, TalkBack traversal, real provider-backed model output, production P2P traversal, or real different-network reachability. Backend memory-injection isolation remains covered by `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testAuthenticatedDevicesCannotCrossReadInjectOrMutateChatAndMemory`; this smoke proves the RuntimeDevServer relay read/mutation boundary.

Verified after this change:

### 2026-06-30 macOS P2P Route Material Redaction Guard

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap items 5 and 6 while the Android phone is disconnected. A GPT-5.5 read-only explorer audited independent next-slice candidates and was closed afterward; GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: macOS Activity technical diagnostics, route diagnostic disclosures, and companion log storage now redact the full P2P rendezvous route-material family in addition to existing relay/provider material. Covered forms include canonical `p2p_class`, `p2p_record_id`, `p2p_encrypted_body`, `p2p_expires_at`, `p2p_anti_replay_nonce`, `p2p_protocol_version`, camelCase/internal variants such as `p2pRecordID`, `p2pRecordId`, `p2pRouteClass`, and `p2pExpiresAtEpochMillis`, plus compact aliases `pc`, `prid`, `peb`, `px`, `pn`, and `pv`.
- Result: safe relay endpoint text such as `relay.example.test:43171` remains visible, but opaque P2P record IDs, encrypted bodies, anti-replay nonces, expiries, and protocol-version route material collapse to the existing localized sensitive-detail redaction text.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testActivityTechnicalDetailsRedactRouteSecrets`, `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testRouteDiagnosticDisclosureRedactsSensitiveDetails`, and `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionLogSanitizerRedactsProviderEndpointsAndSecrets` now cover canonical, camelCase/internal, JSON, and compact P2P route-material forms. `script/check_macos_localization.py`, `script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` pin the source/test contract and the no-device summary `macOS P2P route material redaction`.
- Caveat: this is no-device macOS source/test/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical/camera QR scanning, real device haptics, TalkBack/VoiceOver traversal, live provider-backed chat/cancel, real P2P NAT traversal, production signaling, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-30 RuntimeDevServer Authenticated Model Residency Smoke

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 6 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed that model-residency unload was covered by SwiftPM unit tests and provider wire-format tests, but not positively exercised over the authenticated RuntimeDevServer relay smoke path.
- Result: `RuntimeDevServer` now has a development-only aggregate mock backend mode for smoke tests, enabled by `AETHERLINK_DEV_MOCK_AGGREGATE_RESIDENCY=1`. It exposes an Ollama mock model and an LM Studio mock model through `AggregatingLlmBackend`, uses a short smoke-only idle unload delay, and records unload events only from the mock backend `unloadModel(providerModelID:)` call path.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` now verifies that the authenticated RuntimeDevServer path lists the LM Studio aggregate mock model, repeats the same Ollama mock model without unloading, switches to the LM Studio mock model and observes the Ollama unload event, then observes the LM Studio idle-unload event.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports the RuntimeDevServer model-residency smoke addendum, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the RuntimeDevServer dev-only aggregate mock environment hooks, smoke request IDs, unload event assertions, no-device summary, and docs caveat.
- Caveat: this is no-device RuntimeDevServer smoke evidence over development mocks. It proves the dev-server path can route through `AggregatingLlmBackend` and call backend `unloadModel` for model-switch and idle residency policy, but it does not prove live Ollama or LM Studio unload behavior against real provider processes, physical Android QR scan, physical Android rendering, production P2P traversal, or real different-network reachability.

- `swift build --package-path apps/macos --product RuntimeDevServer`
- `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh`

### 2026-06-30 Authenticated P2P Rendezvous Route Refresh Contract

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 5 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified authenticated `route.refresh` P2P rendezvous material as the next additive contract after QR/storage/restore planning.
- Result: the active authenticated `route.refresh` protocol response can now carry complete opaque `p2p_rendezvous` route material alongside, or instead of, relay material: `p2p_class`, `p2p_record_id`, `p2p_encrypted_body`, `p2p_expires_at`, `p2p_anti_replay_nonce`, and `p2p_protocol_version`.

- Result: Android `RouteRefreshPayload` and trusted-runtime conversion now accept P2P-only refresh material, persist it as trusted remote route material for the pinned runtime identity, and reject incomplete or expired P2P material instead of keeping stale direct or partial remote routes.
- Guardrail: the protocol schema, `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest`, `script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now pin the P2P `route.refresh` field family and complete-or-rejected behavior.
- Caveat: this is authenticated route-material renewal only. It does not implement P2P allocation, candidate exchange, STUN, hole punching, production signaling, a real `RuntimePeerToPeerConnector`, physical Android QR scanning, or real different-network reachability.

### 2026-06-30 Android Trusted P2P Rendezvous Restore

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 5 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer audited the remaining trusted-runtime P2P restore gaps while the implementation stayed local to Android persistence, ViewModel route selection, planner, tests, scripts, and docs.
- Result: Android `TrustedRuntime`, `PairingStore`, and `RuntimeTrustedRuntime` now carry complete opaque `p2p_rendezvous` route material through accepted pairing and QR route-refresh scan paths: route class, record id, encrypted body, expiration, anti-replay nonce, and protocol version.
- Result: stored trusted P2P route material is validated on write and read. Complete future records are persisted; incomplete or expired records are dropped and their preference keys are cleaned up. Expired trusted P2P routes surface through the existing fresh-QR recovery path instead of becoming a direct endpoint fallback.
- Result: `RuntimeRemoteRoutePlanner` now prepares saved trusted P2P rendezvous routes when they match the pinned runtime identity, and trusted reconnect treats saved P2P route material like saved relay material for endpoint suppression, discovery suppression, and auto-reconnect target generation.
- Result: product route-required QR parsing accepts P2P-only remote-route QR payloads, and pending pairing/accepted pairing no longer waits for Bonjour discovery or stores diagnostic direct endpoints when complete P2P rendezvous material is present.
- Guardrail: `PairingStoreTest`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest`, `script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now pin trusted P2P persistence, cleanup, accepted-pairing storage, route-refresh QR storage, product QR acceptance, saved-route planning, injected-clock expiry, and no-direct-endpoint fallback.
- Caveat: this is trusted-route storage and route planning only. A later same-day pass added authenticated `route.refresh` support for renewing opaque P2P route material, but neither pass implements P2P allocation, candidate exchange, STUN, hole punching, production signaling, a real `RuntimePeerToPeerConnector`, physical Android QR scanning, or real different-network reachability.

### 2026-06-30 Android P2P Rendezvous QR Planning

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 5 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer confirmed that the next useful slice after the core P2P route-preparation contract was QR/schema/pending-storage/planner wiring without claiming real NAT traversal.
- Result: Android pairing QR parsing now accepts a separate P2P namespace, `p2p_*` plus compact `pc` / `prid` / `peb` / `px` / `pn` / `pv`, for opaque `p2p_rendezvous` records. The existing `rendezvous_*` fields remain relay aliases, so relay and P2P route material do not collide.
- Result: P2P QR material is complete-or-rejected: route class, opaque record id, encrypted body, expiration, anti-replay nonce, and protocol version 1 are required. Expiration accepts seconds or milliseconds like relay leases.
- Result: pending pairing route storage now preserves complete P2P rendezvous material, drops direct host/port fields, rejects incomplete persisted P2P records, and caps pending route lifetime at the shorter of the pairing window, relay lease, and P2P record expiry.
- Result: `RuntimeRemoteRoutePlanner` now turns pending P2P QR records into prepared P2P routes before relay routes. This is route planning only; no trusted-runtime P2P persistence or real P2P connector was added.
- Guardrail: `RuntimePairingPayloadParserTest`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest`, `script/check_protocol_schema.py`, `script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now cover the P2P QR namespace, compact fixture, complete-family validation, pending storage, and P2P-before-relay planning.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM/source/script evidence. It does not implement STUN, hole punching, candidate exchange, production signaling, trusted-runtime P2P persistence, a real `RuntimePeerToPeerConnector`, physical Android rendering, optical/camera QR scan reliability, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-30 Android P2P Rendezvous Route Preparation Contract

A record maps to `PreparedRemoteRuntimeRoute.PeerToPeer` only when it has an opaque record id, encrypted candidate material, a future expiration, a nonblank anti-replay nonce, and the supported protocol version.
- Result: the prepared P2P route carries no host or port material. The existing connection manager ordering then tries prepared P2P before prepared relay, while preserving relay fallback when the P2P connector fails.
- Guardrail: `RuntimePeerToPeerRoutePreparationTest` covers valid mapping, blank/whitespace/stale/version-mismatched rejection, injected-clock expiration, invalid-record dropping, and no host/port fields. `RuntimeConnectionManagerTest.relayConnectorCanFallbackAfterPreparedPeerToPeerRouteFails` remains in the default no-device gate to keep fallback ordering covered.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the P2P route-preparation source, regressions, and no-device summary string. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the P2P preparation test and reports the P2P route-prep addendum.
- Caveat: this is route-record preparation only. It does not wire P2P material into QR parsing, protocol schema, pending/trusted storage, UI, production signaling, STUN, hole punching, real NAT traversal, or a real `RuntimePeerToPeerConnector`.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM/source/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical/camera QR scan reliability, physical device reconnect, real device haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-30 Android Core Transport Trusted Last-Known Resolver Guard

- Scope: no-device progress on QR-only trusted route hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only audit identified that the core transport default route resolver could still convert a `RuntimeEndpointSource.TrustedLastKnown` endpoint hint into a direct TCP route if a future caller bypassed the already-hardened ViewModel/UI helpers.
- Result: `RuntimeConnectionManager.DefaultRuntimeRouteResolver` now ignores trusted last-known endpoint hints instead of emitting a connectable direct TCP candidate for stale fixed host/port material.
- Result: current-session endpoint hints such as pairing QR, Bonjour/USB discovery, emulator, and manual diagnostic routes keep their existing behavior, and a prepared relay route still connects when a target also carries a stale trusted last-known endpoint hint.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeConnectionManagerTest.defaultResolverIgnoresTrustedLastKnownEndpointHintForPairedTarget` covers the stale direct endpoint skip and verifies the direct transport connector is not called. `RuntimeConnectionManagerTest.preparedRelayRouteStillConnectsWhenTargetHasTrustedLastKnownEndpointHint` covers the fresh relay route path with stale direct hint material present.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the core resolver guard, regression names, and no-device summary string. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the regressions and reports the trusted route core addendum.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM/source/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical/camera QR scan reliability, physical device reconnect, real device haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-30 Android Trusted Last-Known Route UI Recovery

- Scope: no-device progress on QR-only trusted route hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only audit identified that Android UI helpers could still treat stale trusted last-known direct endpoint state as a normal connectable trusted route after the storage and connection layers had been hardened.
- Result: Android route UI now excludes `RuntimeEndpointSource.TrustedLastKnown` from normal connectable route detection. Stale trusted last-known endpoint hints and legacy selected last-known endpoints drive latest-QR recovery instead of `Connect trusted route`.
- Result: Connection status hero copy, route notice action, and chat composer/empty-state readiness now agree with the route resolver: saved relay routes and fresh/current discovery or diagnostic routes can connect, but stale last-known fixed endpoints require scanning the latest QR.
- Guardrail: `AppNavigationTest.routeNoticeActionIgnoresTrustedLastKnownEndpointForNormalQrFirstRecovery` covers helper-level route notice, hero, and composer behavior for stale last-known endpoint state. `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.connectionStatusTrustedLastKnownOnlyRouteScansLatestQrWithHaptic` covers the visible Connection Status action and verifies it invokes QR scanning rather than connect.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the UI helper exclusion, regression names, and no-device summary string. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the regressions and reports the trusted route UI addendum.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM/Compose/source/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical/camera QR scan reliability, physical device reconnect, real device haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-30 Android Trusted Last-Known Direct Fallback Removal

- Scope: no-device progress on QR-only trusted route hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only audit identified the stale trusted last-known direct endpoint fallback that could still be manufactured from in-memory trusted state.
- Result: Android trusted reconnect target generation now drops `RuntimeEndpointSource.TrustedLastKnown` endpoint hints. Trusted reconnect is identity-only unless a complete saved relay route or fresh matching discovery/diagnostic route is available.
- Result: route candidates no longer use a stale trusted last-known direct endpoint as the fallback when matching Bonjour metadata is missing or auto-reconnect has only old fixed host/port material.
- Result: fresh matching Bonjour discovery, explicit current-session pairing/diagnostic hints, USB reverse debug fallback, and saved relay reconnect remain available through their existing route paths.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustedRuntimeConnectionTargetDropsTrustedLastKnownEndpoint`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeRouteCandidatesUseDiscoveredEndpointInsteadOfTrustedLastKnownFallback`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.autoReconnectTrustedRuntimeTargetWaitsForFreshRouteWhenOnlyTrustedLastKnownEndpointExists`, and `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.autoReconnectRouteCandidatesDoNotUseTrustedLastKnownEndpointAsFallback` cover the stale direct fallback removal.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the ViewModel `TrustedLastKnown` drop, regression names, and no-device summary string. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the regressions and reports the trusted route fallback addendum.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM/source/script evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical/camera QR scan reliability, physical device reconnect, real device haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-30 RuntimeDevServer Relay Rejected-Pairing Smoke Expansion

- Scope: no-device progress on roadmap item 6 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; the current work used a GPT-5.5 read-only subagent for roadmap-gap review.
- Result: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay` now sends a relay-routed `pairing.request` with an invalid pairing code before the valid pairing attempt and requires `pairing.result` with `accepted: false`, `code: "pairing_invalid"`, `retryable: true`, and bounded failed-attempt counters.
- Result: after the rejected pairing, the same smoke sends `hello` for that device id and requires `pairing_required`, proving the failed pairing did not create trust or an authenticated session.
- Result: the smoke then continues through accepted pairing, pre-auth runtime-command rejection, untrusted hello rejection, P-256 auth, relay `route.refresh`, `runtime.health`, model list/pull, streaming chat, document attachment chat, cancel, and trusted reconnect.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now pins the rejected-pairing smoke request IDs, rejection fields, and the no-device coverage summary so this E2E relay pairing rejection coverage cannot silently drop out.
- Caveat: this is no-device relay/runtime smoke evidence. It does not prove Android optical QR scanning, physical device reconnect, real device haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network reachability.

### 2026-06-30 Relay Route Material And Auth Replay Hardening

- Scope: no-device hardening for roadmap item 5 while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; one GPT-5.5 read-only subagent identified the Android route-token fallback gap and was closed after reporting.
- Result: Android core transport relay preparation no longer has a `routeTokenFallback` compatibility path. A prepared relay route now requires explicit `relay_id` route material, keeping `route_token` as paired-runtime routing metadata instead of allowing it to become the relay room id.
- Result: macOS runtime authentication tests now prove a previously accepted auth nonce cannot be replayed as a second `auth.response`, and that issuing a second `hello` supersedes the first challenge nonce before authentication.
- Result: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the macOS replay/superseded-nonce regressions, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the Android explicit-`relay_id` route-material contract plus the macOS auth replay tests.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM/source and macOS SwiftPM/source evidence. It does not implement the full production private overlay, NAT traversal, production session key exchange, or production replay protection for all future route records. It does not prove physical Android QR scanning, real device haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-29 Product QR Requires Remote Route

- Scope: no-device Android QR pairing hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: Android product QR entry points now require complete, unexpired remote-route material when a user scans or opens a pairing QR from the app UI. Identity-only QR payloads remain parser-compatible for explicit diagnostic/local-discovery tests, but they are no longer classified as valid product scanner results.
- Result: `RuntimeClientViewModel.trustRuntimeFromPairingQr(...)` now has an explicit `requireRemoteRoute` policy parameter, and `MainActivity` passes it for camera scans and app pairing deeplinks. This prevents a route-less QR from looking accepted and then failing later on unrelated networks.
- Result: scanner tests now prove route-bearing relay QR values are valid, identity-only QR values are invalid under the product scanner policy, and the diagnostic parser mode can still exercise identity-only QR behavior without implying different-network connectivity.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires the product route-required QR policy, the identity-only scanner regression, and the parser regression that maps route-less product QR entry to `pairing_endpoint_unavailable` / `route_diagnostic_remote_pending`.
- Caveat: this is Android JVM/source/script evidence only. It does not prove optical camera scanning, physical Android reconnect, live provider-backed chat/cancel, real public relay reachability, production NAT traversal, or arbitrary-network P2P connectivity. A working different-network run still needs a QR that carries route material both devices can reach.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.AppNavigationTest.pairingQrRawValueAcceptsCompactRelayPayloadsFromScanner --tests com.localagentbridge.android.AppNavigationTest.pairingQrScannerClassifiesRawValuesBeforeConsumingCameraResult --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.productPairingQrParserRejectsIdentityOnlyQrWhenRemoteRouteIsRequired --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.identityOnlyQrPlanStartsDiscoveryAndWaitsForRoute -Pkotlin.incremental=false`
- `python3 -m py_compile script/check_copy_hygiene.py`

### 2026-06-29 Physical External Relay Evidence Contract

- Scope: no-device QA hardening for the physical different-network relay gate while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: `script/check_physical_external_relay_pairing.sh` now treats endpoint reachability and route-level readiness as mandatory success evidence. A successful-looking physical run is converted to failure if the endpoint probe JSON is missing or does not report `probe.reachable=true`, or if the route-readiness probe JSON is missing or does not report `probe.route_ready=true`.
- Result: the wrapper summary now embeds the child probe summaries under `probe_summaries` and exposes `external_relay_probe_reachable` plus `external_relay_route_ready` in `coverage`, so later QA can distinguish "phone can reach the relay endpoint" from "the exact QR relay id has a waiting runtime".
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs a deterministic invalid-host wrapper check with `ADB=/bin/true`, asserting the summary has `success=false`, false endpoint and route-readiness coverage, explicit missing child probe summaries, and the no-direct-provider caveat. `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the stronger physical QA summary fields, shell-level route-readiness enforcement, and this behavior guard.
- Caveat: this is still no-device script/static evidence until a phone and mutually reachable external relay are available. It does not prove optical QR scanning, real Android reconnect, live provider-backed chat/cancel, production NAT traversal, or arbitrary-network P2P connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `bash -n script/check_physical_external_relay_pairing.sh script/android_relay_reachability_probe.sh script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh script/check_no_device_quality.sh`

- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`

### 2026-06-29 Physical Relay Route Probe QA Path

- Scope: no-device script/QA hardening for the same QR-only different-network path. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: `script/android_relay_reachability_probe.sh` now accepts `--relay-id <relay-id>`. With that argument, the connected Android device sends `AETHERLINK_RELAY probe <relay_id>` from its own network stack and requires the relay to return a known route with `runtime_waiting=1`.
- Result: `script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --external-relay-host ... --probe-external-relay-from-device` now performs two device-side checks: an early TCP endpoint probe before runtime startup, then a route-level relay-id readiness probe after RuntimeDevServer emits the QR route material and the runtime host is waiting on the relay.
- Result: `script/check_physical_external_relay_pairing.sh` records both endpoint reachability JSON and route-readiness JSON in its QA summary, so physical evidence can distinguish "relay endpoint reachable" from "QR relay id ready for pairing".
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the route-level physical probe command, `--relay-id`, and the physical QA summary artifact fields.
- Caveat: this is script/static evidence until a phone and a mutually reachable external relay are available. It still does not prove optical QR scanning, real Android reconnect, live provider-backed chat/cancel, production NAT traversal, or arbitrary-network P2P connectivity.

Verified after this change:

### 2026-06-29 Non-Consuming Relay Readiness Probe

- Scope: no-device remote-route hardening for QR-only different-network development paths. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; one GPT-5.5 worker implemented the Swift relay-side probe and one GPT-5.5 explorer performed Android test-impact review.
- Result: `AetherLinkRelay` now supports `AETHERLINK_RELAY probe <relay_id>`. The relay responds with `AETHERLINK_RELAY probe known=<0|1> runtime_waiting=<0|1>` and closes the socket without registering a peer, replacing a waiting runtime, matching a client, or forwarding payload bytes.
- Result: Android relay preflight now sends the non-consuming probe for the prepared relay route and proceeds only when the route is known and the runtime side is waiting. This is stronger than the previous host/port-only TCP preflight and avoids consuming the actual client relay slot before the real connection.
- Result: the Android preflight checker now receives the full prepared relay route, so tests pin relay id, frame secret, expiry nonce, host, and port rather than just host/port reachability.
- Guardrail: `RelayProbeTests` and `RelayMatcherTests.testRuntimeWaitingProbeDoesNotConsumePendingRuntime` pin the Swift relay probe parser, response, line framing, and non-consuming matcher behavior.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeResponseParserRequiresKnownRouteAndWaitingRuntime`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayQrPairingFailsBeforeConnectWhenDeviceCannotReachRelayRoute`, and `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustRuntimeFromPairingQrWithCompactRelayUriConnectsRelayAndSendsPairingRequest` pin Android probe parsing and route-level preflight behavior.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the relay probe regressions, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the probe contract and no-device coverage.
- Caveat: this is no-device Swift/Android source and unit-test evidence. It still does not prove optical QR scanning, physical Android reconnect, real public relay reachability, live provider-backed chat/cancel, production NAT traversal, or arbitrary-network P2P connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter RelayServerCoreTests`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustRuntimeFromPairingQrWithCompactRelayUriConnectsRelayAndSendsPairingRequest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayQrPairingFailsBeforeConnectWhenDeviceCannotReachRelayRoute --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustedRelayReconnectAttemptsRelayBeforeMatchingBonjourFallback --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayProbeResponseParserRequiresKnownRouteAndWaitingRuntime -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`

### 2026-06-29 Android Relay Handshake Rejection Retention

- Scope: no-device Android remote-route hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: `RuntimeRelayTcpClient` now has regression coverage for the case where the socket connects but the relay does not return `AETHERLINK_RELAY ready`; this fails as `Relay did not accept route` instead of hanging or being treated as a usable channel.
- Result: the app-level trusted-runtime path now has regression coverage that this relay handshake rejection surfaces as `remote_route_unreachable` / `route_diagnostic_relay_failed`, preserves the stored trusted relay host and secret, and does not enter an automatic reconnect loop.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeRelayTcpClientTest.relayConnectFailsWhenReadyLineRejectsRoute` pins the transport handshake rejection path.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustedRelayHandshakeRejectionKeepsStoredRelayAndStopsAutoReconnectUntilUserRetries` pins the app-state behavior for stored relay material after route acceptance failure.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs both relay handshake rejection regressions, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins that no-device gate coverage.
- Caveat: this is no-device Android JVM/source/script evidence. It does not prove optical QR scan, physical Android reconnect, real public relay reachability, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or production arbitrary-network P2P connectivity.

### 2026-06-29 Android Trusted Relay Route Retention On Transient Failure

- Scope: no-device Android route-state hardening while the Android phone is disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only QR/route audit completed and was closed after identifying follow-up work in macOS remote QR readiness, Android relay preflight fidelity, and relay wrong-secret rejection.
- Result: transient remote route failures no longer delete the saved trusted relay route. `remote_route_unreachable`, `remote_route_unreachable_from_device`, and `route_diagnostic_relay_failed` still stop automatic retry until the user retries or scans newer connection details, but they preserve the paired runtime identity and saved relay material instead of turning a temporary network failure into a lost route.
- Result: stored relay material is still discarded when the route is actually invalidated: relay frame authentication failure (`remote_route_auth_failed` / `route_diagnostic_relay_auth_failed`) and expired route material (`remote_route_expired` / `route_diagnostic_remote_route_expired`) keep the stricter fresh-QR path.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.routeRefreshQrKeepsUnreachableRelayRouteForRetryOrFreshQrRecovery` now verifies that a same-runtime route-refresh QR with a temporarily failing relay keeps the refreshed relay host, secret, nonce, and scope pinned while surfacing `remote_route_unreachable`.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustedRelayConnectionFailureKeepsStoredRelayAndStopsAutoReconnectUntilUserRetries` now verifies that a trusted saved relay route is not erased after a normal connection failure, while the existing relay-auth-failure and expired-route tests continue to cover route invalidation.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the route-refresh retention regression, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins that no-device coverage.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected for this pass, so this is source/unit/script evidence only. It does not prove optical QR scan, physical Android reconnect, real public relay reachability, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or production arbitrary-network P2P connectivity.

### 2026-06-29 Near-Expiry Relay Lease QR Renewal

- Scope: no-device macOS QR-only remote pairing readiness follow-up from a GPT-5.5 macOS audit. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used, and both audit agents were closed after reporting.
- Result: AetherLink Runtime no longer treats a relay route lease as QR-ready just because it has not expired yet. Pairing QR generation now requires the relay lease to remain fresh through the pairing window plus margin, so a QR shown for remote pairing is not populated with route material that is about to expire.
- Result: configured relay routes with near-expired service leases now refresh route material before the runtime emits the QR, and `route.refresh` uses the same freshness check before returning relay material to a trusted client.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelRegeneratesGUIAllocatedQRCodeWithNearExpiredLease` verifies that a near-expired relay lease is replaced by fresh `relay_id`, `relay_secret`, `relay_expires_at`, and `relay_nonce` before QR payload generation.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now pin the near-expiry remote relay lease QR renewal regression.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected for this pass, so this is no-device macOS SwiftPM/unit, script, and docs evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, optical/camera QR pairing, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network relay reachability.

### 2026-06-29 Clear Draft Attachment Cleanup And Route Copy Polish

- Scope: no-device product-polish follow-up from GPT-5.5 Android and macOS audits. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used, and both audit agents were closed after reporting.
- Result: Android `Clear draft` now clears the full composer draft, not only typed text. The production app shell routes the clear action through `RuntimeClientViewModel.clearChatDraft()`, which clears the active session's persisted text draft and pending attachment draft together.
- Result: Android shows `Clear draft` for editable non-streaming drafts with either text or attachments, so an attachment-only pending draft can be cleared in one action.
- Result: macOS Runtime Memory Inspector rows no longer show the same `Updated` timestamp twice. The row header keeps the status pill, and the metadata row remains the single visible place for Created/Updated timestamps.
- Result: macOS remote-route loopback guidance now says `this device or USB diagnostics` instead of the more host-centric `this runtime host or USB diagnostics`.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.clearChatDraftClearsActiveSessionTextAndPendingAttachments` verifies active-session draft text, persisted draft storage, and pending attachment draft cleanup.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.chatScreenClearDraftActionClearsComposerAndHidesWhileStreaming` now covers pending attachment cleanup from the clear action.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests/testRemoteRouteScopeCopyDistinguishesAutomaticRemoteAndLocalRoutes` now pins the loopback route copy and rejects the stale `runtime host` phrase for that surface.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected for this pass, so this is no-device Android JVM/Compose, macOS XCTest/render-smoke, resource, and script evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, physical TalkBack/VoiceOver traversal, Android system/per-app appearance or locale mutation on hardware, optical/camera QR pairing, real device haptics, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.ui.ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.chatScreenClearDraftActionClearsComposerAndHidesWhileStreaming --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.clearChatDraftClearsActiveSessionTextAndPendingAttachments -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter 'AetherLinkLocalizationTests/testRemoteRouteScopeCopyDistinguishesAutomaticRemoteAndLocalRoutes|AetherLinkRenderSmokeTests/testRuntimeMemoryInspectorRendersAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances'`

- Scope: product-polish follow-up from GPT-5.5 Android and macOS audits. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used, and both audit agents were closed after reporting.
- Result: Android now keeps pending composer attachments in an in-memory per-chat draft map when the user switches between existing chats. Text draft and file draft behavior now match during chat browsing: returning to a chat restores its unsent attachments, while send, message reuse, archive, delete, and explicit New Chat clearing still remove transient attachments.

- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsMemoryAddControlsLocalizeReadinessStateAcrossSupportedLanguages` now also verifies the Memory added live-region clears when a new memory draft starts.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests/testTrustedDeviceRowAccessibilityLabelUsesDeviceContext` now verifies five-language trusted-device display-name fallback as well as the row accessibility summary.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected for this pass, so this is no-device Android JVM/Compose, macOS XCTest/render-smoke, resource, and script evidence. ### 2026-06-29 No-Device Gate Stabilization

- Scope: no-device verification cleanup found while validating the macOS Runtime Memory inspector summary. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: three Android route-refresh ViewModel tests now advance the coroutine scheduler through the scheduled `route.refresh` renewal instead of reading sent envelopes before the renewal delay can fire.
- Result: those route-refresh tests explicitly clear the ViewModel before `Dispatchers.resetMain()`, preventing leftover Main-dispatcher work from leaking across Robolectric/JVM tests.
- Result: `settingsScreenPreferenceAndEmbeddingControlsInvokeCallbacks` now passes the existing Settings scroll-container test tag, matching the current expandable Settings layout.
- Verification scope: focused failing Android tests plus the full no-device quality gate.
- Caveat: this is test-harness and no-device evidence. It does not prove physical Android rendering, physical TalkBack traversal, optical QR pairing, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.routeRefreshAuthenticationRequiredDoesNotRetainRouteMaterialTechnicalDetail --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedRuntimeMarksRouteExpiredWhenRefreshErrorCannotRetryBeforeLeaseExpiry --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedRuntimeRetriesRejectedRouteRefreshPayloadBeforeLeaseExpiry --tests com.localagentbridge.android.ui.ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsScreenPreferenceAndEmbeddingControlsInvokeCallbacks -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`
- `bash script/check_no_device_quality.sh`

### 2026-06-29 macOS Runtime Memory Inspector Summary

- Scope: no-device macOS Runtime Memory inspector readability and localization hardening. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: `RuntimeMemoryInspectorSheet` now shows a compact saved/paused summary above non-empty memory-note rows, matching the higher-level Runtime Memory status card and Android Settings memory summary direction.
- Result: the summary exposes the total saved runtime-memory note count as the primary label and a localized enabled/paused detail line underneath.
- Result: the summary uses a localized accessibility label helper instead of raw interpolated visible text, so VoiceOver-facing copy stays covered by the five-language localization contract.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testLocalizedCountHelpersUseNaturalSingularAndPluralCopy` now covers the inspector summary accessibility label plus Japanese and Simplified Chinese runtime-memory card detail output.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkRenderSmokeTests.testRuntimeMemoryInspectorRendersAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` verifies the inspector renders across supported app languages and appearance modes.
- Guardrail: `script/check_macos_localization.py` now requires the inspector summary source wiring, localized accessibility helper, and focused localization-test snippets.
- Verification scope: no-device macOS SwiftPM XCTest, macOS render smoke, macOS localization parity, script syntax, and whitespace checks.
- Caveat: this does not prove live VoiceOver traversal, physical client rendering, optical QR pairing, production different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

### 2026-06-29 Android Provider Label Normalization

- Scope: Android provider-health UI polish for runtime-reported model provider names. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified this display-only improvement and was closed after reporting.
- Result: Android provider health rows now normalize known provider identifiers before displaying them, so runtime values such as `lm-studio` or `lm_studio` render as localized `LM Studio` instead of leaking raw internal ids.
- Result: provider row accessibility summaries, visible provider names, unavailable/ready details, and diagnostics show/hide labels all use the normalized localized provider label.
- Result: custom providers still preserve the runtime-provided display name when the provider id is not one of the known AetherLink provider ids.
- Result: this is display-only. Android still talks only to AetherLink Runtime and does not call Ollama, LM Studio, or future model providers directly.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.connectionStatusProviderRowsExposeLocalizedAccessibilitySummariesAcrossSupportedLanguages` now feeds raw provider name/id variants and asserts localized summaries across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.connectionStatusProviderDiagnosticsToggleExposesExpandedState` now verifies diagnostics toggle copy still says `LM Studio` when runtime sends `lm_studio`.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires provider label normalization source wiring, tests that reject raw `lm_studio` / `custom_provider` visible copy, and the no-device coverage label.
- Caveat: this proves Android source/test/script behavior. It does not prove physical TalkBack pronunciation, live provider failure payload variety, optical QR scanning, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or true different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `python3 script/check_android_string_parity.py`
- `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh` (syntax only)

### 2026-06-29 Android Drawer Chat Date Groups

- Scope: Android ChatGPT-like navigation drawer history browsing. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer audited the drawer grouping scope and was closed after reporting.
- Result: previous chats in the Android drawer are now grouped by local calendar day: `Today`, `Yesterday`, `Previous 7 days`, and `Older`.
- Result: grouping happens after the existing drawer search filter, so search results stay focused and only visible sessions are bucketed.
- Result: date bucket headings are localized in English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French, and each heading exposes heading semantics for accessibility traversal.
- Result: the grouping helper uses local calendar day starts instead of elapsed milliseconds, avoiding off-by-one behavior around local midnight and daylight-saving boundaries.
- Result: archive from the drawer now uses the lighter selection-change haptic policy because archive is reversible; rename remains a primary action.
- Guardrail: `AppNavigationTest.chatSessionDrawerGroupLabelUsesLocalCalendarDays` verifies today/yesterday/previous-seven-days/older bucket boundaries.
- Guardrail: `AppNavigationTest.chatSessionDrawerGroupsUseStableBucketOrderAndPreserveOrderInsideBuckets` verifies stable bucket order and preserves session order inside each bucket.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.navigationDrawerGroupsPreviousChatsByLocalDateAcrossSupportedLanguages` verifies localized drawer group headings across the five launch languages.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now require the drawer date grouping source, tests, resources, and coverage summary label.
- Physical device evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb devices -l` reported `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device`, model `SM_S936N`.
- Build/install evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleDebug --console=plain` passed, then `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM install -r apps/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` returned `Success`.
- Launch evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM shell am start -W -n com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` started the app, `pidof` returned a live process, and `dumpsys window` reported `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` as focused.
- Screenshot evidence: `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-drawer-date-groups.png`, `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-drawer-date-groups-ui.xml`, `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-drawer-open-date-groups.png`, and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-drawer-open-date-groups-ui.xml` captured the installed app and opened drawer. The drawer capture shows the `Today` group above the visible previous chat.
- Caveat: this proves source, localized no-device Compose behavior, aggregate quality gates, physical install/launch, and a physical drawer capture. It does not prove physical TalkBack announcement timing, real device haptic feel, optical QR scanning, live provider-backed chat/cancel, or true different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-29 Android Streaming Thinking Collapsed By Default

- Scope: Android Chat assistant reasoning/think UX alignment with the requested Ollama-style behavior. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer audited the affected UI/tests/docs and was closed after reporting stale guard locations.
- Result: actively streaming assistant reasoning now stays collapsed by default instead of auto-expanding when `RuntimeChatMessage.isReasoningOpen` is true.
- Result: the visible thinking panel remains muted and short by default, capped to the existing compact preview, while the user can expand it on demand to see full thinking text.
- Result: actively open reasoning streams still expose `LiveRegionMode.Polite`, so assistive technology can receive thinking updates without mixing them into the final answer body or showing the generic assistant typing placeholder.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.chatScreenKeepsOpenStreamingReasoningCollapsedUntilExpanded` verifies streaming reasoning starts collapsed with `Show thinking`, exposes the polite live region, suppresses the generic typing placeholder, and expands to full text only after user action.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now require the collapsed live-region policy instead of the older initial-expansion policy.
- Verification scope: focused no-device Android reasoning tests plus Android copy/docs/string hygiene, followed by connected physical Android build, install, launch, screenshot, and UI dump evidence.
- Physical device evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb devices -l` reported `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device`, model `SM_S936N`.
- Build/install evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleDebug --console=plain` passed, then `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM install -r apps/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` returned `Success`.
- Launch evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM shell am start -W -n com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` started the app, `pidof` returned a live process, and `dumpsys window` reported `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` as focused.
- Screenshot evidence: `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-thinking-collapsed-policy.png` and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-thinking-collapsed-policy-ui.xml` captured the installed app after launch in the latest-QR recovery state.
- Caveat: this proves UI policy and accessibility semantics in no-device Compose tests. It does not prove physical TalkBack announcement timing, real model reasoning output quality, physical haptic feel, live provider streaming/cancel, optical QR scanning, or true different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-29 Android Settings Chat History Rename Action

- Scope: Settings > Chat history per-chat title editing for active and archived runtime-owned conversations. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer audited the chat-history UI path and was closed before implementation.
- Result: Settings > Chat history rows now expose a localized `Rename chat` edit action for both active and archived chats. The action opens the existing rename dialog with the selected session title.
- Result: archived chats can be renamed without restoring them, and the persisted archived state remains unchanged after rename.
- Result: the app-level rename flow now resolves the target session from both active and archived runtime-owned chat lists, so archived-row rename uses the same trimming and manual-title logic as active-row rename.
- Result: the rename action participates in the same streaming guard as other per-chat history actions, with disabled state copy and accessibility semantics while generation is active.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsChatHistoryRowsExposeRenameActionForActiveAndArchivedChats` verifies active and archived row rename callbacks plus haptic feedback.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsPerChatHistoryActionsExplainStreamingDisabledStateAcrossSupportedLanguages` now covers rename-action disabled semantics across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.renamedArchivedChatSessionKeepsArchiveState` verifies renaming an archived session does not move it back to active history.
- Verification scope: focused no-device Android unit/Compose tests, Android string parity, copy hygiene, docs hygiene, diff hygiene, then connected physical Android build, install, launch, screenshot, and UI dump evidence.
- Physical device evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb devices -l` reported `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device`, model `SM_S936N`.
- Build/install evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleDebug --console=plain` passed, then `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM install -r apps/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` returned `Success`.
- Launch evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM shell am start -W -n com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` started the app, `pidof` returned a live process, and `dumpsys window` reported `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` as focused.

- Caveat: this proves implementation, tests, build, install, and launch. It does not prove a manual physical tap on the Settings rename action, physical TalkBack traversal, optical QR scanning, production relay allocation, true QR-only different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

### 2026-06-29 Android Relay QR Device-Reachability Preflight

- Scope: different-network QR pairing failure clarity and route safety. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer audited the QR/pairing/relay path and was closed after reporting.
- Result: Android now performs a short device-network TCP preflight against the QR-provisioned relay host and port before opening the relay transport. If the phone cannot reach that relay route, the app stops before `pairing.request` and reports `remote_route_unreachable_from_device` with diagnostic `route_diagnostic_relay_unreachable_from_device`.
- Result: failed relay QR pairing clears the pending pairing route instead of retrying a route that the device network cannot open. Trusted reconnect failures from the same preflight strip stale saved relay route material and require a fresh QR route.
- Result: Chat empty-state route recovery, route availability notices, runtime error mapping, and QR refresh prompts distinguish "saved relay did not answer" from "this device cannot reach the QR relay route."
- Result: macOS pairing QR ready copy now says that pairing or refresh still requires the scanning device to reach the route, so runtime-side relay readiness is not confused with phone-network reachability.
- Localization: added English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French Android copy for the new device-reachability route failure. Updated macOS English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French QR-ready copy.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayQrPairingFailsBeforeConnectWhenDeviceCannotReachRelayRoute` verifies the relay connector is not invoked when the device-reachability preflight fails.
- Guardrail: `AppNavigationTest.routeAvailabilityNoticeUsesDeviceReachabilityDiagnosticForRelayQrPreflightFailure` verifies route notices use the device-reachability diagnostic.
- Guardrail: `AppNavigationTest.emptyChatPrefersQrRefreshWhenDeviceCannotReachRelayQrRoute` verifies the empty chat surface points the user back to a fresh reachable QR route instead of showing a generic bottom error.
- Verification scope: no-device focused Android ViewModel/UI/resource tests plus Android/macOS localization and copy hygiene checks, followed by connected physical Android build, install, launch, screenshot, and UI dump evidence.
- Physical device evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb devices -l` reported `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device`, model `SM_S936N`.
- Build/install evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleDebug -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain` passed, then `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM install -r apps/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` returned `Success`.
- Launch evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM shell am start -S -n com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` started the app, `pidof` returned a live process, and `dumpsys window` reported `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` as focused.
- Screenshot evidence: `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-relay-preflight.png` and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-relay-preflight-ui.xml` captured the installed app after launch.
- Caveat: this improves failure detection and copy for QR-provisioned relay routes. It does not create a production public relay, production rendezvous fabric, P2P NAT traversal, physical camera QR proof, or real different-network acceptance run by itself.

### 2026-06-29 Android QR Scanner Invalid-Code Recovery

- Scope: Android optical QR scanner behavior when the camera sees a non-AetherLink code, malformed AetherLink pairing URI, or expired route QR. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer audited the scanner-to-pairing path and was closed after reporting.
- Result: the CameraX/ML Kit scanner no longer consumes and closes on a loose `aetherlink://pair` candidate. It now classifies each barcode as a valid AetherLink pairing QR, invalid/expired AetherLink QR, or unsupported QR before closing the scanner.
- Result: only fully accepted AetherLink pairing QR values are passed to `handlePairingQr`. Invalid or expired AetherLink QR values keep the camera open and show an inline localized recovery message asking for the latest Runtime QR.
- Result: non-AetherLink QR values keep the camera open and show a localized inline message that the scanned code is not an AetherLink Runtime QR.
- Localization: added English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French Android scanner feedback strings.
- Guardrail: `AppNavigationTest.pairingQrScannerClassifiesRawValuesBeforeConsumingCameraResult` verifies valid, malformed, expired, and unsupported raw QR values are classified before the camera result is consumed.
- Guardrail: `PairingQrScannerChromeNoDeviceComposeTest.scannerChromeShowsInvalidQrFeedbackWithoutClosingScanner` verifies invalid/unsupported feedback renders across supported languages while the camera preview, scan target, close, and cancel actions stay available.
- Physical device evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb devices -l` reported `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device`, model `SM_S936N`.
- Build/install evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleDebug -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain` passed, then `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM install -r apps/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` returned `Success`.
- Launch evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM shell am start -S -n com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` started the app, `pidof` returned a live process, and `dumpsys window` reported `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` as focused.
- Screenshot evidence: `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-qr-scanner-invalid-code-recovery.png` and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-qr-scanner-invalid-code-recovery-ui.xml` captured the installed app after launch.
- Verification scope: no-device Android scanner UI/resource/unit evidence plus connected physical Android build, install, launch, screenshot, and UI dump evidence.
- Caveat: this improves optical scanner recovery behavior but does not prove an actual physical camera scan of a QR, production relay allocation, true QR-only different-network routing, physical TalkBack traversal, physical haptic feel, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

### 2026-06-29 Android Settings Chat History Open Action

- Scope: no-device Android Settings chat-history usability for opening runtime-owned previous chats from Settings. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Result: Settings > Chat history active rows now expose a localized `Open chat` action next to `Archive`. The action selects the existing runtime-owned session and returns the app to the Chat screen instead of forcing the user to find the same chat only through the drawer.
- Result: archived chat rows intentionally do not expose `Open chat`; archived rows keep the restore/delete-only lifecycle so archived sessions stay out of active chat and memory flows until restored.
- Result: the active-row open action has localized accessibility content descriptions, localized click labels, disabled state copy while streaming, and lightweight haptic feedback.
- Localization: added English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French Android strings for the chat-history open action.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsChatHistoryActiveRowCanOpenChatWithHapticFeedback` verifies the active-row open callback, haptic feedback, and absence of the open action on archived rows.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsPerChatHistoryActionsExplainStreamingDisabledStateAcrossSupportedLanguages` now also covers the open-chat action disabled state across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Physical device evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb devices -l` reported `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device`, model `SM_S936N`.

### 2026-06-29 macOS Runtime History Transcript Preview

- Scope: macOS runtime-host read-only transcript visibility inside the existing Runtime History Inspector. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer inspected the runtime chat transcript APIs and was closed after reporting.
- Result: `RuntimeChatEventStore` now exposes `listAllMessages(sessionID:limit:)` for runtime-host inspection of stored transcript messages across trusted-device owners, while the existing owner-scoped `listMessages(ownerDeviceID:sessionID:limit:)` remains the authenticated client boundary.
- Result: `CompanionAppModel` now publishes per-session `runtimeChatTranscriptMessages` and `runtimeChatTranscriptErrors`, and `refreshRuntimeChatTranscriptPreview(sessionID:limit:)` reads the all-owner store path without mutating chat sessions.
- Result: Status > Quick Actions > `Inspect Runtime History` now shows a two-pane read-only inspector: runtime-owned sessions on the left and a transcript preview on the right. The preview shows stored user/assistant/system-visible messages, timestamps, and muted three-line reasoning snippets when assistant reasoning exists.
- Localization: added English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French macOS copy for transcript preview actions, empty states, warning state, role labels, empty-message fallback, and reasoning label.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionAppModelPublishesRuntimeHistoryTranscriptPreviewAcrossOwners` verifies the macOS app model can preview owner-scoped stored messages and assistant reasoning through the all-owner host-inspection path while the owner-scoped convenience read remains empty.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionAppModelPublishesRuntimeHistoryInspectorError` now also verifies per-session transcript preview read failures surface through `runtimeChatTranscriptErrors`.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkRenderSmokeTests.testRuntimeHistoryInspectorRendersAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` now renders the inspector with transcript preview content and reasoning across supported macOS languages and appearance modes.
- Verification scope: no-device macOS store/model/UI render/localization and hygiene checks only.
- Caveat: this adds host-side transcript preview only. It does not add transcript editing, physical Android interaction, optical QR scanning, production different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

- Launch evidence: `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb -s R3CXC0M76VM shell am start -S -n com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` started the app, `pidof` returned a live app process, and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-connected-current.png` captured the latest-QR route-recovery state.

### 2026-06-29 macOS Runtime History Inspector

- Scope: macOS runtime-host visibility for runtime-owned chat history. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer inspected the runtime chat store, Status surface, localization, and tests, then was closed after reporting.
- Result: `CompanionAppModel` now publishes read-only `runtimeChatSessions` and `runtimeChatSessionsError` from `RuntimeChatEventStore.listAllSessions(limit:includeArchived:)`, sorted by most recent activity first.
- Result: `refreshRuntimeDataSummary()` now refreshes the runtime chat session cache while keeping active/archive counts aligned with the same all-owner runtime-host store.
- Result: Status > Quick Actions now includes `Inspect Runtime History`, which refreshes runtime-owned chat sessions and opens a read-only `RuntimeHistoryInspectorSheet` with active/archived state, model id, message count, last event, last error, updated time, empty state, warning state, and accessibility labels.

- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionAppModelPublishesRuntimeDataSummaryFromInjectedStores` now verifies runtime chat-history inspector sessions are exposed from owner-scoped store rows and sorted by latest activity.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionAppModelPublishesRuntimeHistoryInspectorError` verifies chat-store read failures surface through `runtimeChatSessionsError` without inventing sessions.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkRenderSmokeTests.testRuntimeHistoryInspectorRendersAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` verifies the history inspector sheet renders meaningfully across supported macOS languages and appearance modes.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testRuntimeHistoryInspectorCopyLocalizesAcrossSupportedLanguages` verifies the new inspector copy, message-count helper, status helper, event helper, and accessibility formatter across supported languages.
