### 2026-06-29 Android Chat History Manual Runtime Refresh

- Scope: Android Settings > Chat history data consistency for runtime-owned chat sessions. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; the prior GPT-5.5 read-only explorer was closed before this implementation pass.
- Result: Settings > Chat history now exposes a localized refresh icon action that explicitly re-requests `chat.sessions.list` from the authenticated runtime host. This gives the client a manual repair path when the runtime host is the source of truth for chat history and another device or runtime-side mutation may have changed the list.
- Result: `RuntimeClientViewModel.refreshRuntimeChatHistory()` blocks while a response is streaming, requires an authenticated connected trusted runtime, and reuses the existing pending `chat.sessions.list` guard so repeated taps do not send duplicate history-list frames.
- Result: the Android chat-history refresh action exposes localized content descriptions and state descriptions across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.refreshRuntimeChatHistoryRequestsFreshListAfterPendingListCompletes` verifies that a pending chat-history list is not duplicated, a manual refresh sends a fresh `chat.sessions.list` after the prior request completes, the request includes archived sessions, and runtime-owned message bodies remain redacted from device storage.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsChatHistoryRefreshActionFollowsConnectionStateAcrossSupportedLanguages` verifies the localized refresh action, enabled state, disconnected reason, and streaming lockout reason across all supported Android UI languages.
- Physical device evidence: the connected `SM-S936N` / `R3CXC0M76VM` device was authorized, the debug APK installed successfully after this change, `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` was focused after direct launch, and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-chat-history-refresh.png` captured the running app.
- Caveat: this pass proves no-device Android UI/ViewModel behavior plus physical install and launch. It does not prove physical tap feel, physical TalkBack traversal, optical QR scanning, production different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

### 2026-06-29 Android Runtime Chat Mutation Error Resync

- Scope: Android runtime-owned chat history consistency when a server-side rename, archive, restore, or delete request fails. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified the bounded failure-reconciliation gap and was closed after review.
- Problem fixed: Android optimistically updated runtime-owned chat sessions before the runtime host confirmed the mutation. If the runtime returned `error` or the send failed, the UI could keep a rejected title/archive/delete state because the client did not consistently request a fresh `chat.sessions.list`.
- Result: runtime-owned chat session lifecycle and rename requests now keep request-scoped recovery metadata. On error or send failure, Android surfaces `chat_session_sync_failed`, re-requests `chat.sessions.list`, and lets the runtime host re-establish the authoritative session state.
- Result: failed rename now clears the local manual-title override before resync so the runtime title can replace the rejected local title. Failed delete now removes the local runtime-session suppression tombstone before resync so an archived runtime session can reappear if the runtime rejected deletion.
- Result: the real relay integration test fixture no longer assumes `route.refresh` arrives before `runtime.health`; the fake runtime now reads both messages before releasing the connection, and the test waits for `runtimeStatus == ok` before asserting health.
- Guardrail: `RuntimeClientChatSessionMutationFailureTest` verifies rejected runtime rename, archive, and delete flows, including fresh `chat.sessions.list` requests and restoration from server-provided summaries.
- Physical device evidence: the connected `SM-S936N` / `R3CXC0M76VM` device was authorized, the debug APK installed successfully after this change, `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` was focused after direct launch, and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-runtime-chat-mutation-resync.png` captured the running app.
- Caveat: this pass proves no-device ViewModel/protocol behavior plus physical install and launch. It does not prove a live runtime host rejecting a real mutation, physical Android tap flow, optical QR scanning, production different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

### 2026-06-29 Android Memory Disconnected-Session Read-Only Copy

- Scope: Android settings Memory copy accuracy when a trusted runtime is disconnected but memory entries are still visible in the current UI session. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified the bounded copy/test gap and was closed after review.
- Problem fixed: the old copy said "Cached memory is read-only until the trusted runtime reconnects," but Android does not persist memory entries into the disk snapshot. That wording could imply cold-start cached memory exists when the actual behavior is current-session-only visibility after disconnect.
- Result: `memory_read_only_notice` now explains that memory shown in the current session is read-only, and that reconnecting the trusted runtime is required to refresh or manage it.
- Localization: updated English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French Android strings for the disconnected-session read-only notice.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsMemoryDisconnectedSessionEntriesExplainReadOnlyStateAcrossSupportedLanguages` verifies the localized notice plus disabled add, pause, and remove controls across all supported Android UI languages.
- Physical device evidence: the connected `SM-S936N` / `R3CXC0M76VM` device was authorized, the debug APK installed successfully, `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity` was focused after direct launch, and `/tmp/aetherlink-phone-current.png` captured the running app.
- Caveat: this pass proves targeted no-device UI behavior plus physical install/launch only. It does not prove optical camera QR scanning, production different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat.

### 2026-06-29 macOS Runtime Data All-Owner Summary

- Scope: macOS runtime-host data accuracy for the existing `Runtime History` and `Runtime Memory` status cards. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified the owner-scoping bug and was closed after review.
- Problem fixed: `CompanionAppModel.refreshRuntimeDataSummary()` was using owner-scoped legacy queries with `ownerDeviceID: nil`, which means "legacy unscoped data" in the runtime stores rather than "all trusted devices." That could make the macOS status cards miss device-owned chat sessions and memory entries.
- Result: `RuntimeChatEventStore` now exposes `listAllSessions(limit:includeArchived:)`, and `RuntimeMemoryStore` now exposes `listAll()`. These all-owner APIs are used only by the app shell summary, while authenticated protocol routes continue using owner-scoped queries.
- Result: `CompanionAppModel.refreshRuntimeDataSummary()` now counts active chats, archived chats, enabled memory, and paused memory across all runtime-owned event rows, including device-scoped rows.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionAppModelPublishesRuntimeDataSummaryFromInjectedStores` now uses JSONL chat and memory stores with `ownerDeviceID: "device-a"` rows and verifies that legacy unscoped queries stay empty while the app model summary still counts the scoped data.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires the explicit all-owner store APIs and rejects `CompanionAppModel` falling back to `runtimeChatEventStore.listSessions(...)` for runtime data summary.
- Verification type: no-device macOS Swift/store/source/script evidence only.
- Aggregate evidence: the no-device gate passed with log `/tmp/aetherlink-no-device-macos-runtime-data-all-owner-summary.log`, ended with `No-device quality checks passed.`, and included `macOS runtime data all-owner summary`.
- Caveat: this does not prove live production client sync, optical QR scanning, physical Android haptics, production different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelPublishesRuntimeDataSummaryFromInjectedStores`
- `swift test --filter LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests`
- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `git diff --check`
- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`
- `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh && git diff --check`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/check_no_device_quality.sh > /tmp/aetherlink-no-device-macos-runtime-data-all-owner-summary.log 2>&1`

- Result: `SuggestedQuestions` now shows the localized `Generating next questions...` progress/live-region row whenever `isLoadingSuggestions` is true, even if existing suggestion chips are still visible from the previous runtime result.
- Result: existing suggestion chips remain visible and actionable while the runtime is generating a refreshed set, so the chat surface no longer looks idle during suggestion refresh/retry.

- Aggregate evidence: the no-device gate passed with log `/tmp/aetherlink-no-device-suggested-questions-existing-chips.log`, ended with `No-device quality checks passed.`, and included `Android generating suggestions with existing chips accessibility`.
- Caveat: this does not prove physical Android install, physical TalkBack traversal, real device haptics, camera QR scanning, physical/live-backend streamed chat/cancel, or production different-network connectivity.

- Scope: physical Android device verification after the phone was reconnected. No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used.

- Install evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew :app:installDebug` passed and installed `app-debug.apk` on the connected phone.
- Launch evidence: `adb shell monkey -p com.localagentbridge.android -c android.intent.category.LAUNCHER 1` launched `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity`; `adb shell pidof com.localagentbridge.android` returned a running process id.

- Pairing smoke evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --relay --skip-install` passed in relay mode. The script injected an `aetherlink://pair` VIEW intent and verified the runtime received pairing and health traffic.
- Strong physical smoke evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --relay --skip-install --expect-reconnect --expect-chat-cancel` passed in relay mode with workdir `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.GK46yg`.
- Result: the physical-device smoke observed `chat.send`, streamed `chat.delta`, `chat.cancel`, and `chat.done` through the Android UI, then force-stopped and relaunched the app without clearing data and observed a second `runtime.health` through the saved trusted route.
- Verification type: physical Android device and development-relay evidence, not no-device evidence.
- Caveat: this still uses development tooling: QR delivery is injected through Android's VIEW intent, and the relay path is mapped through ADB reverse. It proves the installed debug app, relay pairing path, saved-route reconnect, and chat/cancel UI wiring on the physical phone, but not optical camera QR capture, production public relay allocation, P2P NAT traversal, or a production different-network route without debug tooling.

### 2026-06-28 macOS Runtime Data Status Cards

- Scope: macOS runtime-host visibility for runtime-owned chat history and memory. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer identified the product gap and was closed after review.
- Result: `CompanionAppModel` now owns and injects the same runtime chat event store and runtime memory store used by `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter`, so the app shell can summarize runtime-owned data without reading a separate hidden store.
- Result: Status now shows `Runtime History` and `Runtime Memory` cards with active chat, archived chat, enabled memory note, and paused memory note counts. These cards make it visible that AetherLink Runtime is the source of truth for chat and memory storage.
- Result: Quick Actions now includes `Refresh Runtime Data`, which refreshes the runtime-owned chat history and memory counts without requiring a client app or logs.
- Localization: added English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French strings for the new runtime data cards, refresh action, and natural singular/plural count helpers.
- Guardrail: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testCompanionAppModelPublishesRuntimeDataSummaryFromInjectedStores` verifies that `CompanionAppModel` reads injected runtime chat and memory stores.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testLocalizedCountHelpersUseNaturalSingularAndPluralCopy` now covers runtime data count copy, and `AetherLinkRenderSmokeTests.testPrimaryCompanionSurfacesRenderAtMinimumDetailSizeAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` covers the expanded Status surface across supported languages and appearances.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now names `macOS runtime data status cards` in its coverage summary, and `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` requires that phrase.
- Verification type: no-device macOS model/store, localization, render, source, and script evidence only.
- Caveat: this does not prove live production client sync, optical QR scanning, physical Android haptics, production different-network routing, or live provider-backed chat/cancel.

### 2026-06-28 Physical Relay Reconnect Stability

- Scope: development relay reconnect stability after the physical Android relay smoke showed a false-positive reconnect. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 explorer independently reviewed the focused relay artifact behavior.
- Result: `RuntimeDevServer` no longer stops the previous route-refresh `RelayPeerClient` when a trusted client asks for `route.refresh`. The development refresher now retains refreshed relay clients while their bridged connection is active and removes each retained client on disconnect.
- Result: the Android physical pairing smoke now treats reconnect as a stronger condition: after app relaunch it waits for a second `runtime.health`, a post-relaunch `models.list`, and verifies the final UI did not fall back to the latest-QR route recovery state.
- Result: the strengthened smoke still verifies `chat.send`, streamed `chat.delta`, `chat.cancel`, and `chat.done` through the runtime path when `--expect-chat-cancel` is used.
- Verification type: mixed physical-device and no-device evidence. The core reconnect behavior was verified by physical-device development relay smoke artifacts; build, shell, documentation, and hygiene checks remain no-device guardrails.
- Physical-device evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --relay --expect-reconnect --expect-chat-cancel` passed with workdir `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.l1w5SG`.
- Artifact evidence: `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.l1w5SG/runtime.log` shows `route.refresh`, the second `runtime.health`, post-relaunch `models.list`, `chat.cancel`, `chat.done`, and no `relay status=stopped`. `/var/folders/n2/vgx0vf052yl248_8cff1xt2r0000gn/T//aetherlink-android-pairing.l1w5SG/aetherlink-pairing-smoke.png` shows the connected chat surface with `Dev Mock Streaming`, the physical cancel-smoke message, assistant `Mock`, and next-question generation instead of latest-QR recovery.
- Caveat: this is still a development smoke. It injects the QR result as an `aetherlink://pair` deeplink and uses the local development relay path with the connected phone available through ADB. It does not prove optical camera QR scanning, a production public relay, P2P NAT traversal, or a real different-network route without USB/debug tooling.

### 2026-06-28 Android Empty-State Latest-QR Composer Alignment

- Scope: Android route-recovery UI alignment after a physical-device screenshot showed a disconnected trusted runtime with no connectable route. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used; a GPT-5.5 worker implemented the bounded Android UI/test patch and was closed after review.
- Result: `shouldScanLatestQrFromEmptyChat` now treats a trusted runtime with no connectable route as a latest-QR recovery state. This keeps the center empty state, primary action, and bottom composer hint aligned instead of showing `Connect to continue` while the composer asks for a fresh QR.
- Result: the `Connect` empty-state action is still preserved when the trusted runtime has usable route material, such as a non-expired relay route candidate.
- Guardrail: `AppNavigationTest.emptyChatPrefersLatestQrWhenTrustedRuntimeHasNoConnectableRoute`, `AppNavigationTest.emptyChatKeepsConnectActionWhenTrustedRuntimeHasConnectableRoute`, and `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.chatScreenTrustedRuntimeWithoutConnectableRouteShowsLatestQrEmptyState` cover the no-route and connectable-route branches.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` requires the QR-first branch and the Compose regression. `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now runs the focused Compose regression and reports `Android empty-state latest-QR composer alignment`.
- Verification type: mixed no-device and physical-device evidence. The behavior is covered by no-device JVM/Compose tests and also installed/launched/screenshot-checked on the physical Android phone.
- Physical device: `adb devices -l` saw `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device` with model `SM_S936N`. `adb install -r apps/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` returned `Success`, the launcher event opened `com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity`, and `/tmp/aetherlink-device-latest-qr-aligned.png` shows the center empty state and composer both asking to scan the latest QR.
- Caveat: this proves build, install, foreground launch, and the visible physical-device empty-state/composer alignment. It does not prove physical QR camera pairing, actual different-network runtime connectivity, physical TalkBack traversal, real device haptic feel, or physical/live-backend streamed chat/cancel.

Verified after this change:

### 2026-06-28 Android Trusted Runtime Forget Confirmation Named Message And Device Install

- Scope: Android trusted-runtime destructive confirmation polish plus physical-device install verification; GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Used one GPT-5.5 read-only explorer to validate that this was a narrow product-polish gap, then closed the agent after review.
- Result: the trusted-runtime forget confirmation body now names the runtime being removed instead of showing a generic saved-runtime message. The same localized body is also exposed as an accessibility description for the dialog body.
- Result: the message copy was shortened so it fits small Android dialog surfaces while preserving the destructive action context and the need to pair again for model access.
- Localization: updated the trusted-runtime forget confirmation body across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French with a `%1$s` runtime-name placeholder.
- Guardrail: `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsTrustedRuntimeForgetRequiresConfirmation` verifies the English visible dialog body and haptic timing. `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsTrustedRuntimeForgetActionNamesRuntimeAcrossSupportedLanguages` verifies localized runtime-named body accessibility and confirm/cancel action semantics across all supported Android app languages.
- Guardrail: `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now require `Android trusted-runtime forget confirmation named message`.
- Physical device: `adb devices -l` saw `R3CXC0M76VM` as `device` with model `SM_S936N`. `adb install -r apps/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` returned `Success`, `pm path com.localagentbridge.android` returned the installed base APK path, `monkey -p com.localagentbridge.android -c android.intent.category.LAUNCHER 1` injected the launcher event, and `dumpsys window` showed `mCurrentFocus=Window{... com.localagentbridge.android/com.localagentbridge.android.MainActivity}`.
- Caveat: this proves build, install, and foreground launch on the physical Android phone. It does not prove physical QR camera pairing, physical TalkBack traversal, real device haptic feel, physical/live-backend streamed chat/cancel, or actual different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

### 2026-06-28 macOS Activity List And Android Trusted Runtime Action Labels

- Scope: no-device UI/accessibility polish across the macOS Activity panel and Android Settings trusted-runtime section; GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Used two GPT-5.5 read-only explorers to identify small, non-overlapping Android/macOS accessibility gaps, then closed both agents and implemented the patches directly.
- Result: the macOS Activity list now exposes a localized list-level accessibility label and count value, so VoiceOver users can hear `Activity log` plus the number of activity items before navigating individual rows.
- Result: the Android first-step `Forget` action for a trusted runtime now exposes the same localized runtime-specific label as both its content description and explicit accessibility click action label, matching the existing confirm/cancel dialog actions.
- Guardrail: `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testActivityLogListAccessibilitySummaryUsesSelectedLanguage` covers Activity list summary copy across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French. `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsTrustedRuntimeForgetActionNamesRuntimeAcrossSupportedLanguages` now asserts the initial trusted-runtime forget action click label across the same five Android locales.
- Guardrail: `script/check_macos_localization.py`, `script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` now require the `macOS Activity log list accessibility summary` and `Android trusted-runtime forget named click label` coverage labels.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected for this pass, so this is no-device SwiftUI/Compose/source/XCTest/Robolectric/script evidence only. It does not prove rendered VoiceOver or TalkBack traversal, physical Android install, camera QR scanning, real device haptics, live different-network routing, physical reconnect after pairing, or live streamed chat/cancel.

### 2026-06-28 Streaming Route Lockout And Runtime Chat Semantic Validation

- Continued under the no-Spark constraint. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Used GPT-5.5 workers for Android and macOS scoped patches, then reviewed and connected the changes to the no-device quality gate in this session.
- Android now blocks user-triggered route, trust, pairing, discovery, connect/disconnect, runtime health refresh, and model refresh mutations while a response is streaming. Internal restore/pairing discovery paths still use private helpers so startup recovery and pending QR reconnects are not broken.
- `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.streamingBlocksRuntimeRouteTrustAndConnectionMutations` verifies those calls preserve endpoint fields, pairing code, streaming state, active request id, trusted runtime state, persisted auto-reconnect state, pending pairing route state, and outbound envelope count while surfacing `generation_in_progress`.
- macOS runtime chat history now validates decoded JSONL event semantics during replay. Blank event/request/session ids, blank request message roles, empty request messages, empty titles/deltas, and malformed error payloads are reported as `RuntimeChatEventStoreError.corruptEventLog(line:reason:)` instead of silently producing partial history.
- `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testRuntimeChatHistorySemanticallyInvalidEventReturnsStructuredError` verifies semantically corrupt chat history returns structured `chat_store_unavailable` errors for both session and message history requests without leaking valid prior transcript content.
- Strengthened `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` so Android streaming route/trust lockout and macOS runtime chat semantic corruption visibility cannot silently fall out of the no-device gate.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected for this pass, so this is no-device source/JVM/Swift/script evidence only. It does not prove physical install, camera QR scanning, physical haptics, live TalkBack or VoiceOver output, live streamed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-28 Android Open Reasoning Collapsed Live Region

- Continued no-device Android Chat reasoning polish while the Android phone was disconnected.
- Updated the assistant reasoning panel so an actively open runtime reasoning stream stays collapsed by default, even when `RuntimeChatMessage.isReasoningOpen` is true.
- Preserved the polite live-region semantic on actively open reasoning streams so assistive technology can notice streamed thinking updates separately from the final answer.
- Preserved the Ollama-style visible policy for all reasoning: assistant thinking remains muted, collapsed to the compact preview, and expandable on demand.
- Preserved user control after first render. If the user manually expands a reasoning panel, the local choice reveals the full thinking text instead of being overwritten by later recomposition.
- Added `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.chatScreenKeepsOpenStreamingReasoningCollapsedUntilExpanded`, proving open streaming reasoning renders the short preview, exposes the localized collapsed state/action, exposes a polite live region, suppresses the generic assistant typing placeholder while reasoning is open, and expands to the full text only after user action.
- Strengthened `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and the default no-device quality summary so Android open reasoning collapsed live-region accessibility cannot silently regress.

### 2026-06-27 Android Relay Route Failure Cleanup And QR Relay Smoke

- Fixed Android trusted-runtime reconnect handling so a saved relay route that cannot be reached from the current network is treated as stale route material, not as a route to retry indefinitely.
- When an initial trusted relay connection fails with `remote_route_unreachable` / `route_diagnostic_relay_failed`, the client now keeps the trusted runtime identity but removes the failed relay host, secret, lease, nonce, and scope from persisted trust state. The UI remains in the latest-QR recovery flow instead of repeatedly dialing the same unreachable route.
- Prevented local discovery from starting when a valid relay route is already present. This avoids Bonjour discovery clearing the structured relay failure error after a relay route fails.
- Marked the current restore attempt as consumed when failed relay material is stripped, so the trusted-runtime store emission does not immediately restart local discovery and erase the recovery state.
- Added focused ViewModel coverage for the relay-connect failure path, including persisted relay cleanup and no retry after the stale route is removed.
- Reinstalled and launched the updated Android debug APK on the physical `SM_S936N`.
- Ran the physical Android QR/deeplink relay smoke with saved-route reconnect. The smoke passed pairing, `runtime.health`, app relaunch, and a second `runtime.health` over the saved trusted relay route.
- Caveat: this includes focused no-device unit coverage plus physical install/launch and a USB-reversed development relay smoke. It proves the QR relay payload path and saved-route reconnect behavior in the development harness, but it does not prove optical camera QR scanning, production relay allocation, public internet reachability, or real different-network connectivity without a relay, tunnel, VPN, or future private overlay reachable from both devices.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustedRelayConnectionFailureClearsStoredRelayAndStopsAutoReconnect -Pkotlin.incremental=false`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.viewModelAutoReconnectsTrustedRelayOnInitAndRefreshesRuntimeState --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.relayReceiveAuthenticationFailureClearsStoredRelayAndStopsAutoReconnect --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.trustedRelayConnectionFailureClearsStoredRelayAndStopsAutoReconnect --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.viewModelShowsExpiredRemoteRouteWhenTrustedRelayLeaseExpiredOnInit --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.freshCompactRelayQrRefreshesExpiredTrustedRelayRouteAndReconnectsViaRelay -Pkotlin.incremental=false`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:installDebug -Pkotlin.incremental=false`
- `$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb shell am start -n com.localagentbridge.android/.MainActivity`

### 2026-06-27 macOS Relay Secret Store Boundary

- Continued no-device runtime trust-store hardening while the Android phone was disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Used a GPT-5.5 read-only audit subagent, which confirmed that macOS relay secrets and bootstrap allocation tokens were still persisted as raw `UserDefaults` values and recommended a Keychain-backed secret-store boundary with legacy migration.
- Added `CompanionRelaySecretStoring` and a default `KeychainCompanionRelaySecretStore` so macOS runtime relay secrets and bootstrap allocation tokens are stored behind deterministic secret refs instead of being written back into raw `UserDefaults` keys.
- Added legacy migration paths for existing `aetherlink.relay.secret` and `aetherlink.bootstrap_relay.allocation_token` values. Valid legacy values move into the secret store on first load, stale refs are removed when no secret can be resolved, and saved settings now remove the raw keys after storing refs.
- Updated focused macOS regressions so relay settings, bootstrap settings, restored QR leases, regenerated secrets, and expired-lease renewal assert secret-ref storage instead of raw defaults persistence.
- Strengthened copy hygiene and the no-device quality summary with `macOS relay secret store boundary` so future changes cannot silently return to raw macOS relay secret or bootstrap allocation-token persistence.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected during this pass. This is no-device source/SwiftPM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical Android install, optical QR scanning, physical Keychain behavior in a signed app bundle, live provider streaming/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-27 Android Relay Secret Store Boundary

- Continued no-device Android trust-store hardening while the Android phone was disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Added a `RelaySecretStore` boundary to Android pairing storage so the trusted-runtime DataStore keeps a deterministic `runtime_relay_secret_ref` handle instead of writing valid relay secrets directly into `runtime_relay_secret`.
- Added an Android Keystore-backed default `AndroidKeystoreRelaySecretStore` that encrypts relay secrets with AES-GCM and stores only encrypted blobs outside the main trusted-runtime DataStore.
- Added legacy migration so an existing valid `runtime_relay_secret` value is moved behind the secret-store handle on first read, then removed from DataStore. Expired, incomplete, or unresolved secret-ref relay routes are still purged.
- Added focused regressions for handle-backed persistence, legacy raw-secret migration, unresolved secret-ref cleanup, expired route cleanup, and `forgetRuntime()` secret removal.
- Strengthened copy hygiene and the no-device quality summary with `Android relay secret store boundary` so future changes cannot silently return to raw DataStore relay secret writes.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected during this pass. This is no-device source/JVM/SwiftPM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical Android install, optical QR scanning, Android Keystore behavior on physical hardware, physical haptics, live provider streaming/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verified after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew :core:pairing:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.core.pairing.PairingStoreTest -Pkotlin.incremental=false`

### 2026-06-27 Android Expired Relay Secret Store Cleanup

- Continued no-device Android connection hardening while the Android phone was disconnected. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Used a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer, which confirmed that valid relay secrets still need durable storage until a Keystore-backed secret abstraction exists, but expired relay secrets can be safely purged now.
- Updated `PairingStore` so trusted-runtime relay routes are only loaded and persisted when the lease is currently valid. Expired or incomplete relay routes are sanitized out of trusted state and their relay host/id/secret/expiry/nonce/scope keys are physically removed from DataStore.
- Replaced the old expired-route preservation test with regressions that prove expired relay material is dropped both when writing a trusted runtime and when reading a legacy stored route.
- Strengthened copy hygiene so future changes must keep `PairingStore` on valid-route-only persistence and must retain the expired/incomplete relay physical-cleanup regressions.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected during this pass. This is no-device source/JVM/SwiftPM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical Android install, optical QR scanning, physical haptics, live provider streaming/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-27 Android Composer QR Hint And Expired Relay Purge

- Continued no-device product-path hardening under the no-Spark constraint. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Used a GPT-5.5 read-only explorer, which identified that expired Android relay routes were reported in UI but stale relay host/secret material remained in durable trusted-runtime state.
- Updated Android chat composer readiness logic so trusted-runtime route-refresh states show a latest-QR hint instead of a generic connect-first hint. This keeps the disabled composer aligned with the QR-first recovery action when a trusted runtime has no usable route or a stale route.
- Added `chatInputHintRes(...)` for testable readiness-copy selection and localized the new latest-QR composer hint across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Updated expired relay handling so, when a saved relay lease is already expired, Android clears relay host/id/secret/lease/nonce/scope from UI state and persists the trusted runtime without stale relay material. A fresh QR can still replace the cleared route and reconnect through relay.
- Strengthened copy hygiene and the no-device quality summary with `Android composer latest QR readiness hint` and `Android expired relay route purge`.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected during this pass. This is no-device source/JVM/SwiftPM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical Android install, optical camera QR scanning, physical haptics, live provider streaming/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-27 Android Manual Diagnostic Host QR-First Guard

- Continued no-device product-path hardening under the no-Spark constraint. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used, and no subagent was needed for this narrow change.
- Tightened Android route recovery so a leftover manual diagnostic `runtimeHost` value no longer makes the normal Chat/Status route notice offer a generic connect action when the trusted runtime has no usable QR/relay/discovery route.
- Added `hasConnectableTrustedRuntimeRoute(...)` so route notices only offer `Connect` for a usable relay route, a trusted endpoint hint, or a non-manual route source such as QR/discovery. Manual diagnostic host values remain development tooling and do not override the QR-first recovery path.
- Added regressions proving a trusted runtime with only a manual diagnostic host still prompts `Scan latest QR`, while non-manual trusted route hints still offer connection.
- Strengthened copy hygiene and the no-device gate coverage summary with `Android manual diagnostic host QR-first guard`.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected during this pass. This is no-device source/JVM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical Android install, camera QR scanning, physical haptics, live provider streaming/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-27 Android OS App-Language Handoff

- Continued no-device Android language-state polish under the no-Spark constraint. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Used a GPT-5.5 read-only audit subagent to identify that Android exposed `localeConfig` metadata but did not hand OS per-app language selection into app state.
- Android now reads Android 13+ per-app language through `LocaleManager` on launch and reconciles it into `RuntimeClientViewModel` only while the user has not selected a language inside AetherLink.
- Persisted language data now records whether the active language came from the default, OS app-language handoff, or an in-app choice. Existing persisted non-English language values migrate as in-app choices so OS handoff does not overwrite old user preferences.
- Locale normalization now accepts region-qualified OS tags such as `ko-KR`, `ja-JP`, `fr-FR`, and `en-US` while keeping the supported five-language set.
- Strengthened Android string parity, copy hygiene, and the default no-device gate summary so `localeConfig` cannot remain without the actual OS app-language handoff and regression coverage.
- Caveat: the Android phone was disconnected during this pass. This is no-device source/JVM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical Android install, Android system Settings app-language UI behavior on a real device, camera QR scanning, live streamed chat/cancel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-27 Android Relay Auth Failure Auto-Retry Stop

- Continued no-device connection hardening while the physical Android phone is disconnected.
- Android now treats relay frame authentication failure as terminal stale-route evidence. When a trusted relay connection opens but the encrypted frame cannot be authenticated, the client clears the stored relay route material, keeps the trusted runtime identity, and stops automatic reconnect attempts until the user scans the latest QR with fresh route material.
- Added ViewModel coverage for both the retry predicate and the full auto-reconnect path: the relay receive failure removes `relayHost`/`relaySecret` from UI/store state and does not increase relay connection attempts after the retry delay.
- Strengthened copy/no-device guards so this remains part of the default no-device quality gate.
- Caveat: this is source/JVM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical QR scanning, real relay allocation behavior, physical device haptics, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-27 Route Refresh And Route Token Hardening

- Continued no-device security hardening for the paired route boundary. GPT-5.5 worker support was used for the macOS runtime slice and closed after completion. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Android pairing QR parsing now rejects whitespace inside identity-only `route_token` values before the token can be used for trusted runtime discovery or persisted route identity matching.
- macOS `route.refresh` now returns a fixed retryable `route_refresh_unavailable` error when route material refresh throws, instead of forwarding raw error text that could contain relay secrets, route tokens, provider URLs, or backend endpoint material.
- Added focused Android parser and Swift runtime-router tests for both cases, and strengthened copy hygiene so the Android route-token rejection remains guarded.
- Caveat: this is source/JVM/SwiftPM/script evidence only. It does not prove physical QR scanning, real relay allocation failures, production relay/signaling security, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

### 2026-06-27 Android Expired Remote Route QR Recovery

- Continued while the Android phone was disconnected, so this pass stayed no-device only.
- Used one GPT-5.5 read-only explorer and closed it after review. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Added focused Chat empty-state coverage for the stale different-network route path: when a trusted runtime has expired relay metadata and the client is in `remote_route_expired` with `route_diagnostic_remote_route_expired`, Chat shows `Scan latest QR`, explains that the user must scan the latest AetherLink Runtime QR with connection details, triggers the QR scan callback, and does not fall back to a generic reconnect.
- Added five-language Chat coverage for the same expired remote-route recovery path across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French, including the localized action label, detail text, state description, and haptic callback.
- Fixed a terminal `route.refresh` expiry state mismatch. When an authenticated runtime cannot refresh the relay route before lease expiry, Android now clears `isConnected`, `isConnecting`, `runtimeStatus`, and `activeRouteKind` before showing `remote_route_expired`, so the UI cannot simultaneously look connected and ask for the latest QR.
- Strengthened `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` so expired remote-route QR recovery, localization, and terminal route-refresh state cleanup remain part of the no-device gate and aggregate coverage summary.
- Caveat: this is source/Robolectric/script evidence only. The phone was disconnected, so it does not prove physical install, optical camera QR scanning, real-device haptics, live backend streaming, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verification after this change:

- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.ui.ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.chatScreenExpiredRemoteRouteShowsLatestQrRecoveryAction -Pkotlin.incremental=false`

The existing streaming cancel test still verifies that `Send message` is hidden, `Cancel generation` is clickable, the cancel callback fires, and destructive haptic feedback is dispatched.

- Used one GPT-5.5 read-only explorer and closed it after review. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. The explorer proposed system-locale first-run defaults, but that conflicts with the current product decision that the default language is English, so that proposal was not applied.
- Tightened the macOS Connection Recovery `Generate Latest QR` action. When saved connection details are not QR-ready, or the action is unavailable from the current view, the button now exposes a localized ready/unavailable value plus a concrete accessibility hint/help reason instead of only appearing disabled.
- Added focused macOS localization coverage for the Connection Recovery QR action value and ready/not-ready/unavailable hints across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Strengthened `script/check_macos_localization.py`, `script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, and `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` so this QR action accessibility reason stays named in the default no-device gate.
- This is source/XCTest/script evidence only. It does not prove a live VoiceOver session, physical QR scanning, physical Android reconnect, live streamed chat/cancel, physical haptic feel, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verification after this change:

- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `swift test --filter AetherLinkLocalizationTests/testConnectionRecoveryGenerateLatestQRActionAccessibilityUsesSelectedLanguage`

### 2026-06-26 macOS Connection Scope UI Polish

- Added a connection-scope status row to the macOS Advanced Connection Setup / Connection Recovery surface. The UI now distinguishes reachable connection details, connection details that need attention, local diagnostic loopback, local-network-only `.local` or private addresses, private-overlay addresses, automatic QR connection preparation, and missing route details.
- Kept the product boundary explicit in visible copy: ordinary private LAN addresses are shown as local-network-only unless the user explicitly marks them as a shared private overlay, while public/reachable relay-style hosts are shown as connection details that can be embedded in a QR for devices outside the local network.
- Added five-language localization coverage for the new status row in English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Added `AetherLinkLocalizationTests.testRemoteRouteScopeCopyDistinguishesAutomaticRemoteAndLocalRoutes` so the status copy stays clear for automatic route preparation, reachable relay-style details, private LAN addresses, and private-overlay addresses.
- The Android phone was disconnected during this pass, so this is no-device macOS UI/source evidence only. It does not prove arbitrary different-network reachability; that still requires QR material containing a relay/P2P/private-overlay route reachable by both devices.
- No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used in this pass.

### 2026-06-26 Physical Relay Route Refresh And Device Smoke

- Confirmed the latest physical-device condition is a true different-network condition for the route check: Android device `R3CXC0M76VM` reports active default `MOBILE[LTE]`, while the runtime host is on Wi-Fi address `192.168.0.102`.
- Fixed the development runtime relay renewal gap. `RuntimeDevServer` now wires `route.refresh` into `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter` and allocates fresh relay material through the configured bootstrap/development relay path.
- Kept route refresh replaceable and connection-safe by starting refreshed relay routes on a separate retained `RelayPeerClient`, so the runtime can send the `route.refresh` response over the current channel before clients move to the fresh route.
- Added Android terminal route-refresh handling for routes that cannot be retried before lease expiry. The client now surfaces `remote_route_expired` with `route_diagnostic_remote_route_expired` instead of silently holding a stale relay route.
- Added Android unit coverage for terminal route-refresh failures, alongside the existing retry-before-expiry and rejected-payload retry cases.
- Extended `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay` so the relay smoke now verifies `route.refresh`, validates the returned runtime identity, relay endpoint, relay lease, and nonce, then reconnects using the refreshed trusted route.
- Ran a live local relay reachability probe from the connected Android phone while it was on LTE: `script/android_relay_reachability_probe.sh --serial R3CXC0M76VM --host 192.168.0.102 --port 43171 --timeout 5 --json build/qa/aetherlink-different-network-relay-probe-20260626-lte-private-ip.json --include-network-summary`. Result: expected failure, `nc: Timeout`, host class `private_or_cgnat`.
- Interpretation: the remaining different-network failure is not QR parsing, app installation, model-provider access, or the runtime chat pipeline. It is route reachability. A QR that advertises a Mac private LAN address cannot work from LTE or another unrelated network. A production/public, VPN, tunnel, or private-overlay relay/P2P route that both devices can reach is still required for arbitrary different-network QR-only pairing.
- Ran the physical Android relay/deeplink smoke with USB only as automation support: `script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --relay --expect-reconnect --expect-chat-cancel`. Result: passed. It installed the current debug app on `R3CXC0M76VM`, injected the pairing URI, paired over development relay mode, reconnected after app relaunch using the saved trusted route, and observed `chat.send`, `chat.delta`, `chat.cancel`, and `chat.done` through the physical Android UI.
- Preserved latest device smoke artifacts in `build/qa`: `aetherlink-runtime-relay-chat-smoke-20260626-1653.log`, `aetherlink-relay-chat-smoke-20260626-1653.log`, `aetherlink-pairing-chat-smoke-20260626-1653.png`, and `aetherlink-chat-cancel-smoke-20260626-1653.png`.
- No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used. One GPT-5.5 read-only audit subagent identified the runtime `route.refresh` wiring gap and was closed after completion.

Verification after this change:

- `swift build --product RuntimeDevServer`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedRuntimeRetriesRouteRefreshErrorBeforeLeaseExpiry --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedRuntimeMarksRouteExpiredWhenRefreshErrorCannotRetryBeforeLeaseExpiry --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.authenticatedTrustedRuntimeRetriesRejectedRouteRefreshPayloadBeforeLeaseExpiry -Pkotlin.incremental=false`
- `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay`
- `script/android_relay_reachability_probe.sh --serial R3CXC0M76VM --host 192.168.0.102 --port 43171 --timeout 5 --json build/qa/aetherlink-different-network-relay-probe-20260626-lte-private-ip.json --include-network-summary`
- `script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh --relay --expect-reconnect --expect-chat-cancel`
- `./script/check_no_device_quality.sh`

### 2026-06-26 No-Device Attachment MIME And HWPML Ingestion Guard

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, rendered attachment picker behavior, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.
- Tightened Android attachment picker MIME coverage so runtime-supported document inputs stay explicit for HWPML, Hancom HWPML, HTML, RTF, XHTML, Markdown, reStructuredText, and AsciiDoc while image selection still appears only when the selected chat model advertises vision/image/multimodal capability.

- Added macOS `DocumentIngestion` support for HWPML XML documents and the Hancom HWPML MIME alias. HWPML now goes through the XML text extraction path and returns `application/x-hwpml` metadata.
- Strengthened `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` with an attachment-ingestion guard so the Android picker, Android fallback MIME mapping, macOS HWPML extractor path, and focused regression tests cannot silently drift.

### 2026-06-26 No-Device Android QR Pairing Hardening

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.
- Audited the Android QR pairing path with one GPT-5.5 read-only subagent. No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used, and the subagent was closed after completion.
- Tightened Android direct-route candidate generation so selected Bonjour routes and matching discovered routes cannot become direct TCP candidates when they point at local model-provider ports `11434` or `1234`. This preserves the product boundary that the client connects to AetherLink Runtime, not directly to Ollama or LM Studio.
- Tightened Android scanner raw-value acceptance so the camera flow only closes on QR values that the runtime pairing parser can actually accept under the same debug/product policy. Incomplete legacy `lab://pair` values are now ignored by the scanner instead of closing the scanner and surfacing a later invalid-pairing error.
- Narrowed the Android exported browsable deep-link manifest entry to `aetherlink://pair` and legacy `lab://pair` host matches, removing the broad scheme-only filter for unrelated custom-scheme actions. Superseded on 2026-06-29: exported Android OS pairing now exposes only `aetherlink://pair`; legacy `lab://pair` remains parser-only QR compatibility.
- Added Android no-device regression coverage that a relay QR remains persisted after an initial relay connection failure and that a recreated ViewModel restores that pending relay QR and sends the pairing request without using direct TCP.
- Strengthened `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` so Android pairing deep links must stay pair-host scoped, scanner acceptance must keep using the runtime pairing parser policy, and DirectTcp route candidates must keep blocking model-provider ports `11434` and `1234` across selected, discovered, and target endpoints.

### 2026-06-26 No-Device Android Runtime Cleanup And Provider Detail Redaction

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real device streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.
- Audited the Android trusted-runtime route-refresh cleanup path with one GPT-5.5 worker subagent, audited Android chat auto-scroll behavior with one GPT-5.5 read-only subagent, and audited the next no-device safety gap with one GPT-5.5 read-only subagent. No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used, and all subagents were closed after completion.
- Audited macOS Advanced Connection QR readiness copy with one GPT-5.5 read-only subagent. No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used, and the subagent was closed after completion.
- Fixed a stale route-refresh cleanup gap: `closeRuntimeConnection()` now clears the pending route-refresh request id as well as cancelling the lease/retry job.
- `forgetTrustedRuntime()` now closes the active runtime connection before removing trusted runtime state, so a forgotten runtime cannot keep an authenticated channel, scheduled route refresh, or pending refresh request alive.
- Added Android ViewModel regression coverage for manual disconnect and forget-trusted-runtime paths, proving delayed route-refresh retry jobs do not send another `route.refresh` after cleanup.
- The tests keep relay-route cleanup on the runtime-mediated path and fail if the route-refresh cleanup flow falls back to direct TCP.
- Extended Android connection cleanup so pending runtime-owned chat rename requests are also cleared on disconnect, matching existing cleanup for lifecycle, history, memory, suggestions, title, and route-refresh requests.
- Redacted Android provider status detail messages before storing them in UI state and again before rendering diagnostics. Safe product-level provider copy remains visible, but backend URLs, local model ports, and provider API paths are hidden from the client UI.
- Extended the Android provider diagnostic redaction guard to route/relay/pairing secrets. `route_token`, `routeToken`, `relay_secret`, `relaySecret`, `pairing_secret`, and compact `rt`/`rs` query material are now suppressed from runtime-visible error details, provider messages, and provider error-code display.
- Provider diagnostics now render only when at least one sanitized message or sanitized structured code remains, so a fully redacted provider status cannot open an empty diagnostics panel.
- `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` now requires Android source and tests to keep the endpoint and route-secret redaction guards in place.
- Added an Android interaction-feedback policy so ordinary taps, selection changes, and toggles use a lighter haptic feedback path while destructive and clipboard actions remain distinct.
- Replaced direct high-frequency `LongPress` feedback calls in the Android chat composer, navigation drawer, permanent navigation rail, model picker, search clear buttons, settings sections, pairing actions, route notices, memory controls, and QR scanner controls with the shared policy.
- Added unit coverage for the haptic policy and selection-change behavior, and confirmed no direct `LongPress` or `TextHandleMove` haptic calls remain in the main Android UI files outside the policy helper.
- Made Android route-safety notices actionable from the Status and Settings connection surfaces. Missing, expired, incomplete, or failed remote route states now resolve to a "scan latest QR" action, while a trusted runtime with a usable saved route resolves to a connect action.
- Route availability error notices now share the same action policy instead of always hard-wiring the route action to QR scanning. Remote route failures still prefer a fresh QR, preserving QR-first route refresh.
- Refined Android chat auto-scroll so incoming assistant-only appended messages no longer pull the user away from older messages. New local user-send bursts still scroll to the latest row, and streaming deltas continue to follow only when the list is already near the bottom.
- Refined macOS Advanced Connection QR readiness copy so a prepared-but-not-ready relay route now distinguishes stopped, connecting, reconnecting, failed, and ready states instead of showing one generic start/wait message.
- Added English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French localization for the new relay QR readiness states.

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Private Overlay QR Relay Scope

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real device streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.
- Added `relay_scope=private_overlay` to the QR route policy. Scope-less private/CGNAT/ULA relay literals are still rejected by the Android parser, but a QR that explicitly marks a user-controlled VPN, tunnel, or private-overlay route can now carry `10.x`, `100.64.x`, `172.16-31.x`, `192.168.x`, or ULA IPv6 relay addresses without being mistaken for stale same-network direct IP fallback.
- Updated Android trusted-runtime route persistence and route planning so private overlay relay routes survive restart and are attempted when their `relay_secret`, `relay_expires_at`, and `relay_nonce` lease material is still valid.
- Updated the macOS companion and RuntimeDevServer QR generation paths to support `relay_scope=private_overlay` for private/CGNAT/ULA relay literals. The macOS GUI now requires explicit private-overlay opt-in before it emits that scope. Loopback remains `relay_scope=usb_reverse` and debug-only; loopback, `.local`, unspecified, link-local, and multicast relay hosts are still blocked for normal remote QR.
- Updated the macOS Advanced Connection Setup flow so private relay hosts require a deliberate VPN/tunnel/private-overlay opt-in before they can be QR-ready. A QR is still generated only after a relay lease is prepared and the relay route is ready.
- Updated `script/verify_pairing_qr.swift` and `script/android_pairing_deeplink_smoke.sh` so private overlay QR routes can be verified with explicit scope/flags while default smoke paths still fail closed for accidental private fixed-IP routes.

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Development Relay Allocation Token

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real device streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.
- Hardened the development relay allocation path used by QR-only different-network testing. `AetherLinkRelay` can now require an allocation token before issuing route material, so a public/VPN/tunnel test relay is not an open route-material minting endpoint.
- Extended the relay line protocol from `AETHERLINK_RELAY allocate <route_token> [relay_secret]` to also accept `allocation_token=<token>` while preserving the old development format.
- Wired `AETHERLINK_BOOTSTRAP_RELAY_ALLOCATION_TOKEN` / `AETHERLINK_RELAY_ALLOCATION_TOKEN` through the runtime relay allocator and RuntimeDevServer bootstrap path.
- Updated `script/run_allocation_relay.sh`, `script/run_different_network_dev_runtime.sh`, and `script/no_adb_external_relay_pairing_smoke.sh` so relay startup, preflight, and QR emit-only smoke can use the allocation token consistently.
- Documented that the allocation token gates route-material issuance only; trusted-device pairing, runtime identity pinning, and challenge-response authentication still gate runtime commands.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter RelayAllocationTests`
- `swift test --filter LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testEnvironmentRemoteRelayRouteAllocatorPassesBootstrapAllocationToken`
- `swift build --target RuntimeDevServer`

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Runtime-Owned Chat Storage Boundary

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real device streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.
- Audited runtime-host chat persistence and mobile local history with one GPT-5.5 read-only subagent. No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used, and the audit was closed after completion.
- Confirmed AetherLink Runtime already records chat processing events to a runtime-host JSONL store: requests, assistant deltas, reasoning deltas, completion usage, cancellation, errors, generated titles, session summaries, and archive/restore/delete lifecycle events.
- Added runtime session summary processing metadata: `last_event`, `last_finish_reason`, and `last_error_code`. This lets clients display the latest runtime-side processing state without becoming the durable transcript authority.
- Updated the Android protocol model and protocol schema for the new optional runtime summary fields.
- Changed Android device storage behavior so runtime-owned chat sessions keep local metadata such as title, model, status, message count, and processing metadata, but runtime-owned message bodies are removed from the persisted SharedPreferences snapshot. Active in-memory UI state can still show the current stream, and transcripts should be rehydrated from AetherLink Runtime through `chat.messages.list`.
- Kept local-only drafts/notes unaffected by this redaction path.

Verified after this change:

- `swift test --filter 'LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testRuntimeChatStoreSessionSummaryExposesCancelledAndErrorProcessingState|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testRuntimeChatStoreListsSessionsAndMessages|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testRuntimeChatHistoryMessagesAreAuthenticatedAndReturnedFromStore'`
- `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.deviceStorageSnapshotDropsRuntimeOwnedMessageBodiesButKeepsLocalDrafts --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeSessionSummarySyncReplacesStaleRuntimeOwnedSessions -Pkotlin.incremental=false`
- `python3 script/check_protocol_schema.py`
- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Android QR Failure Classification

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real device streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Headless QR Pairing Verification

- The Android phone is currently disconnected, so physical-device install, camera-based QR scan, app-level pairing completion, haptic feel, real device streamed chat/cancel, and true external-network runtime connectivity were not validated in this pass.
- Confirmed the existing headless runtime smoke already exercises the QR pairing contract end to end: it starts the runtime, reads the emitted `aetherlink://pair` URI, parses the QR fields, sends `pairing.request`, authenticates a fresh connection with challenge-response, calls `runtime.health`, runs model/chat/cancel checks, and verifies trusted-route reconnect.
- Verified the same headless pairing/auth/runtime loop over the development relay route, proving the code path no longer depends on a fixed same-network IP for the scripted relay case.
- Verified the no-ADB QR artifact path can allocate a development relay route, emit the pairing URI, render a QR PNG, and decode it back successfully.
- Used one GPT-5.5 read-only audit subagent for no-device QR smoke review. No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used, and the audit was closed after completion.

Verified after this change:

- `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift`
- `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay`
- `script/no_adb_external_relay_pairing_smoke.sh --relay-host 127.0.0.1 --start-local-relay --emit-only`

Remaining before claiming user-visible QR pairing works on a phone:

- Reconnect the Android phone.
- Install the latest debug APK.
- Scan a fresh runtime QR with the device camera.
- Confirm the app sends `pairing.request`, receives `pairing.result`, persists the trusted runtime, reconnects without rescanning, and can call `runtime.health`.
- Repeat with a mutually reachable relay route from a different network, not a loopback/local diagnostic relay.

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Suggested Question Readability Cleanup

- The Android phone is intentionally disconnected for this pass, so no APK install, camera QR scan, optical QR validation, or physical-device suggested-question UI check was run.
- Used one GPT-5.5 read-only audit subagent for generated assistant UI and localization quality. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used, and the subagent was closed after completion.
- Hardened macOS runtime suggested-question parsing so strict JSON remains preferred, fenced JSON remains accepted, and numbered/bulleted fallback lists are accepted only when lines clearly start with a list prefix.
- Cleaned generated suggested-question strings on both macOS Runtime and Android cache paths by stripping leading list numbers, bullets, and wrapping quotes, then deduplicating case-insensitively before display/storage.
- Reworked Android suggested-question chips from a horizontally scrolling row into a wrapping `FlowRow`, with up to three visible lines per chip so Japanese, Chinese, Korean, French, and longer English suggestions are less likely to be truncated.

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Fenced JSON Generation Cleanup

- The Android phone is intentionally disconnected for this pass, so no APK install, camera QR scan, optical QR validation, or physical-device suggested-question/title UI check was run.
- Hardened macOS runtime parsing for generated chat titles and suggested next questions when local models wrap the required JSON in a fenced code block labelled `json`.
- Direct strict JSON remains the preferred path, fenced JSON is accepted as a compatibility cleanup, and malformed fenced title JSON now returns an empty title instead of saving the code fence as a visible chat title.
- Added runtime-router regression tests for fenced suggested questions, fenced chat titles, and malformed fenced title JSON.

### 2026-06-25 No-Device Relay QR Readiness And Runtime Log Polish

- Rechecked the Android chat reasoning surface while the phone is disconnected. Current Android code already separates runtime `reasoning`/`thinking` deltas and inline `<think>` blocks from final answer text, shows reasoning dimmed, limits the collapsed preview to about three lines, and allows expansion.
- Used one GPT-5.5 read-only audit subagent for this Android reasoning review. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used, and the subagent was closed after completion.
- Re-ran the authenticated mock relay smoke. Pairing, P-256 challenge-response authentication, `runtime.health`, `models.list`, `models.pull`, streaming `chat.send`, `chat.cancel`, and saved trusted relay reconnect all passed through the relay route.
- Re-ran the no-ADB external relay QR emit-only smoke. It started an allocation-required relay, generated the pairing URI, rendered the QR PNG, and verified QR PNG round-trip decode without using a physical device.
- Polished `RuntimeDevServer` startup logging so a relay-backed QR route no longer prints USB reverse or fixed `127.0.0.1` connection instructions as the normal path. Relay runs now tell the user to scan the emitted `aetherlink://pair` QR/URI and explicitly state that no USB port forwarding, fixed network address, or model-provider address is required for that route.
- Cleaned user-facing development script output in the USB install/smoke, runtime launcher, pairing deep-link smoke, no-ADB relay smoke, and allocation relay helper so USB loopback and local ports read as diagnostics instead of the product connection model.
- Reworded the real Ollama smoke skip message so it tells the user to start Ollama on the runtime host instead of exposing a direct provider loopback URL as the action.
- Updated the different-network development runtime helper so help/error text uses trusted-device and runtime-host wording instead of generic client or machine wording.
- Updated RuntimeDevServer development pairing info so newly emitted `AETHERLINK_DEV_PAIRING_INFO` uses canonical `runtime_device_id`, `runtime_name`, and `runtime_key_fingerprint` fields. Legacy `mac_*` QR aliases remain accepted by clients for compatibility, but the current dev output no longer emits them as the primary visible fields.
- Added `AETHERLINK_DEV_RUNTIME_DEVICE_ID` and `AETHERLINK_DEV_RUNTIME_FINGERPRINT` as runtime-first development identity environment names while keeping the older `AETHERLINK_DEV_MAC_*` names as compatibility fallbacks.
- Extended `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` to include `RuntimeDevServer` source logs and user-facing shell smoke/helper scripts, so QR-first, OS-neutral, no-direct-provider wording is guarded in the development runtime surface as well as the app UI/resources.
- Added `docs/qa-evidence.md` and ignored newly generated `artifacts/*.png` / `artifacts/*.xml` files by default. Existing historical captures remain in place, but generated artifacts must be refreshed and explicitly referenced before they are treated as current proof of UI, QR, or route behavior.
- The Android phone remains disconnected for this pass, so no physical-device install, camera scan, optical QR validation, or real different-network runtime validation was run.

### 2026-06-25 Android Localization Placeholder Guard

- Strengthened `script/check_android_string_parity.py` so it now checks duplicate Android string keys and format placeholder parity across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French resources.
- The new placeholder guard compares placeholder sets rather than raw order, so translated strings can reorder `%1$s` and `%2$d` while still failing if a placeholder is missing or has the wrong type.
- This makes future Android translation work safer for runtime-formatted copy such as provider status, model metadata, route notices, delete confirmations, and attachment metadata.

### 2026-06-25 Android ViewModel Compact Relay QR Pairing Guard

- Added a narrow internal dependency seam to `RuntimeClientViewModel` so QR pairing can be tested with fake transport, fake local stores, fake trusted-runtime store, fake discovery, and fake device identity without launching a real socket, Android Keystore, DataStore, or Bonjour discovery.
- Kept the public Android ViewModel constructor unchanged. Production still wires the real `RuntimeTransportClient`, `RuntimeRelayTcpClient`, `BonjourDiscovery`, `PairingStore`, `DeviceIdentityStore`, `RuntimeLocalStore`, and lifecycle callbacks through the default dependency factory.
- Extracted the QR parse and pending-pairing connection-plan steps used by `trustRuntimeFromPairingQr(...)`, then added a direct ViewModel unit test that calls `trustRuntimeFromPairingQr(...)` with a compact relay QR URI.
- The new test proves compact relay QR pairing uses the relay connector, does not attempt direct TCP, binds relay host/port/id/secret/nonce from the QR route, and sends a `pairing.request` envelope with the expected pairing nonce, code, and client identity.
- Tightened pending pairing route planning so route-bearing relay QR payloads do not start local discovery just because they have no direct endpoint hint. Identity-only QR payloads still start discovery and wait for a reachable route.
- Enabled Android unit-test default return values and added `kotlinx-coroutines-test` so ViewModel coroutine tests can run under JVM unit tests.
- Used one GPT-5.5 read-only subagent to audit the smallest safe ViewModel test seam. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used, and the subagent was closed after completion.
- The Android phone is disconnected for this pass, so no physical-device install, camera scan, optical QR validation, or real different-network pairing validation was run.

### 2026-06-25 Runtime Inline Reasoning Hardening

- Audited the reasoning/think pipeline with two GPT-5.5 read-only subagents: one for Android UI/state and one for macOS runtime/protocol behavior. No GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark subagent was used.
- Confirmed the existing protocol path already carries `reasoning_delta`/`thinking_delta`, Android renders a muted collapsed reasoning section, and Ollama/LM Studio adapters preserve common field-based reasoning outputs separately from answer text.
- Added runtime-side inline `<think>...</think>` and `<thinking>...</thinking>` splitting in `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter`, including chunk-boundary handling when the tag itself is split across streamed deltas. Inline think text is now stored and forwarded as `reasoning_delta`; visible answer text stays in `delta`.
- Applied the same split to runtime-generated chat titles and suggested next questions so hidden thinking does not pollute generated title/suggestion text.
- Tightened Android reasoning preview expansion so short line counts still become expandable when the text is long enough to wrap beyond the compact three-line preview, and added an explicit expand/collapse click label for accessibility.
- Polished Android reasoning action copy in English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French from developer-facing "reasoning" wording toward user-facing "thinking" wording. The UI still keeps reasoning text separate, dim, and collapsed by default.
- The Android phone is disconnected for this pass, so no physical-device install, camera scan, or on-device reasoning expansion validation was run.

### 2026-06-25 QR Remote Route Verifier Parity

- Audited the current different-network QR path with two GPT-5.5 read-only subagents. The current app architecture already fails closed for normal remote QR generation: macOS does not show a normal pairing QR unless a reachable, allocation-backed remote route is eligible and ready, and Android persists relay route material only after accepted pairing or route refresh.
- Confirmed the likely cause of off-network QR failure without a configured public/VPN/tunnel/future-overlay relay: identity-only QR cannot cross unrelated networks, local/private relay addresses are rejected, and complete QR relay route material must include `relay_host`, `relay_port`, `relay_id`, `relay_secret`, `relay_expires_at`, and `relay_nonce`.
- Tightened `script/verify_pairing_qr.swift` so QR image verification rejects private IPv4, carrier-grade NAT, link-local, multicast, and local/private IPv6 relay hosts. The verifier now matches the Android parser and macOS route-readiness policy more closely, so a QR that would fail on-device should not pass the pipeline just because the verifier only checked for loopback or `.local`.
- The Android phone is disconnected for this pass, so no physical-device install, camera scan, or on-device different-network validation was run.

Verified after this change:

- `script/render_pairing_qr.swift --input <public-relay-uri> --output <tmp.png>`
- `script/verify_pairing_qr.swift --image <tmp.png> --require-relay-route --forbid-direct-endpoint --expected-relay-host relay.example.test --expected-relay-port 443`
- `script/render_pairing_qr.swift --input <private-relay-uri> --output <tmp.png>`
- `script/verify_pairing_qr.swift --image <tmp.png> --require-relay-route --forbid-direct-endpoint` fails with exit code 2 and `invalidRelayHost`
- `git diff --check`
